posts.title: The Dragon King Ft Chemistry
posts.writeup: Recently I just started following Bay Area emcee L*Roneous on twitter after seeing he had a new album out (Notes Of A Righteous Outlaw). I originally came across his music in 2004 when he dropped his must have album, Purposely Powerful featuring some underground classics. Last night L*Roneous tweeted "i feel if you're really a hip hop head, you should be familiar w my name or my work." I definitely agreed with that statement. But then I thought that not everyone has the time to search out good music and know about rappers like L*Roneous. So today I would like to introduce you to a heavily slept on emcee. Peep some of my favorites from him below. You can grab his music from the above link and look for a review on his new album "Notes Of A Righteous Outlaw" soon.

posts.title: Impaled Nazarene
posts.writeup: On the other side of the fork in the road are rappers like Ill Bill and Vinnie Paz who hold absolutely nothing back when it comes to the content in their raps. It may be easy to pass these guys off as "crazy" conspiracy rappers but to those of you who have switched up your whole perspective of whats going on in this world, you will know damn well what they are talking about. I find their energy completely honest and inspiring. These guys aren't mad, they are just trying to tell us something and we should probably listen. Both rappers hold down their own respective solo and group careers but have now come together on a common theme of content that will slap you in the face with some reality. Take a listen to the song below, but be warned if you may not be ready yet. Grab this album from the link above.

posts.title: BTSU Ft Intuition
posts.writeup: Indie rapper Intuition is back with another round of his "I Ruined These Songs For You" series with part 2 that see's him sneak in some verses on tracks from Sublime, Phantogram, Erykah Badu, The Strokes, Frank Ocean + many more. Download this for FREE HERE.

posts.title: Ja
posts.writeup: Protoje is an heralded Jamaican reggae artist who has drawn comparisons to the Marley's with his brand of reggae. 7 Year Itch is his debut album that has been a 7 year process from its conception to the final product that was finally released back in January of this year. Below are standouts from 7 Year itch as well as the visuals for Rasta Love Ft Ky-Mani Marley. Purchase the album via itunes from the link above.

posts.title: Sleeping Children Are Still Flying
posts.writeup: Blue Sky Black Death are gearing up to release their newest album (NOIR) on April 26th. They released another teaser from NOIR yesterday and I must say it is pretty damn epic with its mulit-layered symphonic-electro masterpiece of a track. The track clocks in at 6 minutes but you may loose track of time when you let this one play out. After hearing this track I assume we will all be anticipating the release of NOIR. Pre-order a physical copy HERE.

posts.title: Verbal Intercourse Pt. 2
posts.writeup: Today Elzhi dropped the bonus cut off his upcoming mixtape, Elmatic, that will be dropping sometime this year. AML member and recent Red Bull Big Tune champ 14kt lends out his production on this one. On Verbal Intercourse Pt. 2, Elzhi speaks on his experiences from the shady side of the music industry. Elzhi is one of the best emcees in hip-hop right now and after hearing this track, we all will be anticipating the release of Elmatic.

posts.title: Favorite Town
posts.writeup: Usually I am very tentative to these type of compilations with 20 something tracks of posse cuts but when I came to "Favorite Town" I was pleasantly surprised. This is DJ Deadeye's first compilation and features a ton of emcees. You can purchase this from the link above.

posts.title: Cameras
posts.writeup: Riding a wave of hype for his debut album has been new artist Wiz Khalifa and his brand of hip-hop that appeals to a wide range of listeners. Rolling Papers contains a good chunk of music that will hit home with his listeners and will no doubt bring him much success. Rolling Papers will be released on March 29th. The link above will allow you to purchase this album on March 29th.

posts.title: Handshakes And Middle Fingers
posts.writeup: Classified has been holding down Canada as one of the hardest working independent artists for the last decade. Classified has continually grown with his music and has always kept a constant quality with his beats and lyrics throughout his discography. With his newest album that dropped today, we see another step taken on his destined path to becoming a national figure for Canadian hip-hop. Handshakes And Middle Fingers just added another 10+ years to his career because if he can keep putting out albums like this, he should probably never stop. Favorites from HS&MF include High Maintenance, Stay Cool, Maybe It’s Just Me, Young Soul, The Day Doesn’t Die, They Don’t Know, Step It Up, Desensitized and The Hangover. Listen to some of my favorites below then proceed to grab this from the itunes link above. For fans of SonReal, Ghettosocks, DL Incognito.

posts.title: All I Wanna Do
posts.writeup: This Seattle emcee is a prime example of a guy who has the timbre of what a rapper's voice should sound like and the content he speaks on is nothing to cringe at. His cadence is smooth and effortless. The production provided on the Timeless EP is provided by Seattle's Jester and easily matches the skill of Grynch. When its all said and done this EP provides replay value and will further add to a overflowing pool of talent in Seattle. Click HERE to grab this EP for free from DJbooth.net.

posts.title: Professional Dreamers
posts.writeup: Today (March 8th) marks the release date of Sweden's Looptroop Rockers fifth studio album, Professional Dreamers.  Not only is Professional Dreamers their best work to date, it is also their most complete yet.  There is no filler contained on this album, it is 13 tracks deep where no track can be dismissed as being below par.  The production handled by DJ Embee can easily put him on par with some hip-hop's best with his work on Professional Dreamers.  Professional Dreamers also sees longtime member Cosmic back in the fold.  Listening to this album is like eating a pack of gum, it will take forever to digest. So be prepared to NOT be disappointed and give yourself a good hour of time for this album.  Its definitely worth buying,  so I suggest you get your hands on this album ASAP.  Grab it from the link above.  Peep some of my favorite cuts below.

posts.title: Keep It Live
posts.writeup: L.A emcee, Sin dropped this album in 2005, blew my mind and then proceeded to fall off the face of the earth. Welcome To The Show was an underground classic that featured that great style of hip-hop I grew up with. Dope beats and dope rhymes was all you needed and this album was exactly based on this premise. Going over this album brings back many memories and still plays out as good music that stands the test of time. Purchase this album from CDbaby above.

posts.title: Fix You
posts.writeup: Being an avid follower of the SEA hip-hop scene for the past 5 years I have never taken my time to give a listen to these "townfolk" who just released their newest single, "Fix You". The Physics are Thig Natural, Just "Justo" D'Amato and Monk Wordsmith. Fix You is a very cool track that twists and turns with its bubbly synths, off beat drums and soulful vocals. The wonderful vocals of Kelsey Bulkin are also on this track. You will remember her from her recent collaboration with Sabzi (Made In Heights). Stream the track below. If you want to download it, hit up the bandcamp logo above to rightfully give The Physics your email address in exchange for the free download.

posts.title: Helena Beat
posts.writeup: Incredibly catchy, fun, careless and free flowing are the adjectives that sprout to the mind when describing this LA bands music. They released this EP on Jan 18th of this year but have been making their rounds through word of mouth and the Hype Machine via the blogs. Foster The People are mostly known for their most successful song, "Pumped Up Kicks". This EP is only 3 tracks and the other 2 we find on the EP are just as good. Being a one hit wonder is definitely not in the cards for this band. One of my other favs is "Houdini" that is lead by an infectiously fun lead synth on the chorus that will evoke dance moves out of more people than hipsters with hula hoops at festivals. The first track on the EP (Helena Beat) is on the same level as the other two. This EP is the definition of a tease and will have people definitely looking forward for more. Grab the EP from the link above.

posts.title: Derek From Northcliffe
posts.writeup: Canadian emcee, D-Sisive, has been through a lot over the past ten years of his life but still continues to pursue his passions. Over the past few years, the guy has been putting out a lot of material with most of it being for free (check out more of his music through the bandcamp link above). Jonestown 2 is also a free release. It features some pretty damn good tracks. You can check out some of my personal standouts below.

posts.title: Will DO
posts.writeup: The indie rock outfit TV On The Radio are gearing up to release their follow up to their very successful 2008 album, Dear Science, with the new album Nine Types Of Light. The album will be released on April 12th, so for now the guys have released the first single from the album with "Will Do". I have been holding on to this song for a few days because I was not sure If I liked it. But after getting other valued opinions its pretty obvious that its a great song. Peep it below.

posts.title: Macklemore & Ryan Lewis w/ Grieves + Budo @ FiveSixty [Feb 26/11]
posts.artist: Macklemore & Ryan Lewis w/ Grieves + Budo @ FiveSixty [Feb 26/11]

posts.title: Makeshift Kingdom
posts.writeup: Still plugging away making quality hip-hop is the very underrated hip-hop group from Vancouver: Sweatshop Union. Combining beyond average abilities in the form of the production and raps always elicits good music whenever Sweatshop release anything. The Bill Murray EP is "Hands down the BILLest" with more material for us hip-hop aficionados to sink our teeth into. The Bill Murray EP is out now, click the itunes link above to purchase.

posts.title: Lasers
posts.writeup: Lasers is an diverse album when you consider the different styles incorporated on the album.  While the music stays rooted in hip-hop you can find dance floor hits, truth anthems and synth heavy production that allows lupe to rip it.  Lasers shows a great balance between the raw/truth and the pop/commercial appeal that rounds it out on all fronts.  To think that Atlantic Records was jerking Lupe around with releasing this album!  There is a place for these labels and their purpose but its the deeply disconnected people that run them are at fault.  We are coming for your spot and Lasers will be in our earbuds while we march toward a change.  Preorder the album HERE and then proceed to check out some of my favorite cuts below.  They are stream only because I will get in trouble if I give em away for free.  Plus we should probably support Lupe and show Atlantic Records what's up! Lasers drops March 9th.

posts.title: Rockit Man Ft Silk E
posts.writeup: This March is proving to be a huge month for music as it will also hold the new Z & G album release date on March 22nd. Their new collaboration, Heroes In The Healing Of A Nation follows up the very successful Heroes In The City Of Dope from 2006. Amplive will of course handle the production and will be accompanied by the refreshingly good vocals of The Grouch and Zumbi. Rockit Man is our first taste of the album and wow is it a good track. Can you say repeat treatment? You can download this track for free from their site HERE. But first take a listen below!

posts.title: All Black Everything
posts.writeup: All Black Everything is the latest track to be released from Lupe's new album Lasers.  March 8th is juuust around the corner and that means an album that will be on steady repeat is almost at your ears.  All Black Everything is another no holds barred on the lyrical content.  Its pretty clear that Lupe is at his best when he speaks the truth on topics that inspire him.  Most of his lyrics will go over peoples heads, but for those of us who are attuned to this effed up state of the world, its a splash of cold water on the face. Pre-order HERE.  Stream the song below.

posts.title: Just For Show
posts.writeup: Atmosphere are like your older sibling, they have been there for the ups and downs and still continue to be there for you as the years go on. In 2011 we can all re-live our love for Slug and Ant as they gear up to drop their new full length album, The Family Sign on April 12th. Just For Show is the first official single from the new album. You can pre-order the album HERE . Stream only below.

posts.title: Luxury
posts.writeup: Easily one of my favorite Arrested Development songs that may have flew under a lot of people's radar back in 2004.  I remember all the girls taking a liking to this one.  Luxury is an upbeat catchy song that will be stuck in your head forever.  Reminiscing on this track takes me waaay back.  If you are familiar with this track, what does it remind you of?  This track reminds me driving down the highway in a 1987 Subaru GL (aka "The Boob") blasting this song on a stereo that was worth more than the car.  There is nothing like the memories attached to music and this one brings back plenty!  If you have never heard this track then definitely take a listen below.  If you would like to purchase this from the album, go to the itunes link above.

posts.title: City Woman
posts.writeup: Kyprios of the Sweatshop Union has just released a new song that takes on the classic from The Stampeders "Sweet City Woman".  You may not remember it right away but when you hear his version below you will definitely remember it.  There is a cool story behind the song in how he was finally able to legally do a cover of the song.  If you are curious check out the story behind the track go HERE.  The track is stream only so if you want to buy it for a buck, hit up the itunes link above.

posts.title: I Follow Rivers
posts.writeup: Swedish pop singer Lykke Li has always been a well known artist in most circles, but if you're like me you only know her from her feature on the N.A.S.A - Gifted song from 2009....  Yes I know that is pretty sad if you have always been a fan of hers.  At least I am on board now after a few listens with her latest, Wounded Rhymes.  Check for it on March 1st on itunes.  Purchase her first album Youth Novel's from the link above.  And of course take a listen to the songs below.

posts.title: Baby Roulette Ft Train
posts.writeup: Chiddy and Xaphoon have released another new track that will not be on their new album, The Swelly Life due this year.  Baby Roulette is a clever title for a track about safe sex that embodies everything we like about Chiddy Bang.  Swelly Life should be an album to definitely look forward to in 2011.  For now grab yourself this track below.

posts.title: Stars Shine Brightest (In The Darkest Of Night)
posts.writeup: One of the best hip-hop groups will be blessing our ears on March 22nd with their new album, Oneirology (Oneirology is the study of dreams). If you are familiar with the type of music these guys make then an album title like that can only spark the imagination of what we are going to be in for. Kno will of course be handling all of the production with Natty and Deacon heading up the vocals. Stars Shine Brightest (In The Darkest Of Night) is the first single from the album that you can purchase from the link above at your desired price.

posts.title: Stay With Me
posts.writeup: Tightest Whitest is easily the most questionable rapper name you could ever call yourself. Finding a decent quality picture of his 2003 album, Reverse Discrimination is impossible so we are stuck with a better representation of what visual to accompany with this song. lol!!. This track was from 2003 and was a hidden gem back then. Today its kind of funny to look back on but still remains a good song regardless of all things on the surface. And if you are this artist, please don't get mad at me. And if you think this song is terrible, well I don't blame you but I have to expose my past loves regardless of how they sound today.

posts.title: Pack It Up
posts.writeup: Grieves & Budo also released a free single and video in light of Valentines day yesterday. It's a song that won't be on their Rhymesayers debut but will definitely find some replay value for sure! Check the visuals below. Click the itunes link above to check out more music from Grieves and Budo.

posts.title: For The Love ft. Dice
posts.writeup: Sol released this track yesterday in light of valentines day so I am of course a day late with this one. Keep on the lookout for Sol's full length album this year. For now chill out on this track and listen to Sol spit some truth. "....and pray to god for love, cause this shit out here ain't shit but sex and empty hugs" < #cosign.

posts.title: Future Shock
posts.writeup: Sims is a demanding talent on the mic and after one listen through Bad Time Zoo you're going to want to have it on repeat for a few hours.  Bad Time Zoo is easily Sims best work to date with his intricate, fast paced, well portrayed rapping over mind blowing production from Lazerbeak.  The music on Bad Time Zoo will simply blow you away.  I am just calling it like it is and not over exaggerating.  Do yourself a favor and buy this album when it comes out.  Then you can proceed to agree with me.   You can pre-order the album HERE or hit up itunes on Feb 15th for a digital copy.

posts.title: Too Long (feat. Black Thought)
posts.writeup: Saigon saw the rise of his career in the mid 2000's with anticipation of his debut album and his appearances on Entourage.  He was subjected to the politics of the record label he was with and the album was shelved.  That was until Just Blaze's Fort Knocks label decided to finally release it on Feb 15th, 2011.  Chances are people have forgot about Saigon and his unfortunate luck with getting this album out.  But all that will fade to dust when people hear Too Much featuring the legendary Black Thought.  Too Much serves as the hidden bonus track on the album but easily surpasses the rest of the cuts on the album for the best track on The Greatest Story Never Told.  The rest of the album is heavily laced with hard hitting beats and grimey raps from Saigon.  I have personally grown tired of hip-hop like this but that does not mean there won't be a lot of you that will definitely dig this album.  Click the link above to grab the first single of this album and when Feb 15th comes you will be able to purchase the album through the above link..

posts.title: Black Bug
posts.artist: Big Boi Vs. The Black Keys
posts.writeup: I know I am hella late on this one but man the mashups provided by Wick-It The Instigator are some of the best mashups I have ever heard.  The combination of Big Boi and The Black Keys works so well that you forget about the originals.  This free mashup mixtape is a must have, you can download it for free here.

posts.title: That Aint Classy
posts.writeup: Handshakes and Middle Fingers.  That Ain't Classy is the first single from the album and like all good lead singles this one opens the door of anticipation once again to one of Canada's best exports.  Judging from the production on That Ain't Classy, Class has continued to build on his craft and still keeps it moving and progressive.  March is shaping up to be a big month in music!  Purchase the single from the link above.  Support!

posts.title: Professional Dreamers (Single)
posts.writeup: Easily one of the best hip-hop acts out of Europe is the Looptroop Rockers from Sweden.  One of my favorite things about these guys is the content and attitude they portray in their music.  Its genuine and admirable.  They are artists you can look up to.  In 2011 Looptroop will be releasing a brand new album in March.  Professional Dreamers is the first single and it is pretty much self-explanatory.  That lead synth is hella filthy!.  Purchase the single above and peep the video below.

posts.title: Young Girl
posts.writeup: This acoustic rendition of Charmaine was my first introduction to UK artist, Plan B and his contrasting style of thick English accent rapping with his amazing singing voice.  Nowadays you will find Plan B singing more than rapping but it was tracks like this from his early days that made you realize the raw talent and potential.  Plan B has enjoyed his most success to date through his latest album The Defamation Of Strickland Banks with charting singles and multiple awards.  I definitely reccomend you also check out his earlier releases with his 2006 debut album Who Needs Actions When You Got Words and his mixtape from 2007, Paint It Blacker.  Hit up the itunes link above to grab his music off of itunes.

posts.title: Let Them (JJ Remix)
posts.writeup: Toronto rapper iSH is posturing up to make a big push in 2011 with a string of singles and new projects to makes us bloggers inform you of his music.  We are mostly anticipating iSH's new remix project: Why Are We Whispering? with producer Don Diablo.  A few weeks ago they released their first remix and it has been been on repeat since.  I like it so much that it will be added to the playlist and the next HD show!  Yesterday iSH released this new remix of a remix that he goes in over the Swedish group JJ that originally remixed an Akon song.  Peep the track below.

posts.title: All This Time
posts.writeup: Who else but the guys from Iration could ease you into your seat and take you away from this miserable winter to the sunny beaches of Hawaii.  The Fresh Grounds EP is their first release after their infectious album Time Bomb that was a top 10 album on Hillydilly.com for 2010.  Their sound has taken off since the Sample This EP and still continues the same winning formula for another EP full of feel good reggae music.  Iration are self proclaimed "nice guys" but trust me these dudes ain't finishing last in anything.  Grab this EP from the link above and stream my favorite songs from the EP below.

posts.title: The W's - 2011 (Free)
posts.writeup: Shwayze and Cisco Adler are fresh out of the studio with a new free project for us full of their California influenced music. The formula is always simple with these dudes: California+ Girls + Sunshine = Shwayze. Download this for free via the bandcamp page HERE. For fans of Chiddy Bang, The Five One, Close Enough.

posts.title: Words I Never Said
posts.writeup: The lyrics Lupe spits on Words I Never Said is nothing but the truth.  The song reflects many of the happenings in the world right now and the inner conflicts we all face when talking about such things with our peers.  Lupe is an artist with a ton of influence and to have someone like him putting so much truth into his lyrics it really is a step forward for the awareness of everyone.  Make sure you listen to the last verse very carefully.  It is very moving stuff.  Combine that with an intense beat and you got yourself a very empowering song.  After a listen to this song you can see why Atlantic was holding back on releasing Lasers.  Lasers drops March 8th!

posts.title: Just Got Started ft. Pez
posts.writeup: 360 is gearing up to release his highly anticipated debut album 'Falling And Flying' this March.  Just Got Started is the first single from the album and has been featured on triplej.  360 is on my list of who to watch in 2011 and with only a month away from the release of his debut I am pretty excited to finally hear it.  Peep the video below!

posts.title: Eyes On Fire
posts.writeup: The Danish group Blue Foundation are best exemplified by the track posted below, Eyes On Fire, through its melancholic indie-trip-hop style lead by beautiful vocals and slow building instrumentation.  Life Of A Ghost is their third album that can be purchased through the above itunes link.

posts.title: End Credits Ft Plan B
posts.writeup: The UK dubstep duo Chase And Status are gearing up to release their sophmore album on Jan 31st that features guest shots from Plan B, Maverick Sabre, Cee-Lo Green, plus many more.  Over here in North America they are not as well known as the UK but thankfully we have the internet and there are no country lines on here.  Click the itunes link above to preorder.

posts.title: Robots Cant Drink
posts.writeup: After a solid year of dropping his Freestyle Fridays, E-Dubble has come full circle in finishing it off with the very last one today.  There is plans of a mixtape on Feb 4th so don't worry about your dose of new music from E-dubble because there is more on the way.  Go ahead and check out FF #53 below.  For fans of Atmosphere, Grieves, Macklemore.var hcode="cx14vk5qmmgj";var htitle="e-dubble - Robot's Can't Drink (Freestyle Friday _53).mp3";var hwidth="410";var zombie="raven";E-Dubble - Robot's Can't Drink  Download!

posts.title: The Last No Needz LP....I Think
posts.writeup: When talent is evident and you know that not enough people know about No Needz, you get a bit more motivated to express the music.  No Needz has always been pushing out good music with his solo albums and his work with Close Enough.  The album title is not the direction he should be thinking but rather the opposite because his skills are impressive and enjoyable.   He is one of those rappers that you fantasize about linking up with some of your favorite producers.  If I was NoNeedz and had money to blow I would have M-Phazes, Sabzi, Khrysis, Kno, Eligh, & Countbounce all on one album.  If I were to win the lottery tomorrow making this happen would be on my list of things to do along with creating a festival.  Not to be overshadowed or disrespected  is the in house production on The Last No Needz  from Tony D.  Brand New Light and Hustle Minded are some pretty serious beats on any level of comparison.  Brand New Light also features cuts from DJ Artistry.  Remember when someone said "Don't judge a book by its cover"? Yea? That applies here.  You can get this on CDbaby right now by clicking the link above.  This album will be on itunes soon.

posts.title: Hello (Why Are We Whispering Remix)
posts.writeup: Why are we whispering is a side project with DJ/Producer Don Diablo and rapper Ish.  This remix of Hello from Martin Solveig and Dragonette is a first taste and I must say that I am very impressed.  It's hella catchy and I can definitely see people loving this track.  It has party jam written all over it.  Take and listen below and then proceed to listen to it again.  Enjoy!

posts.title: So Damn High
posts.writeup: Seattle keeps pumping out the talent lately as another new emerging artist from Seattle is compounding the reputation for Northwest Hip-Hop.  He goes by the name of Sol, he is young and definitely has what it takes to make a career out of rapping.  Sol's main inspiration in his writing is derived from his experiences with the ladies and his relationship with mary jane.   This 21yr old has a ton of potential so if you're like me you will be keeping an eye out on this dude.  Dear Friends Vol 1 & 2 are both available for free download HERE.  Make sure you take the time to download them so you don't sleep on him like I did.  n>

posts.title: That Good ft. Sapient
posts.writeup: Onry Ozzborn was in the ears of a lot of people in 2010 with his Dark Time Suninshine project with producer extraordinaire Zavela.  Having a definitive style like Onry Ozzborn's creates music that holds itself above your average expectations.  Hold On For Dear Life is a perfect example, through its melodic continuity in the raps and the production.  The production is mostly handled by his in house Old Dominion crew (Sleep & Taco Neck) and features 2 incredible beats from Sapient on "That Good" and Zavela's "I Give Up".  Hold On For Dear Life is out now and can be purchased digitally (above) or physically HERE.

posts.title: Trust Me
posts.writeup: The Paperboys were an instrumental part of my life musically back when I was young in my mp3 digging days. Their music instantly inspired anyone who came across it and prompted you to tell everyone you knew about their music. The added coolness was the fact that these guys were from Norway and chances were that nobody over here was ever going to hear their music. So I will always attest the Paperboys as being apart of the foundation for my motivation to start this blog and begin a life long goal to spread this music to people. As a relative side note to this story is my personal story of back in 2006 when I was backpacking Europe. I flew over to Norway just to see them preform in Oslo. I approached them that night and told em I was Canadian and that I was a huge fan. Needless to say they lost it, we ended up partying that night backstage and eventually passed out on Vinnie's couch. That night set the stage for me to take this love for music and make a real difference. The Paperboys are Vinnie (emcee) and DJ Pope (production/cuts). They have released 4 full length albums: No Cure For Life (2002), The Great Escape (2003), When Worlds Collide (2005) and The Oslo Agreement (2009). Below are some of my favorites from the Paperboys in hopes that you can become a fan as well. These guys do not really care for the internet so their net presence has always been very low. But regardless make sure you are aware of these guys and their music.

posts.title: So Low
posts.writeup: Talib is known for being a very talented emcee so needless to say whenever the guy drops a new album, hip-hop heads everywhere make a point to listen and support.   Gutter Rainbows is his newest solo endeavour since 2007's Eardrum. This album is also his first away from Warner Bros and his first on his new imprint Talibra   The production on Gutter Rainbows comes assisted by M-Phazes, Khrysis, S1 and Ski Beatz.  With an emcee like Talib it is never a question of the lyrical ability that he brings consistently.  Its the production that will be a determining factor in giving the material from this album replay value.  With a producer list like that you can't go wrong.  The group of producers stated above are all a good fit with each other and they do give Talib a level playing field between the beats and rhymes.  Gutter Rainbows is a impressive album from Mr. Kweli and should stand the test time by sticking around on your playlist.  Favorites include So Low, Cold Rain, Palookas, Mr. International, Tater Tot and How You Love Me.  Gutter Rainbows is a digital only release and will be available on Jan 25th.  Click the itunes link above to pre-order it.

posts.title: Finally Begin
posts.writeup: The Long Beach. CA based band Cold War Kids are releasing their third full length album on January 25th.  This is my first taste of hearing the Cold War Kids and it has prompted me to pass it on to you guys.  Their music definitely deserves a listen. Check out 2 of my favorite tracks from the album below.  If you like what you hear and want to hear more click the itunes link above to pre-order this album.  Mine Is Yours will be released on Jan 25th.

posts.title: Wings
posts.writeup: Mack & Ryan Lewis have remarkably sold out 3 shows in Seattle all within 48 hours of releasing tickets. This all occurred within 2 weeks! The venue is the Showbox Theatre in downtown Seattle that has a capacity of 1200 persons!! Not only is this great news for Macklemore & Ryan Lewis but it shows the force behind good music and good people who support it. We the people have the power and who better to represent us musically than Mack & Ryan Lewis. That's some pump your fist in the air kind of shit right there! In light of this amazing achievement they have decided to release the first leak from their yet to be titled full length album that will be dropping this year. "Wings" is a song about Macklemore's youthful affection for Jordans and the misguided influences we receive. Mack shares another story that we all can relate to. Self-awareness makes for great song writing and "Wings" embodies another quality song that will deserve your attention. Production credits are as follows: Ryan Lewis (producer), Andrew Joslyn (Violin), Danah Olivetree (Cello), Noah Goldberg (Keys), Owuor Arugna (Trumpet), Zach Fleury (Bass), Hollis Wear (wrote the chorus), Ray Dalton & Camila Recchi (BG Vocals), and finally with Denny Middle School with the childrens choir.

posts.title: Payback
posts.writeup: It has been a pretty long hiatus for Collie Buddz since he dropped his self titled debut album in 2007.  Collie Buddz tapped himself a solid fanbase for the material he produced and has had fans wondering when he would follow up with some new material ever since.  4 years later we get his new EP with 7 new tracks.  Seven different producers provide the beats on this EP that each have different styles to give this EP a good dynamic.  Not every producer brought the riddims/dancehall vibe with their production either.  The Playback EP also sees Collie Buddz over synth laden hip-hop beats and string/keys driven production complimented by some DJ cuts.  Playback is available for FREE download, go to his site and give em your email to get yourself a copy!

posts.title: Champions Freestyle
posts.writeup: This "freestyle" was originally from Mr. SOS' 2004 mixtape, Mr. SOS For President.  It was always confusing to me because Mr. SOS never raps on this track but I am assuming he was the one responsible for flipping the sample?  Regardless this track was a slap in the face back in '04 that introduced everyone to Tonedeff and his incredible skill.  If you would like to download the mixtape, hit up the itunes link above.

posts.title: Dreams Up
posts.writeup: Everybody's favorite new emcee, Hoodie Allen, is quick to follow up on the hype of the very dope Pep Rally mixtape with the new single Dreams Up. Hoodie will be releasing a new mixtape titled Leap Year very soon and Dreams Up is our first taste. RJF is responsible for the production while sampling Oh Land's "White Nights". Stay tuned for the release date of Leap Year by joining Hoodie's FB page HERE. For now go ahead and take a listen below and download this for FREE too!

posts.title: All Die Young
posts.writeup: The expression that age is just a number applies to music as well because these young-ins make music that defies any kind of age oriented box we can put them in. From listening to Dye It Blonde you would never would of guessed that these guys are fresh out of high school. Not that age has anything to do with the music I just thought it is kinda cool. Smith Westerns music is reminiscent of a throwback 70's psychedelic rock style. They bury their vocals in reverb and have absolutely epic guitar riffs. The song structures stay progressive and end up sounding like playful jams with no regard for any formidable structure. Dye It Blonde is my first taste of Smith Westerns and has got my attention. Make sure you give Dye It Blonde a chance, its a very enjoyable album. Dye It Blonde is out now and can be purchased on itunes, click the link above.

posts.title: Sorry For The Delay
posts.writeup: 3400ml make music that is dispositioned from what you expect.  These South Africans disregard any direct influence from other bands as well as a refraining from one typical style.  When you hear music that catches you off guard and changes throughout the course of the album you begin to appreciate the music on a deeper level.  It sticks with you and prompts you to give the album more spins and attention.  340ml represent Johannesburg via Mozambique and hold it down with music that deserves attention worldwide.  You can buy this album HERE.  Listen to some of the best tracks off the album below.

posts.title: Opium Dream
posts.writeup: Sweeping through the cosmos back in 1999 was this slept on album from vocalist Ann Colville and beatmaker Dub-L. One Hundred also features cuts from DJ JS-1 and vocal features from a young Aesop Rock. Take a listen to some of the highlights from this album below. Purchase this album HERE.

posts.title: Live Forever
posts.writeup: Shad has linked up with Alexisonfire vocalist Dallas to drop this 2 song EP that features one new song and one remix.  Live Forever is an instant standout with Shad's portruding vocals, nice production and soothing vocals from Dallas.  Dallas is mainly known for being apart of Alexisonfire but now has found time to focus on his solo side project, City And Color.  His solo project is a complete 180 from his work with Alexisonfire by turning to a folk/indie influence.  If you are not famliar with City And Color (like I am) this track below will give you a taste.  The second song on this EP is a remix of Listen that also features vocals from Dallas.  Listen is taken from Shad's 2010 breakthrough album T.S.O.L.  Click the link above to purchase this on itunes.

posts.title: The Touch Ft Ill Poetix
posts.writeup: Edinburgh is a beautiful city full of history, character and charm that can only help inspire the people who live in that city.  SciFi Stu is one of those inspired residents and the first hip-hop producer I have ever heard from Scotland.  From The Heaviest Heart To The Coldest Soul is his debut album that sees him handling all the production with vocal assistance from Ill Poetix, Moka Only, L.I.F.E Long, Count Bass D, and many more.  Click the link above to purchase this and listen to the best track off the album below.

posts.title: Random Images
posts.writeup: Greyspace are a 4 piece rock/alternative hip-hop band from Stockon, CA.  Random Images is their debut album that is worth your time.  Rosa Parkin was my favorite cut from the album, so go ahead and check it below.  Check these guys out on FB here and click the itunes link above to purchase this album...

posts.title: Lucha Contra de la Injusticia!
posts.writeup: Brighton, UK producer Black Grass has a knack for being extremely creative with his production by keeping the instrumentation fresh and interesting.  Melodies and chord progressions frequently change as well.  Boring or slow will never cross your mind as this almost 7 minute song below takes you for a journey.  The reggae influenced Oh Jah was the lead single on this single EP but it was Lucha Contra De La Injuticia that stole the show.  Click the itunes link above to purchase the EP.

posts.title: Realize
posts.writeup: Yes I just used a picture of Jebidiah Springfield and yes Jebidiah The Universal MC is a real stage name.  In 2003 Jebidiah The Universal MC dropped his debut EP titled, The Sound Effects.  After the release of this EP Jebidiah basically fell off the map, who knows who this guy is or what he is up to these days.  He has no website or myspace and any info on him is very scarce. If anyone knows him or his where abouts def let him know that his music is still alive!  Realize was from this EP and will always be considered a random classic from 2003.  You can get this EP digitally HERE.

posts.title: Second Chance
posts.writeup: Peter Bjorn and John have always been a creative force with their music through making infectiously catchy songs that is almost impossible not to like. For example their most well known song, 'Amsterdam' from their third album from 2006, Writers Block. You can't tell me you don't like that song. And ok yea its been over played by that dude with the same ipod playlist for the last 5 years. But regardless, we all remember our first time hearing Amsterdam. In 2009 PB&J released Living Thing and now are back with a new album. Gimme Some unfortunately did not stick on me through a first listen, although it did not go with out its gems. To me Second Chance, Eyes, May Seem Macabre and Cool Off were musically bigger stand outs than the rest. Usually if an album has 4 songs that you leave on your playlist it is most definitely worth checking out. You can do so on March 29th.

posts.title: Safe & Sound (Zion I Remix)
posts.writeup: Amp Live and Zumbi have remixed one of Rebelution's best tracks from their 2007 debut and injected it with a their style of progressive hip-hop.  The remix features Zumbi dropping a great verse with Amplive handling the synth duties with a thick dub-step style bass line as well as his usual bag of tricks.  On January 11th Rebelution will be releasing this Remix EP that features remixes from Zion I, Yeti Beats & more.  Hit Rebelution up HERE via their website for the link to buy this when it drops.

posts.title: Joy & Pain 2
posts.writeup: Sick of that jazzy hip-hop yet? no? Well me either because if the music is good why not listen to it right?  Your usual suspect, Japan is responsible for this compilation that features cameos from an international cast of emcees and producers from Moka Only, Fat Man, SoulChef, SmooVth, Shorty Raw, DJ Ezasscul, Ghostra Nostra + more.  Besides Moka and SoulChef I have not heard of anyone on this either but as the saying goes "if you don't know now you know ____!"  Purchase this album HERE.

posts.title: Drift Away
posts.writeup: Canadian producer Incise makes very good beats and has been making em for awhile now.  The guy works with talented emcees from all over the world and there is a reason why people want to spit over his beats.  His production sparks the soul and almost makes me want to write some raps to em.  Okay maybe not but you get the picture.  Familiar Voices is being released as a free download that features instrumentals from Incise's 2 albums (Inside Story & Daily Methods).  If I was an emcee I would be hitting him up for a few instrumentals FOR SURE. So maybe you should do that, or just be a listener....   Hit up the links above to see what was said about his albums and click the itunes link above to purchase them.  Click HERE to download this!

posts.title: Global War
posts.writeup: Reggae veteran Alborosie released this album in 2010. I saw it on some reggae blog's top 10 lists and figure I better give it a listen. Global War was a song that stuck on the playlist after a listen through so I figure I'd pass it on to you guys. It has that classic reggae sound with a cool bubbling popping sound throughout the track. The song is titled Global War that is sang by a Rastafarian so you can imagine what the content is going to be about. Alborosie is originally from Sicily, Italy but now resides in Kingston, Jamaica. You can grab his album via itunes from the link above.

posts.title: The End
posts.writeup: If there was ever a hip-hop themed western movie this track would be the perfect theme music.  Its dark, dusty, grimey, rusty and downright filthy.  The End was my first introduction to the American hip-hop group Maspyke: consisting of DJ Roddy Rod and emcees Tableek & Hanif Jamiyl.  The End was a special track because it was never featured on an album but rather just on a promo cd titled Masterworks and Bukarance Presents.  Maspyke are still making music these days but will always be remembered for this underground classic track.  Do yourself a favor and get acquainted!  You can download their latest album from 2005, Static, from the itunes link above.

posts.title: Marijuana Dreams
posts.writeup: There is always good music floating around in this wonderful world of ours that still has not been heard enough.  Take Dubblestandart for example, they are a dub band from Vienna, Austria who have released 11 studio albums.  Marijuana Dreams was my first introduction to their work and its one of those eye openers that prompts the thought: "who the hell are these guys and why do I not know about them?" 11 albums is a lot to go through but I will tell you that they regularly work with Lee "Scratch" Perry who rarely does any recording these days and has lent out his vocals to their albums (as well as being their backing band at live shows) on a few occasions.  If your a legend and you rarely record, its obvious that Dubblestandart's music inspires Lee enough to record.  Below is my favorite track from Marijuana Dreams.

posts.title: Skylark Interabang!
posts.writeup: Sabzi of Blue Scholars and his friend female vocalist, Kelsey Bulkin recorded this EP last week and are now giving it away for free via bandcamp. Click HERE to go to the bandcamp page to grab this. Check my favorite cuts from the album below and then proceed to download the EP.

posts.title: Boxes
posts.writeup: One of our beloved readers hit me up via email in regards to The Steelwells.  I must say kudos to you Talitha because not only do I like these guys but many others that come to this site will as well.  I would say their sound is good for a chill day with coffee in hand and thoughts on low.  You can grab this EP HERE.  If you would like to hear and see more check out their website HERE and become a fan on FB.

posts.title: Cold Stars
posts.writeup: This week e-dubble has reworked Sufjan Stevens' "Star Of Wonder".  Once again its another track that will deserve to be on your playlist.  Stay up on e-dubble's freestyle fridays by becoming a fan on FB HERE or check out his website HERE.

posts.title: Everything Changes
posts.writeup: SOJA have been preforming this song at their live shows for some time now but have finally released a studio version of the song.  The single is available right now on itunes as a deluxe single EP featuring two remixes.  Take a listen to the original below and the best remix of the two with added drums, strings and keys.  SOJA remind us that they are still here and a force to be reckoned with when it comes to this art we call music.  Get the Everything Changes deluxe single from itunes HERE. Sorry stream only below, its only 2 bucks, so go buy it!!

posts.title: The Cool And The Deadly
posts.writeup: Mike Pinto has just released a new single that is available right now on itunes.  It has been a while since we have heard anything from Mike Pinto and with this new track it will remind us all that this dude reps the reggae.rock with the best of em.  The whistling is catchy as f*ck.

posts.title: Ashes For Your Urn
posts.writeup: Musical styles become a product of their environment: they reflect the landscape, weather, language and culture in the music.   Hawaii is one of those places that has a knack for creating music that tunes into the island lifestyle of beaches, surf and sun.  In turn Hawaii has created a group like Inna Vision that portray everything about the Hawaiian lifestyle into their music.  Ashes For Your Urn is the bands second album that will deserve a look for fans of reggae & dub.  You can grab this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Playback
posts.writeup: The LA emcee has been kicking it around on some hip-hop releases lately and now hits us with a free EP showcasing his skills over jazzy hip-hop production.  You can download this for FREE HERE.  If you don't think its worth your time take a listen to the track below as your motivation.

posts.title: The Shell ft. Wordsworth & Hus
posts.writeup: Despite the obvious hints from the album cover to that name, this dude is not from Japan but rather Blanc Mesnil,  France.  Kyo Itachi (stage name) draws from his influences of a sound reminiscent of Pete Rock, Khrysis, Marco Polo, Kno, 9th Wonder..etc.  The emcees that Kyo got down with on this album is pretty impresive.  I guess you could say "real recognize real".  On Musikyo you will find verses from Wordsworth, Rasco, Wildchild, LMNO, Finale, Sense, Copyright + many more.  You can grab this album HERE.

posts.title: Salud
posts.writeup: Plum is lead by the heavenly vocals of Evan Roman and backed by Jeff Mackey (guitar) and Sean How on the keys.  Macklemore just gave us the heads up on this EP via his blog Bengalyucky.com.  Evan was featured on Macklemore's debut album, The Language Of My World, on the tracks Love Song and Contradiction.  You can grab this EP from itunes HERE.  And check out Salud below!

posts.title: Your Song (Blackmill Dubstep Remix)
posts.writeup: Ellie has been dominating the blogosphere for the last few months with her undeniable talent.  One of her strongest vocal performances on Your Song has gotten the remix treatment and can be seen at the top of Hype Machine right now. The original will always hold its own as a beautiful song with its minimal look of keys and strings.  But if you are looking for a different kind of twist with epic synths and drums take a listen to Blackmill's remix.

posts.title: Ghosts
posts.writeup: In case you haven't noticed but Trevor Hall has officially hi-jacked hillydilly.com this weekend by forcing his will through his music.  The Elephants Door was recorded back in 2007. The internet has changed so quickly over the last few years because if this album was to drop in 2010 it would be all over the blogs.  I have no idea how this album flew under the radar back in 2008.  The songs below are beyond obvious when you listen to them.  Trevor Hall has one of the more unique voices in music and will forever be a standout after taking a listen to this album. Sadly this album was never commercially released but can be now found floating around on the internet.  His label dropped him right before the album was due for a release.  (You gotta shake your head on that one).  Success is no longer measured in record sales so lets get the word out about this guy's music.  We are the force behind the change so lets get that crap of the radio!

posts.title: Well I say
posts.writeup: This Is Blue was a stripped down studio album that Trevor recorded in 2008 with an acoustic guitar and percussion from Chris Steele.  Take a listen below and go from soulless to soulful as soon as that first chord is hit.  (That album art must of really hurt the budget)  Get this album physically or digitally from CDbaby HERE.

posts.title: Standing Tall
posts.writeup: E-dubble has a simple formula that has worked very well for the past year during his weekly freestyles and that is make good honest music.  The people have responded by writing about him on their blogs and have now pushed his music on many ears in 2010.  I would say his path is a great story for aspiring artists.  If you make good music the people will support you.  Think about it: If you see a show poster with e-dubble's  name on it, you will go see that show.  E-dubble has taken the ball in 2010 and now has all our ears in anticipation for whats to come!  Freestyle #47 uses a Smith Western's sample that gives us another uniquely chopped up sample for e-dubble to drop his weekly raps on us.  Download below.

posts.title: Troublemakers Ft Raekwon, Method Man & Redman
posts.writeup: Ghostface will always be a welcome sight for hip-hop heads as he always represents with a style and sound that is timeless. Apollo Kids is Pretty Toney's 6th solo album that will be released on Dec 21st. Right from the get go on Apollo Kids you get a wave of awareness that reminds you why Ghostface is a benchmark for quality in hip-hop. All the man needs is solid production and on Apollo Kids you can expect the beats to match the skill of the emcee. Purified Thoughts is the lead track on Apollo Kids that features GZA and Trife over mulit-layered chanting samples with drums that will make you want to BLAST this out your car. The verse from GZA is a sight for sore ears and makes you wish for some new material from him. Apollo Kids also features Redman, Method Man, Raekwon, U-God, Busta Rhymes, Jim Jones, Sheek Louch, Black Thought, The Game + more. Make this album a quick additon to your xmas wish list because it is definitely worth the purchase. Look for it on Dec 21st.

posts.title: And It Goes
posts.writeup: Red Clay released their debut, Da Royal Era circa 2003 on what looked like a blank CDR.  But regardless of the appearances, the album was a good one full of boom bap raw hip-hop.  Red Clay is Baron (emcee), Ten Chakar (DJ/Producer) and General Lou (Hypeman).  And It Goes was a song that was adored by friends who enjoyed all types of music thus making it a personal favorite from the mid 2000's.  Today its time to hear what you missed.  Check out what Red Clay is up to via their Reverbnation page HERE.

posts.title: Cigarettes Will Kill You Ft Jane Tyrrell
posts.writeup: Ben Lee's - Cigarettes Will Kill You (yea I have never heard of it either).  I have said this before and I will say it again: Jane Tyrrell has the voice of an angel and will hopefully visit me in my dreams someday to sing to me.  That may be borderline obsessive but after years of Jane tearing apart my heart with her vocals it only increases with each song she is on.  Sadly she is already taken by her boss/husband Urthboy (he runs the label Elephant Traks).  But you are here for the music and not my pointless rants on females.  So use your mouse and listen to the song below.  Download and share!

posts.title: Comme Des Enfants (Le Matos Andy Carmicheal Remix)
posts.writeup: Coer De Pirate's wonderful voice first hit my hears a few months ago with her feature on the latest Bedouin Soundclash record with the best track on that album, Brutal Hearts.  Naturally whenever I see her on anything from now on its going to get my attention.  And with this remix it will deserve to hit your ears.  This song is en français for those of us that speak English, but it does not matter because it's a great song regardless.

posts.title: Election Time
posts.writeup: Wyclef Jean has been a very active ambassador for Haiti recently by using his musical clout to bring much needed attention to the situation in Haiti after the recent earthquake.  In August of 2010 he ran for the Haitian presidency but was unfortunately denied due to complications of the requirements to live in Haiti for at least 5 years.  It's a damn shame because I am sure he stood a real chance to get elected.  This EP garners Wyclef's motivation and spirit behind his desires to make a real change for his homeland.  Just because Wyclef was denied the chance to run for president the man will continue to make a change through awareness and educating us on the real face of Haiti and not let us get our info from biased media outlets.  Wyclef is a good man and so is his music on this EP.  You can grab this on itunes HERE.

posts.title: Counterfeit Girl
posts.writeup: Counterfeit Girl is a hidden jem that can only be found on Sense Boardwear's compilation album, Livin Life To The Fullest from 2005.  Sense Boardwear released 3 compilations from 2002 to 2005 and are now officially out of print and are not being sold digitally.  Although I think its a travesty that these guys are not selling it digitally now.  I am guessing there was no digital distribution agreement when these compilations were made back then.  You can however grab vol 4 + 5 and as well as their latest acoustic compilation that features a who's who of the reggae.rock scene.  You can grab everything from the Pier.org store HERE.  For now just enjoy this reggae jam from the Long Beach group.

posts.title: So Far Gone
posts.writeup: J-Boog's music has the power to induce thoughts of being in Hawaii on a rock cliff overlooking the ocean as the sun sets with his amazing voice.  So Far Gone is the second single J-Boog has released in the last 2 months (via itunes).  One can only assume he will be dropping his sophomore album soon.  It was Boog's 2006 debut, Hear Me Roar that got all of us hooked on his brand of soulful reggae and with the hype of these singles we can expect the new album to be great .  Support the man and grab So Far Gone via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Youve Got The Love (Theophilus London Remix)
posts.writeup: Brooklyn emcee, Theophilus London has taken the leisure to go in over The Xx's cover of You've Got The Love that was originally done by Florence And The Machine.  The verse fits perfectly within the track prompting us to wonder if these 2 acts could do some work together in the future.  This tracks was spotted at TheChuckness.com, its a great site and I definitely recommend it.

posts.title: Lights
posts.writeup: Ellie Goulding makes wonderful music for human beings.  Are you a human? Yea ok well if you are then there is no way you can listen to Ellie and not tell me her voice is amazing.  She  tastefully combines folk and pop with electronica.  It's a mix that goes very well and will NOT be a guilty pleasure.  Her music shall be something for a different kind of mood and a switch up from the everyday blasting of hip-hop.  So if your like me give your soul a switch up and let Ellie do her thing.  You can grab this album via iTunes HERE.

posts.title: The Same Tattoos (Sabzi Remix)
posts.writeup: Sabzi and Fences have just released a second remix collaboration between the Blue Scholars beatsmith and Seattle folk singer Fences. This remix works just as well as My Girl The Horse and makes you wonder if these two will release an EP or album in the future. Get this single via iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Echoes (Feat. Access Immortal)
posts.writeup: Cologne, Germany producing mates JR and PH7 are back again with their follow up to their debut album, The Standard from 2009.  The Update features a montage of emcees from the US from the likes of Elzhi, Evidence, Bambu, Rasco, Trek Life, Oddisee, Sean Price, Access Immortal, Strange Fruit Project, Buff1 plus many more.  Get this via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Castle Walls Ft Christina Aguilera
posts.writeup: There is no denying the fact that T.I has the skills with his words when he uses that southern drawl in his raps. Without ever knowing anything about T.I you can tell the man carries a healthy confidence. T.I comes off as someone who is doing something that they SHOULD be doing. Beside the fact that (for those of you who are jaded towards "rap") T.I leans more towards the rap than the hip-hop, its impossible not to deny the quality, sincerity and truth in his voice. One of the highlight tracks on the album, Castle Walls is a crossover jam that will appeal to a wider audience with assisted vocals from Christina Aguilera. This song is just simply good and I implore you not to like it. No Mercy also contains That's All She Wrote with Eminem that will be given the mandatory repeat treatment of one month. I am a few weeks in right now on that trend but still can't lock down Eminem's vocals. Good luck with that because Eminem goes NUTS and drops one of his best verses ( the 2nd verse) in years! You can check out the rest of the album by purchasing it HERE when it drops on Dec 7th.

posts.title: Palm Trees
posts.writeup: Not only is E-dubble chewing on the AT-4050 but he will also be chewing up your ears with this weeks Freestyle Friday #45 that samples Billy Joel.  It has been awhile since I have posted up an E-dubble freestyle but with this week it has caught my ear and will be getting some repeat spins for sure.  E-dubble is an emcee to keep your eye on as there is no doubt the man has more good music in store for us.  Keep up with him via Facebook.

posts.title: Share My Sky
posts.writeup: As Madcon have continued to grow along with their fame so has their music and this time around for their 4th studio album the guys have taken their sound towards a more pop/commercial appeal.  Don't be mislead by the word commercial because regardless of the mass appeal of the new material it is still good and will deserve a good look.  If you have been a fan of Madcon since the early days you will not be disappointed.  Just get a hold of this album and sit down with it for a few hours.  Let it sink in, play out and have some drinks with it.  Before long you will be digging what you are hearing.  Or maybe not because they sure do love their autotune now.  Contraband is out now and can be grabbed HERE (Norway itunes only).

posts.title: Sacrifice I
posts.writeup: Sacrifice I is another one of those rare songs that has probably never been unearthed in the current era of blogging and gives it that much more necessity to pass it on to you guys today.  The Prophet is an emcee from the Bay Area who is still making music today, you can check out his myspace page HERE.  Sacrifice I is taken from his 2003 album Block Venom that you can get HERE.

posts.title: Revolution
posts.writeup: Mighty Mystic has all the key elements in what makes reggae music good:  the voice, the instrumentation and the words.  Don't be fooled by the ordinary cover for this album because it really does not do justice for the music contained on Wake Up The World.  Kevin Mark Holness aka Mighty Mystic gets his musical roots from his youth in Jamaica but now has been growing from the influence of the big city life in Boston for the last 20 years.  Mighty Mystic has combined his life experiences into some really great music that makes up for a pretty damn good debut album.  You can grab this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: I Need A Dollar (Bapt Edit)
posts.writeup: Music is becoming such a huge factor in everybody's lives lately.  Who doesn't have ipod earbuds in? Who doesn't have way more knowledge of independent music these days? And which country is not putting out great music?  Music is a necessity for us humans and on a worldwide scale its starting to show.  Take Baptman for example with his eclectic production style from France.  Over the last 5 years artists like Baptman are starting to get their name heard through the internet and its a wonderful thing because this music needs to be heard.  Under The Influence is a remix project that was inspired by the documentary Influencers.  I have yet to watch it but after reading the description you can bet I will be watching it.  Under The Influence features remixes of Mike Snow, J. Cole, Common, Jay Dee, Aloe Blacc + many more.  Baptman's remix of Aloe Blacc's I Need A Dollar is the best remix I have heard of that track and his reggae remix of Animal is fuggin aweseome!  Download this project for FREE HERE and check out Baptman's myspace here.

posts.title: Coffee + Snow 2
posts.writeup: Here goes the mp3 version of Coffee + Snow 2 for those of you that wish to have this on your ipod.  Check the visuals in the post below if you have not seen the video already.

posts.title: Daily Greens
posts.writeup: Beatballs is an instrumental collection that Sabzi has compiled for The Meatball Shop in NYC.  I am not going to tell you Sabzi's story about how this project came to be because I am sure he would rather you read it from his words.  But what I can show you are 2 of my favorite beats from Beatballs and the link to go download this yourself .  "Meatballs in your mouth, Beatballs in your ear"  Below is a new video sabzi, geo and zia (the director) just released that follows up the coffee and snow themed video from 2008.  Its pretty funny because right now sabzi is in NYC with the warmer weather and they show him holding a pineapple to accentuate the tropic nature of NYC.  Sabzi also drops a few bars at the end as well.

posts.title: It Lives
posts.writeup: Corucopia was Zavala and Onry's free album, Believeyoume that dropped in 2009.  It Lives was the instant stand out from Believeyoume and if you would like to hear more go HERE to download it.  And while your at it check out each of their other releases (links above) and get in the know of one of the coolest new hip-hop acts.  Once again Zavela provides his progressive style of production that sounds like it was composed in zero gravity.

posts.title: I Just Wanna See Ft K.O The Knockou
posts.writeup: Soulchef is a producer/composer from Auckland, New Zealand.  He joins good company with this style of hip-hop that has a solid niche in the music world.  Your usual suspects are on Escapism including the always dope Tunji, The 49ers, Nieve + many more.  You can grab this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: A Fire Burns For Freedom
posts.writeup: The eldest Marley keeps the tradition going with his latest endeavour in music with his "Wild And Free" single series where he is giving away his songs for free via his website.  A Fire Burns For Freedom is his latest giveaway that you can download below.  You can also download the first free song Africa Land on his website.

posts.title: Waiting On The Rain
posts.writeup: The Hawaiian via Compton singer J-Boog is back once again to follow up his breakthrough debut album Hear Me Roar from 2006.  Hear Me Roar contained J-Boog's most successful single, Ganja Farmer.  Waiting On The Rain is the lead single from J-Boog's new album that should be dropping soon.  I can't find any info as what the title or the date the album will drop.  But rest assured It will be on here when it does! Buy the single from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Echo Ft Liz Rodrigues
posts.writeup: Eminem and Royce have linked back up again to form the deadly duo aptly dubbed, Bad Meets Evil.  Echo is the second leak from the supposed new  Bad Meets Evil EP that will be dropping soon.  Judging from the first real taste of this album with Echo there is no doubt that Em and Royce will have our ears in anticipation of its release.  Featured on the track is female vocalist Liz Rodrigues who was also featured on 25 To Life from Eminems latest, Recovery.  DJ Khalil is the one responsible for the production.  Credit to 2DBZ.

posts.title: Thats All She Wrote Ft Eminem
posts.writeup: T.I will be dropping his new album , No Mercy on Dec 7th and this is the first lead single from the album that sees Eminem on the feature tip.  All She Wrote has been on blast for the last day and is one of those great rap songs that is hard not to like.

posts.title: My Girl The Horse (Sabzi Unscrewed Remix)
posts.writeup: Sabzi has just released another remix to another one of the Seattle's 'Townfolk' and that is singer Fences. Fences is a indie/folk singer with an album and a EP under his belt. Sabzi has taken the Fences track My Girl The Horse and has injected it with his musical touch. I would assume the majority of you (who frequent this site) have only heard of Fences due to his recent collaboration with Macklemore on the Otherside Remix. You can head over to Sabzi's tumblr page and read up on his mini-interview that he did with Fences (complete with a video). They released 2 different versions of the remix to My Girl The Horse with the Sabzi preferred screwed vocals and the Fences/Ryan Lewis preferred version without the screwed vocals. I have included the remix below (that I liked) without the pitched shifted BG vocals but if you would like to hear the other version and agree with Sabzi then you better download the whole thing via Sabzi's blog ( just give em your email). Also included is the instrumental. Make sure you tweet at @sabzi and @ffeenncceess and let em know which version you liked better with the hashtag of #FENCESxSABZI. If you would like to hear the original of My Girl The Horse you can get it on Fences' album that was released in Sept of this year HERE.

posts.title: Straight High
posts.writeup: Straight High was one of those tracks from back in 2003 that will always go down as standout from that time period. Reverse Priorities is a 5 man Vancouver based hip-hop group who never really pushed their music on a big scale. To be honest I don't even know if these guys still make music or even do shows anymore. You can grab the album with Straight High on it digitally or physically from CDbaby HERE. It has been many years since I have taken a listen to their self-titled album and who knows there could be more be other good tracks on there that is missed.... They still have a myspace page up as well.

posts.title: Mattafix [Throwback Tuesday]
posts.writeup: In light of the sad news of the UK duo Mattafix breaking up today and since we are in discussion of the throwback posts I have decided that you new readers NEED to know about Mattafix. They are Marlon (vocals) and Preteesh (beats) who make some incredible music. Marlon is the singer with the amazing vocal range that makes you wonder if it's actually a female singing sometimes (no diss). Don't get it twisted and think the lower toned Caribbean accented raps are Preteesh. Nope its alllll Marlon. He gets this accent from growing up in the multi island nation of St. Vincent & The Grenadines. Mattafix on the musical end, musically, is untouchable. Preteesh carves out his own sound in ways that people dream about. Take Angel for example, that beat has so much feeling in it that it literally cuts your chest open. Anyone who has a love for music will get into Mattafix, GUARANTEED. I have never seen so many people from all walks of genres have a soft side for Mattafix. Shake Your Limbs, Angel & Things Have Changed are all from their 2007 release Rhythm and Hymns which you can get from iTunes HERE. And the absolute classic Big City Life from their debut in 2005, Signs of A Struggle. Grab Signs of A Struggle HERE. Both albums are very very good and will always be a go to sound that will be sorely missed.

posts.title: Wake Up
posts.writeup: The Green (repping Oahu, Hawaii) are quite possibly the next group from the reggae.dub section to fall onto your lap and never leave your side.  The Green create mellow mood music that keeps you in the now with positive vibes.  Going through this album is pretty damn easy, prepare to give yourself a good hour of listening attention.  You can catch these dudes on tour right now, hit up their Myspace page for dates and while your at it become a fan on Facebook HERE.  You can also take a visit to their site and download an additional 4 tracks for free in exchange for your email address.  Go ahead and grab this album HERE.

posts.title: Before Im Gone
posts.writeup: J. Cole - Friday Night Lights - Mixtape - 2010(Hip-Hop)If you are a doubter of the hype that J. Cole is for real, you need to listen to the best representation of what this guy has to offer below.  I admit I am always weary of internet hype via hip-hop blogs that just blast us with crap all day but J. Cole has the skills to back up all this talk.  Regardless of what you have heard or read about J.Cole will all change when you hear what J.Cole offers up on his latest free mixtape.  Look for his official debut in Spring 2010.  You can grab this for FREE HERE.

posts.title: Maybe
posts.writeup: Maybe is the itunes bonus cut from Mr. Rager's latest album Man On The Moon II: The Legend of Mr. Rager that is in stores and available online right now. Maybe is a spaced out track with Cudi doing what he does best.  Go HERE to get the links to purchase MOTMII.

posts.title: Chain Heavy Ft Talib Kweli & Consequence
posts.writeup: G.O.O.D Fridays continue to roll on despite Kanye just putting out one of the best albums in the last decade. Back when Kanye was doing his promotional visits to the offices of Facebook + Twitter there were youtube clips of some acapellas of Ye spitting verses from his new album. Chain Heavy was one of these songs along with Mama's Boyfriend. Both of these tracks did not make the final cut for My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy. Chain Heavy was released last night that features Q-tip on the beat and Talib x Consequence on the raps. Lets hope Mama's Boyfriend gets released aswell because the lyrics are awesome. Check the vid HERE.

posts.title: All Of The Lights
posts.writeup: If this album could be compared to classic works of art then My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy would be hanging in the Louvre right now behind 3 inch thick glass and a lineup that goes out the door. This album is a career rejuvenating album that will shoot Kanye back onto the tongues of people discussing the sheer magnitude of how good this album is. Kanye has evolved into a league of his own. You can not compare anyone to Kanye. The way he combines music and art is beautiful and on this album he has crafted a classic. Each track is a separate sonic story that depicts a piece of the puzzle to conform to your personal daydreams. These songs fall into the "epic" category that can vary from 4 minutes to 9 minutes (minus the interludes) and take more twists and turns than a episode of True Blood. You might as well delete everything you have on your iPod and just put this album on it because it's all you are going to listen to for the next 2 months. MBDTWF will easily be your favorite album of the year. Featured on MBDTWF is Kid Cudi, John Legend, Rick Ross, Nicki Minaj, Bon Iver, Jay-Z, Pusha T, Raekwon, RZA, Cyhi Da Prynce, Elton John + many more. Enough talk just do yourself a favor and pre-order this right now from itunes HERE, it will be released on Nov 22nd.

posts.title: Miss Busdriver (Rachel)
posts.writeup: Grey Crow is a masterpiece full of sonic wizardy that can easily be called one of the years best.  Grey Crow is Eligh's newest solo project album since 2003's Poltergeist.  The cohesiveness and quality is evident from beginning to end on Grey Crow.  Eligh's production skills are at an all time high and it shines brighter than the sun on Grey Crow.  Eligh refers to himself as the "Grey Crow" that references  the growth and wisdom gained from his struggles with drug addiction over the past few years.  (Eligh is now 5 years clean). The struggles have morphed Eligh into a better musician and with this new found direction in life the music has also been given an upgrade.  Grey Grow is not just a good underground hip-hop album but an album that deserves a listen from all types of music listeners.  There is no denying the massive amount of skills on the raps and production on Grey Crow.  Featured on Grey Crow is Inspired Flight, Brother Ali, The Grouch, K-Flay, Zumbi of Zion I, Scarub, Aesop Rock + many more.  Make sure you support Eligh as this album has no major label backing.  This album will succeed on the backs of people like us who love good music.

posts.title: On Repeat
posts.writeup: The Looptroop Rockers are back with a brand new song that was inspired by the recent elections in Sweden.  It is in response to a racist party being voted into Swedish parliament.  Their feelings of frustration have clearly fueled the inspiration for the song and are now channeling it to us.  Promoe, Embee, Supreme & Cos M.I.C have never shied away from saying whats on their mind and On Repeat is a song that us revolutionaries can appreciate.  History repeating itself has always been evident throughout our time and now we have a dope song for this subject.  Peep the visuals below.

posts.title: Cameras
posts.writeup: This is another one of those tracks that has not left my playlist over the last week so it is time to pass it on to you guys.  This album has been well received on the internets from a lot of people so I had to check out it for myself.  I like the production on this album but the vocals not so much.  Yea I understand this is prob the worst blurb on a album ever but hey I could of just not wrote about it.  Any press is good press right?  You can get this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: How We Live Ft Hassaan Mackey
posts.writeup: The Left is Detriot hip-hop personified with the concussion inducing production from Apollo Brown and raw raps from Journalist103.  The cuts are handled by DJ Soko.  Gas Mask is a great surprise with the amount of quality packed in this album.  You can grab this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Polarity
posts.writeup: Atomic Clock is Zion I's 5th studio album that is being released on Nov 9th. Every time Zion I release an album the sound has always progressed and has never been stagnant. Atomic Clock carries that tradition of bringing us a new sound with tracks like Many Stylez (w/Rebelution) that mix the hip-hop with the reggae skanks. Amplive and Zumbi have once again delivered an album for music heads to slow cook in the oven leaving our stomachs full of healthy musical energy. You can pre-order the album right now HERE and get 5 songs for free before the release date on Nov 9th. For fans of The Grouch, Eligh, Living Legends.

posts.title: Champion Ft Laura Reed
posts.writeup: The Venezuelan born rapper, Emilo Rojas is dropping another free mixtape with DJ Green Lantern on November 11th.  Emilio has always been a solid emcee making good music on his mix tapes and features for some time now.  He busted onto the scene back in the mid 2000's surfacing on those milk crate compilations.  He called himself Sycorax-One back then has changed his name more times than Diddy since then.  The man has not had an official album yet but is proving that you can still make some noise without an official album.  Champion is a punch in the kidney with a headnodic beat from Illmind and slick rapping from Rojas.  Look for Life Without Shame on here when it drops.  Check out his last free project HERE.

posts.title: Bright Lights Bigger City
posts.writeup: Sometimes life can be very easy and when it comes to anything that Cee-Lo Green does it is both easy and simple to know that this dude is on top of his game.  The Lady Killer has been on the back of every music fan's mind since we were blown away from the awesome lead single 'Fuck You'.  The rest of this album will not disappoint.  It carries that classic soul sound and features perfect production for Cee-Lo to sing his ass off.  The production is handled by the likes of Fraser T. Smith, Jack Splash, Salaam Remi, Paul Epworth, + more.  Show this album to everyone you know no matter what age they are because this album is one for the people!!  The Lady Killer drops on Nov 9th.  Get it HERE.

posts.title: All Of The Lights Ft John Legend, Kid Cudi, The Dream, Rihanna, Charlie Wilson, Elly Jackson, Elton John, Fergie, Ryan Leslie...
posts.writeup: All Of The Lights is the rumored next single to be released from Kanye's new album dropping on Nov 22nd.  The guest list on this track is long enough to crash your ipod.  The horns on this track have been stuck in my head ever since I first heard the piece of this song on the Runway film.  Through the first taste of this album on Runway I am pretty sure nothing will touch this album this year.  All Of The Lights is absolutely epic and will be stuck on replay for the next week.  Pictured above is the one of 5 front covers of My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy.  This cover is the controversial one Kanye posted on his twitter stating that this one is banned in the US.  If you look on the itunes pre-order page you can see its been blurred out.  LAME.  Anyways pre-order the album HERE.

posts.title: Logic
posts.writeup: The BC Trees have taken a cheese grater to multiple music genres and melted it down into one tasty mix that can go with anything.  The BC Trees are Matt Houghland aka Glympse (Vocals), Eddie Houghland (Bass), Ted Campbell (Guitar), Marek (Drums) and Madhu (Backup Vocals).  One could describe their music as hippie-hip-hop with their topics on the various trees species, gardening, eating veggies and taking stabs at Monsanto.  Matt's vocals are clear, precise and easy to understand so you can bet you will be rapping along once you get to know the words.  One of the main musical focal points on this album is the guitar work from Ted Campbell.  The mans guitar chord progressions are intoxicating and give this album a whole other dimension of dope.  The 4 tracks below are some of my favorite tracks off the album.  If you are not convinced yet take a listen to Revolution and tell me you are not amped!!  Imperfect Paradise is the BC Trees debut album.  It was recorded at AI studios in Vancouver that was engineered by me during my time in audio school.  Additional thanks goes out to Warren Larkam for his assistant engineering duties.  The BC Trees and I would also like to thank Mike Foster from Blackfish Sound for mixing, photos, advice and his countless hours working on this album.
