Mike was also responsible for engineering Revolution, Walking Through The Door and Get Inside.  You can get the entire album for FREE from Bandcamp GO >> HERE .  Become a fan of Facebook HERE.

posts.title: Rasta Love Ft Ky-Mani Marley
posts.writeup: Kingston, Jamaica artist Protoje is set to release his debut album, The Seven Year Itch in 2011 and this is our first peak at the album.  I have never heard of this guy but after reading his bio and listening to this song, Protoje is for real.  It's great to hear Ky-Mani again, his 2007 album Radio is VERY underrated.  You gotta love his weird chants throughout the song.

posts.title: Keep On Ft Slim Kid Tre
posts.writeup: Iron Lyon is a well respected DJ/emcee from NYC who can be seen spinning with the likes of DJ Premier, Questlove, Q-Tip, Funkmaster Flex, etc...  The Foundation is his latest project that showcases his raps and turntablism. It also features appearances from Sean Price, Slim Kid Tre, Edo G, Craig G and Vordul Mega.  You can get this EP for 5 bucks from bandcamp HERE.  Check out his website HERE.    -

posts.title: Ninety Now Promo (Bucks Four Course Meal)
posts.writeup: No sleep recordings is a new record label that specializes in releasing shelved/unreleased, never heard before recordings, instrumentals, remixes and re-issues.  No Sleep Recordings is focusing on the 'classic' era of hip-hop from the period of '86 to 96'.  No Sleep Recordings released this sampler to give us a taste of some of the gems these guys have swept the dust off.  The two Celph Titled & Buckwild songs are not off of Nineteen Ninety Now and that Nautilis track embodies that classic sound with a absolute banger of a beat.  No Sleep Recordings are responsible for releasing Nineteen Ninety Now.  Check out their myspace HERE.

posts.title: Doncamatic Ft Daley
posts.writeup: The Gorillaz have just surprised us with a new single that features newcomer Daley on the vocals.  Included with the single is a remix done by Joker and I think it outshines the original. Take a listen below,  it's a pretty fresh track.  F

posts.title: Marijuana
posts.writeup: The expectations are sky high with Cudi's sophomore album that follows up one of the best debut albums I have ever heard.  Cudi spoke of wanting to make this album a more positive upbeat album but found that during the recording process he was struggling to write those types of songs due to the head space he was in.  It has been well documented that he has been struggling with drugs and dealing with the life change he has gone through in the last few years.  MOTM2 is even more mellow and darker that his debut.  Cudi recently just publicly made it known that he is tired of rapping and sadly it really does show on this album.  I do not get a sense of enthusiasm or inspiration from listening to this album at all.  From my perspective it seems Cudi is just on cruise control.  I am actually at a loss of words and am actually a bit sad.  I love Kid Cudi but this album does not reflect his true potential as and artist and human being.  That being said its still an album that you need to hear to judge yourself.  MOTM2 hits stores Nov 9th.  Pre-order it HERE.

posts.title: The Show Goes On
posts.writeup: Lupe's new album, Lasers, will finally be released in March of 2011.  The Show Goes On is the first single from the album produced by Kane Beatz that samples Modest Mouse.  You can buy a higher quality version of this track from Lupe HERE.

posts.title: Its A Stick Up
posts.writeup: Blame One is an emcee that you should be familiar with by now.  The dude always drops consistent good albums on a yearly basis.  Last year it was Day Chasing Days and this year its Endurance. Check out my favorite track off the album below.  Get this album digitally HERE and physically HERE.

posts.title: Bright Lights Bigger City
posts.writeup: Cee-Lo has let loose another track from his highly anticipated album, The Lady Killer that will see a release date on Nov 9th.  You can pre-order the album HERE.  The track features a walking bass line, intense 80's synth, a string quartet, guitar strings and the classic vocals of Cee-Lo.  The Lady Killer should be one of the years best (hopefully).

posts.title: Fuckmaster Sex
posts.writeup: Celph Titled is the king of the punchline and has so many quotables that he could make the bible look like a short story.  It has been awhile since we have last heard a full length Celph Titled album.  His last project was back in 2007 with his joint album with Apathy, No Place Like Chrome.  As clearly shown on the cover this album is entirely produced by Buckwild from the legendary D.I.T.C crew.  The production has that nineties feel with dusty drums, dj cuts and true school rhymes from rappers who have always represented for the real heads.  This album will be released on Oct 26th digitally and physically.  Swashbuckling is an easy favorite with 4 different beat switchups and some great verses from Celph, Apathy, Esoteric and Ryu from Styles of Beyond.

posts.title: Fantasy
posts.writeup: Hillydilly.com is proud to introduce you to the BC Trees from Vancouver, BC. The Trees are brothers Matt (vocals) & Eddie (bass) with Ted (Guitar) and Marek (Drums). Back in July 2009 when I was attending audio engineering school I was presented with a recording project to record one full song. At the time I was in touch with the guys from a Vancouver based reggae/rock band Fuzzcat and their guitarist Ted. Ted told me he had another band on the go and would love to get some tracks recorded. I had absolutely no expectations and the plan was just to record 1 or 2 songs. But obviously this was not the case. I will never forget the moment we started recording. I was immediately blown away and was instantly inspired. We ended up recording 9 tracks that night and the rest is history. A year and a half later the album is mixed, mastered and ready for your ears. I know you guys will love this album and for your first taste we have selected the lead single 'Fantasy'. The debut album, Imperfect Paradise will be released for FREE on Nov 2nd on Hillydilly and also via their Bandcamp page. To become a fan on Facebook go HERE.

posts.title: Looking For Love (But Not So Sure)
posts.writeup: Over the course of the past year producer Pretty Lights has released three free EP's with Glowing In The Darkest Night being the last of 2010.  You can grab the other 2 EP's plus this EP for FREE from his website HERE.  His compositions are layered like a black forest cake with so many sounds that it tantalizes all of your senses.  Case and point with Looking For Love as it will easily blow your mind.  You will find yourself picking apart each sound and being blown away how he fits it all together.  Thank god we are the listeners.

posts.title: Lead Us
posts.writeup: Katchafire have gained legendary status within the reggae dub scene over the years with their relentless touring and quality work on their albums.  On The Road Again is their newest album since 2007's Say What You're Thinking.  This album was released on Oct 11th, so you can grab the physical HERE and digital HERE.  On The Road Again is a smooth massage for the soul that flows from front to back.

posts.title: Vipassana (Ryan Lewis Remix)
posts.writeup: Seattle hip-hop is officially taking over the hip-hop world right now and on the front lines are Macklemore and Ryan Lewis with their arsenal of beats and rhymes that turns spears into bullets. Just short of a year ago these guys dropped the infamous VS. EP (some of you may remember that as one of last years best). The EP set off a bomb that hit music lovers all over the world and is now getting gasoline on the fire with the VS. Redux EP. We get remixes from Budo, Jake One, P-Smoov, Ryan Lewis and the Town Remix by Sabzi. This EP is not for free this time but it is only 6 bucks. Earlier today the album peaked at #8 on the itunes hip-hop charts! It's so great to watch everyone support these guys and make it up that far. Get the EP from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Things Have Changed (feat. Pismo)
posts.writeup: The Japanese six piece band are back again with another wonderful musical escapade full of instrumentation that will tug on your heart strings.  Japanese musicians/beatmakers are truly the masters of composing music when it comes to keys, horns and string sections.  The Eternal Truth instrumental is absolutely beautiful.  You can just close your eyes and let this track overwhelm you with emotions.  Its simply magnificent.  Transcended Elements features a star studded lineup of CL Smooth, Camp Lo, Substantial, Aloe Blacc, De La Soul, Guru + more.  Listen to more songs from their album via myspace.  Grab this via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Ray Ban Vision Ft Cyhi Da Prynce
posts.writeup: Cyhi Da Prynce wants you to know that he always rocks his Ray Bans no matter what he is doing.  Go ahead listen, he will tell you.  A-Trak has set up the jungle gym with this absolute banger.  The beat basks in all its glitchy glory that could have Christopher Hawking cutting up the rug.  Its hard not to enjoy this track purely on this beat provided by Kanye's old DJ.  This track is from a new compilation, Fools Gold Vol. 1 that is set to be released on Oct 25th.  This compilation is being released on A-trak's newly formed label with DJ Nick Catchdubs.

posts.title: Make Alotta Money
posts.writeup: Apathy is back with a new EP in the works and this is his first single from the new EP.  The disappointment and anger with the way hip-hop and the music industry has gone has clearly pissed off Apathy.  Make Alotta Money lays it all out and Apathy holds nothing back.  Apathy has always quietly been a force to be reckoned with in hip-hop and with Make Alotta Money he once again puts down his two cents over a dope instrumental.  Apathy makes me feel quilty after listening to this song and probably hates me as well.

posts.title: So Gangster
posts.writeup: Pigeon John has always been dropping his own kind of music on his terms with a style that is all his own. Pigeon first caught everyone's attention back in 2003 with his second album, Is Dating Your Sister. It was highlighted by the awesome Life Goes On Ft Abstract Rude. Ever since that track I have been a fan and have followed what ever he has done. Dragon Slayer is his latest album in which he has strayed away from the majority of hip-hop production to more of a pop/alternative/indie feel. You can grab this album HERE.

posts.title: The Otherside Remix ft. Fences
posts.writeup: Macklemore & Ryan Lewis are gearing up to re-release their highly acclaimed VS. EP that dropped last year. This re-released version will feature bonus tracks and remixes from P-Smoov, Sabzi, Jake One + more. The EP will be released on Oct 20th coinciding with the big show with the Blue Scholars & Brother Ali going down at the Paramount in Seattle. Get your ticketsHERE. Macklemore & Ryan Lewis will also be selling their brand new Irish Celebration tees as well. Sadly I won't be able to make it but If you are in Seattle and you are not going to this show, wtf is wrong with you?!! This remix features fellow Seattle native, Fences, who adds his soothing vocals to the remix provided by Ryan Lewis. Macklemore's vocals are re-recorded with the same lyrics. The instrumental is lead by slow moving piano chords that picks up with a kick drum that sounds like a big piece of aluminum being hit with a beater (It could of easily been a cool micing technique). But nevertheless its a great change up that adds to the emotion of this epic song. Not to be forgotten are the strings, guitar chords and vocal samples that add layers of texture to this beautiful remix. So go ahead and enjoy.

posts.title: Switchin Lanes
posts.writeup: Just caught this atop the hype machine charts and I must say that I am stoked to hear Cudi on a track where he is rhyming instead of his signature melodies.  This Emile produced track was originally recorded in 2009 for the video game Midnight Club: Los Angeles.  Pictured above is the official cover art for Man On The Moon II: The Legend Of Mr. Rager which drops on Nov 9th. Seen @ Indie Shuffle.

posts.title: Words Of Wisdom
posts.writeup: Seeing as reporting on good music is my job I just showed up 3 hours late and pissed off the boss. Because It took me a few weeks to come around to this one. Pep Rally was released for a free download at the end of Sept. You can grab it for free HERE. Pep Rally is full of flipped samples from The Black Keys, The Cold War Kids, Beach House, Yaysayer + many more. Below are my favorite cuts from the mixtape.

posts.title: Drive On
posts.writeup: Ok here is a simple thing I want you to do.  You come to this site for a reason right?  So I can direct you to the good music.  Well there is a big neon sign pointing towards this album.  Are you ready to hear one of the best new reggae.dub.rock bands you probably have never heard? (Or maybe you have).  Hearing music like this reminds you that there are bands out there in this genre that can still hold a unique sound that is original.  A Certain Shade will have your attention within seconds.  A light will go off in your head and from there these guys will take care of the rest.  After a solid listen through this album I am left to go over this one again.  Yes its that good.  You can preview the whole album on bandcamp right now then proceed to buy this for 4 bucks!  By the way these guys are from the UK.

posts.title: Pasa de Kaka
posts.writeup: The Five One are quickly becoming one of my favorite indie bands with the music these guys keep putting out.  The Five One are the swiss army knife of music right now because with each new song they release it can be from hip-hop to rock to alternative and any other genre you can conjure up.  Pasa De Kaka is a left over song from a scrapped EP idea that never saw the light of the day.  Pasa de Kaka is led by layered acoustic guitars over some head banging drums.  Be on the lookout for their new album that will be dropping early 2011.  The video for the lead single (Mandatory) has seen 28,000 views so far and has caught the attention of MTVU by giving the guys some video spins on the channel.  Look for that video to hit your screens next week.  Also hit up their Kickstarter page and help the guys raise some money for the proper album release campaign they are hoping to push.  Hit up the page for more info and special rewards for donating your cash.  Check the video for Mandatory below.

posts.title: Maniac Ft Cage
posts.writeup: Maniac is the sixth leak from the new Cudder album, Man On The Moon II: The Legend of Mr. Rager dropping on November 9th.  Maniac is a Cage assisted ghoulish track with undertones of jaw rattling low end that adds another dimension to the senses.  The song samples St. Vincent's "The Strangers".  I am not sure if this leak is approved or what but I don't see  having six leaks from your album as a good thing.  But then again its 2010 and its going to eventually come out right?

posts.title: Miss Busdriver (Rachel)
posts.writeup: Living Legends stalwart Eligh is about to release a brand new album (GreyCrow) full of his amazing raps and production. From the 2 tracks that are included on this single EP we can expect to be in for another Living Legend classic. The lead single Miss Busdriver is a song that Eligh holds close to his heart as it is about his Aunt that passed away. Miss Busdriver is lead by beautiful layered piano chord progressions, sultry horns, nostalgic synth pads and Eligh's melodic raps. The B side is another banger featuing fellow legend Luckyiam and singer Paris Hayes. You can get this single EP complete with 2 instrumentals from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Sunshine Ft MIA
posts.writeup: Sunshine is the second single to be released from Rye Rye's new album Go! Pop! Bang! that will be released sometime next year.  Rye Rye gets the cosign from MIA and using this track as an example we can see why.  It features a funky beat with MIA on the chorus and Rye Rye on the verses with her rapping style.  Sure this song is not one of my favorites but It has been stuck on the bottom of my playlist for the past week and whenever it comes on I always listen to it.  So I am sure there are some of you who are down with this track.

posts.title: Love In Our Community Ft Aston Barrett of The Wailers & 77 Jefferson
posts.writeup: Mr. Heinrichs keeps plugging along in his music career with another solid release under his belt.  Josh enlists help from Clear Conscience, 77 Jefferson, Cas Haley, Katchafire, Aston "Family Man" Barret (Bob Marley and The Wailers) plus many more.  This album has been fairing well on the itunes charts in its first week.  Currently the album is #5 on the charts.  Hit up itunes HERE or get the album via bandcamp HERE.  Below are 2 highlights from this album.

posts.title: Slow It Down
posts.writeup: Stone Ave are like eating a freshly baked piece of bread.  Its fresh out of the oven, hot and tastes like something you could eat everyday.  These dudes bring a fresh sound with their debut album.  The album starts off with its best track, Slow It Down and will continue to cruise along.  I think you should probably check this album out if you are a fan of reggae.rock.  This album is available right now from bandcamp, go HERE to grab it.

posts.title: Drunk Girl
posts.writeup: Pepper are the thorns on the spiked bat in the reggae.rock scene as one of the go to bands when you think of the genre.  Every album these guys have put out has always been good from the classic Kona Town to more recently with No Shame and Pink Crustaceans And Good Vibrations.  There are always the people who complain that Pepper have not been true to form since In With The Old, but I beg to differ.  Stitches is an EP that is 5 tracks deep.  The guys had this to say about the recording of this EP > "These new tunes mark a exciting awakening for us. This music is all our  own and making it has been some of the best times we have ever had as  Pepper! With the construction of the  secret music chamber know as "The  Hatch", a place where life, meets music, meets a refreshed and  invigorated band.......This is just the beginning".  We can assume a new full length will follow shortly.  You can get this EP from bandcamp HERE.

posts.title: Tired
posts.writeup: Baltimore emcee E-Dubble has created a wave pool full of music that is crashing on music heads within the internets right now with his weekly Freestyle Fridays.  Each friday he will release a new track for free download.  You will find E-Dubble going over beats from RATATAT, Black Keys, Broken Bells, Tom Petty plus many more. He has a unique style that is realistic and honest.  I have gone through every single that he has released with his freestyle fridays and it should warrant you to become a fan on FB.  You can listen to all of his tracks via Reverbnation HERE.

posts.title: Call It Off
posts.writeup: The Swedish group, Hoffmaestro will make quick work of making you a fan  of their music with another album full of great music.  In Sweden these  guys are huge but over in North America not so much.  But you can see  all that changing because good music like this will travel through word  of mouth from people like you.  Hoffmaestro make genuine original music  that resonates on a level of pure enjoyment.  The musical styles on Skank-A-Tronic Punkadelica ranges  from up-tempo ska reggae to soulful ballads and back to skanking reggae  infused jams.  Its got everything you could ask for and more.  So do  the world a favor and tell a friend about these guys, hit em up on FB,  go see a show and toss these songs on your i-pod.  If you are from Sweden go HERE to get this album.

posts.title: Not At The End ft. Tunji
posts.writeup: Not At The End is the last leak from Kno's debut album Death Is Silent that is dropping digitally next Tuesday (Oct 12th) and physically Oct 26th.  Don't even bother streaming this track because you already know its going to be good.  The always dope Tunji leads this track off with another quality verse.  I wish all music was this good.

posts.title: Nothing On We
posts.writeup: Chiddy Bang have been making their rounds on the internet lately and yet to have been featured on Hillydilly.  For reasons I have no excuses for because after a listen through The Preview I am down with everything these guys are about.  The music carries an upbeat vibe with a commercial appeal reminiscent of something you would want to hear if you were in a good mood.  The Preview is more of an EP with 8 quality tracks making this a eye opener for any music fan.  The Preview is dropping on Oct 11th(UK) and Oct 12th in the US.  It will be available on itunes. Pre-order it HERE.

posts.title: Close My Eyes
posts.writeup: Offshore are a reggae/rock band based in Den Haag, Netherlands.  These guys will definitely impress reggae/rock fans with their brand of music.  The lead vocalist (Vincent Tonk) has the right kind of voice and sounds great on this album.  The instrumentation is on the mark as well especially with the horns on No Easy Way Out.

posts.title: Melodie
posts.writeup: Dub Inc was first introduced to us music junkies from Umberto Echo's remix of Dub Inc's Metissage from Dub The World.  This is of course if you have never heard of these guys before.  Dub Inc are big in the French dub scene so some of you may be shaking your heads.   Hors Controle (Out Of Control) is their newest album that was just recently released today (Oct 5th).  Most of the album is in French but there are some tracks with English. Most notably with Melodie that sees the raspy vocals of Aurélien Zohou "Komlan" contrasted with the irie vocals from Hakim Meridja "Bouchkour".  You can get this album from Amazon HERE.

posts.title: Many Stylez Ft Rebelution
posts.writeup: A few weeks ago we found out that Amplive and Zumbi were in the studio with the guys from Rebelution working on new music for Zion I's new album Atomic Clock.  The sessions proved to be a success because they are leading Atomic Clock off with the Rebelution assisted track.  Zion I always push the envelope with each new album and with Atomic Clock I am sure we will be treated with a fresh new sound.  Atomic Clock will hit stores November 9th.  You can also hit up itunes right now and check out another new song they released HERE, titled North Star.  Many Stylez is a great way to start off this album as it will please a wide range of musical tastes.

posts.title: Three Bad Men Ft Grynch & Krukid (Produced By Budo)
posts.writeup: In light of the To All My Friends Tour reaching its way to the Midwest of the States, Grieves and Budo have just released this Budo produced track that also features verses from Seattle's Grynch and Krukid.  Click the link to check for a show near you.  Of course Vancouver ain't on that list!  Check the video below of Grieves and Budo in their hotel room with two hotties giving us an update personally.

posts.title: Believe It
posts.writeup: Kokayi (pronounced Ko-Kah-Yee) is the newest artist from the Qn5 camp who has now released his debut for the Qn5 imprint.  Qn5 has always been a front runner within the music world as one of the most respectable labels for quality and being on the cutting edge of the music industry.  So its a given for any hip-hop head to know that Kokayi is the real deal if he has Qn5 behind him.  Robots & Dinosaurs is written and produced by Kokayi with the exception of the Oddisee produced track, Autumn Rules.  You will also find Tonedeff, Substantial and female vocalist Alison Carney as the featured artists.  Kokayi is a Washington, DC native who takes his name from the Swahili meaning "to summon the people".  The album is very diverse in the instrumentation from track to track leaving this album with a high level of pleasure on the musical end.  Combine that with Kokayi and his entertaining style of rapping and singing and you got yourself a must listen album.  The digital release can be grabbed HERE, but if you prefer the hard copy/ limited edition package go HERE.  The physical ships Oct 19th.

posts.title: Jump for Joy
posts.writeup: Dark Time Sunshine have been dropping some fresh new hip-hop on the people recently with album number 2 to have been released in the same year.  The first album, Vessel was the eye opener and now you will have those eyes injected with a visual drug from the contents of Cornucopia.  The beatmaker Zavala has once again hit the mark with his production all over this album.  Onry Ozzborn has never sounded better with anyone else.  You can only get this album on their tour right now with a limited amount of quantities. Check their myspace HERE or click the pic to check the tour dates.

posts.title: Sun Will Rise
posts.writeup: Ninja Tune is a London based indie label started by DJ's Matt Black and Jonathon More.  Ninja Tune are best known for their compilations they release that are full of remixes and exclusive tracks from artists all over the world.  Their latest compilation is 6 cds full of music to make your mind explode.  You will see artists like Blockhead, Bonobo, Daedelus, El-P, Roots Manuva, Prefuse 73, Speech Debelle, plus many many more.  This whole set is being released as a box set that includes a book, posters and special privileges.  More info about this box set can be read HERE.  There are only 3500 being sold, so make sure you get your hands on one of these collectors items.  Below are some of the shrapnel leftovers after the mind explosion I experienced after going through these cds.

posts.title: Pyro
posts.writeup: Relentless with their touring and recording has seen Kings Of Leon become one of the biggest bands on the planet.  On Oct 15th we will see their follow up to the amazing Only By The Night which sold 6 million copies worldwide.  Come Around Sundown was recorded in New York at Avatar Studios and is produced once again by Angelo Petraglia and Jacquire King.  Prepare to give this album hours of your time because its so easy to just let this album play right through.  Knowing which song is which will also take some time because everytime I sit down to listen to this album I always just let it play and I drift out of thought.  Through this quick examination you can't really pick a favorite.  This is the type of album that will showcase personal favorites in intervals.  Right now I am content with the whole album just playing in the background.  So as you should know by now, make sure to get your hands on this album and enjoy another solid album from the Kings Of Leon.  Pre-Order the album HERE.

posts.title: Christian Dior Denim Flow Ft Ryan Leslie, Kid Cudi, John Legend, Pusha T & Lloyd Banks
posts.writeup: During the middle of last week a track from Kanye's new album (Lost In The World) leaked to the internet and was seen on many blogs without authorization.  This track was said to be snatched from Kanye's email from a hacker.  Kanye was pissed and let it be known via his twitter that he was not going to do the G.O.O.D Friday release this week.  Needless to say I can understand this and blogging has taken another blow to its already brutal reputation.  But to no regard Kanye went ahead and released a G.O.O.D Friday track yesterday and its another good one.  It features Cudi and John Legend on the chorus with verses from Kanye, Pusha T, Cudi, Ryan Leslie and Lloyd Banks.  Kanye seems to be obsessed with adding effects to his vocals because on his verse this time he adds distortion.  I love the timbre of Kanye's voice so I wish he would just leave it alone!

posts.title: Take Off (Sky Is Falling) [M-Phazes Remix]
posts.writeup: M-Phazes has produced a beautiful new life into 360's lead single from his debut album, Falling & Flying that is due later this year.  The M-Phazes remix features a slow building beat with layered strings and off beat drums that gives this track a more serious feel than the original.  I actually like it more that the original, but that is up to you to decide, so check it out.

posts.title: Flyest (Insight Reggae Remix)
posts.writeup: After banging this album all day today I realized I missed the last track on the album which is probably the best.  So there is just no way I could not share it with you guys.  It happens to be the most reggae influenced remix as well.  Purchase this album HERE.

posts.title: It Aint Hard To Tell (Insight Reggae Remix)
posts.writeup: Not only is Insight talented with the raps but apparently he is good on the production side also.  Because his remixes across this Dancehall Is Dead project will be on repeat like those homemade sextapes you made.  Think more hip-hop reggae than dancehall.  These remixes are not mashups so expect hip-hop drums with reggae vocals and percussion samples over classic Nas tracks.  Max Tannone has made some noise on the internet lately with his Mos Dub and Dub Kweli projects, but after hearing Dancehall Is Dead I think Max may have a challenger in the arena of mixing hip-hop with reggae in 2010.  You can buy this from Amazon.com HERE.

posts.title: Brutal Hearts (ft. Coeur de Pirate)
posts.writeup: Today sparks the release date for the new Bedouin Soundclash album Light The Horizon that is the follow up to 2007's Street Gospel.  My favorite cut off the album strays away from their expected sound with the progressive percussion and awesome featured vocals of Coeur De Pirate.  You can get this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: La Petite Mort (Come Die With Me)
posts.writeup: >Kno has kindly released the second single from his new album Death Is Silent that will see a release date in a few weeks on Oct 12th.  As everyone in the music world knows that Kno is one of the best producers of our time.  You can expect this album to be on that same level.  The words I use are from no worry because the man produces amazing music year after year and Death Is Silent will no doubt continue that tradition.  La Petite Mort is a track in the minor key with a overtone of melancholy driven by an acoustic guitar and a punchy kick drum.  Check it below.

posts.title: Survival Got Me Buggin
posts.writeup: >The Others - Past Futuristic - 2004 (Hip-Hop)Past Futuristic is one of those albums that slipped through the cracks when it was released in '04 from the North Carolina based group.  The Others is 2 emcees, Johnny Madwreck and Mr. Mattic with the DJ/Producer DR.  These 2 tracks below were my favorite from the album. Purchase HERE.

posts.title: Cosa Say
posts.writeup: Original Melody are representing Iceland with some hip-hop that is worthy of your ears.  Forget the economic woes and geysers of Iceland, just think about the music from this Icelandic group from now on.  Cosa Say is their first sample of their new album that will be dropping on Oct 1st.  Check out Cosa Say with its sped up vocal samples over keys and banging drums.  Seen @ www.thewordisbond.com.

posts.title: So Appalled Ft Jay-Z, Pusha T, Swizz Beatz, RZA & Cyhi The Prynce
posts.writeup: Its another G.O.O.D Friday so its a given for music bloggers to frantically post this track up in hopes to reach the hypem charts to get some hits!  Or really just to pass on the music to the twitter-less peoples who are not geeks like me.  So Appalled is spearheaded by 2 intense verses from Ye' and Jay with standout lines, beat drops, and a chorus that is "f**kin ridiculous".  Cyhi The Prynce must be proud to get on this track because I am pretty sure no one has heard of this cat until now.  His verse is the last one with the comical line -  "If god had a iPod I'd be on his playlist".

posts.title: The Recluse (Nero Remix)
posts.writeup: Plan B has been absolutely killing it in the UK and Europe with his latest album The Defamation Of Strickland Banks.  The Recluse is the next single to be released off the album and has already gotten the remix treatment from the UK production duo, Nero.  The track is HUGE and stays true to the reasoning of doing a remix by kicking the wow factor up a few notches.

posts.title: Bound
posts.writeup: Lazerbeak  is known in our musical world as a member of the Doomtree collective as  one of the in house producers who has a creative skill that is  unmistakable.  I pictured this album would be more along the lines of  his counterpart Paper Tiger.  With mostly instrumentals and vocal  features from Doomtree members.  But to an intriguing delight it is not  what you will expect at all and that is a fresh look.  If you do a quick  google search on the man you will find out that before the Doomtree gig  he was in a indie band called The Plastic Constellations who went on for 12 years and gained critical acclaim.  Lazerbeak now has bloomed full circle on Legend Recognize Legend  to incorporate everything he has learned in his music career to blend  it all into a musical experience that will have you intrigued.   Lazerbeak wrote, sang, composed and programmed everything on this  project.  You can pre-order the album HERE.  It drops Sept 28th, 2010.

posts.title: Liquor Store Blues Ft Damian Marley
posts.writeup: If you have Damian on a track you get an instant pass to be featured on Hillydilly.  There is no production credits listed but I am pretty damn sure this is a Damian/Stephen Marley beat.  Liquor Stores Blues is from Bruno Mars' debut album, Doo-Wops & Hooligans dropping on Oct 5th (seen at 2DBZ).

posts.title: Connection
posts.writeup: Cas Haley has teamed up with Easy Star Records (Easy Star All Stars, John Brown's Body, The Black Seeds) to release his first official debut album.  Cas Haley first caught his glimpse of fame when he was on America's Got Talent a few years ago.  Now you are more likely to see him touring around the US or appearing on a John Heinrichs record.  Connection is the time for Cas to shine with a full length album that will allow him to stretch his arms out to reach a bigger audience.  The album is musically tasteful and is accentuated by Cas' incredible voice that is many octaves above your average singer.  Connection is out now, get it HERE.

posts.title: Lord Lord Lord Ft Mos Def, Swizz Beatz, Raekwon & Charlie Wilson
posts.writeup: Ye was up all night on this one with this G.O.O.D Friday installment that features a slow moving vibe with some highly notable guest verses from Mos Def & Raekwon.  Only a couple of hours ago Ye noted on his twitter that Swizz Beatz was laying down his verse.  As a music fan Fridays are beginning to become that much better!

posts.title: Ruthless
posts.writeup: >Ima Robot - Ruthless - (Single) - 2010(Rock/Pop/Alternative/Indie)Ima Robot is Alex Ebert who is also the lead singer of Edward Sharpe And The Magnetic Zero's with Tim Anderson (Guitar) and Fillip Nicolic (Bass).  They have a new album titled Another Man's Treasure that will be released on Oct 19th.  Ruthless is a pretty cool lead single that caught my ears and will have me checking for this album when it drops.  Check the badass visuals for Ruthless below that features skateboards x fire!!.

posts.title: Closer Ft Seth Sentry
posts.writeup: 360 is back with part 2 of his Please Be Seated free mixtape series where he raps over anything and everything.  PBS2 sees 360 go in over tracks from Big Boi, Kings Of Leon, Plan B, Justice, Soulwax, and Tame Impala.  Who the eff is 360 you ask?  Well if you are up on your Aussie hip-hop then you would know this dood from Pez' instant classic, The Festival Song from A Mind Of My Own.  Well he has been making some noise lately on these internets and hype for his new full length, Falling & Flying, will furthermore surmount as he readies it for a 2010 release.  You can bet Matik is going to have some bangers on there so its pretty much a no brainer that you will see Falling & Flying on here soon.  If you want to grab this mixtape become a fan of 360 on Facebook HERE.  The mixtape isn't out yet but if you become a fan you will be notified in your FB feed.

posts.title: Run
posts.writeup: Dark Time Sunshine will put you down a dark and dusty alley within an abstract reality that will have you grasping for the handrails.  But of course there are no hand rails when it comes to the type of hip-hop shown on Vessel.  Dark Time Sunshine is Onry Ozzborn of Grayskul/Old Dominion fame and Portland producer Zavala.  Run showcases the eerie production from Zavala that sounds like a beat you would play if you were putting on a exorcism.  When the beat comes on at 4:00 you will find yourself engulfed in the abyss of musical blackness.  Vessel is being released on the Fake Four label and also features Ceschi, Aesop Rock, Solillaquists Of Sound, Xperience,  Qwel + many more.  Get this album HERE.

posts.title: Runaway Ft Pusha T
posts.writeup: Runaway was the track Kanye preformed on the VMA's yesterday and was the only reason I cared about watching them.  I thought for sure this track was going to be the next leak for his G.O.O.D Friday series but that is not the case because its already on the internets.  Whether it was approved or not I have no clue.  People keep asking if these tracks are from the album and truth be told I don't know or care because every track he is releasing is reminding us why Kanye is one of the best.  His creativity is undeniable and as far as I am concerned the man can do or say whatever he wants because if the music is this good, I could care less.  The man is what he is and the only reason he should be in our lives is because of his music and not is shenanigans.  Ok enough preaching just listen to the song.

posts.title: One Step
posts.writeup: The music vibes come simple through soothing vocals over well knit instrumentation and how could you possibly go wrong when your album cover looks like a trees vagina? The music is organic and flows as your go through this album.  You may want to give this album a few listens over because its easy to drift out of thought and just let the music just play.  Kooii are a 7 piece band from Brisbane, Austalia that come with horns, a saxaphone and of course all the other instruments that make music possible.  Their myspace says this album is due in Sept but I can't seem to find a link to buy it.  Hopefully these guys have a google alert on their band name so they can hit me up with a link to buy this.  Until then here are some of my favorite tracks off this album.  You can get their first album from 2005 HERE.

posts.title: Good Friday Ft Common, Kid Cudi, Pusha T,Big Sean & Charlie Wilson
posts.writeup: Another G.O.O.D Friday has come and gone and I am a bit late on this one  due to being away from the comp when Kanye released this last night.   But no worries as I assume its not the end of the world.  Good Friday  is another great track led by piano keys, a dirty snare, Kid Cudi doing  his melodies, Kanye on his band pass raps, Common being Common, Pusha T  pulling off his sly raps, Charlie Wilson with his sweet la la la's and  Big Sean with his playful style.  I think I will be staying in and chilling on  twitter next Friday just to post the next track up.  Or not.

posts.title: Lookin For Ya Ft Andre 3000 & Sleepy Brown
posts.writeup: This track was originally slated to be released on Big Boi's latest album but was shelved due to Jive Records not allowing Andre 3000 to be on the album.  Label politics make no sense! These people need to go back to the drawing board and understand why good music is made in the first place.  Its so people can HEAR IT!.  Luckily for us this track was released to the internets yesterday.

posts.title: The Art Of Work Featuring Steph The Sapphic Songstress
posts.writeup: Substantial & Marcus D form up the emcee-producer duo, Bop Alloy to give us an absolute gem of an album that is based in hip-hop with tinges of classical jazz & soul.  Do NOT let this album slip through the cracks because it deserves your ears.  Marcus D is a perfect fit for Substantial who mesh together like a modern day Pete Rock and CL Smooth.  The instrumentation on the beats will leave your senses heightened for Substantial's clear and precise raps to take you to a perfect mindstate of musical bliss.  You can get this album via bandcamp HERE and if your lucky enough to get your hands on the Japan import from CD Baby (HERE) then you will be treated with the bonus cut that may just be my favorite off the album.  Click HERE to check out the bonus track "Commencement".

posts.title: The Number None
posts.writeup: Slug and Ant have released 2 separate EP's that they pressed onto one cd in promotion of the cross US tour with Grieves x Budo, Blueprint & DJ Rare Groove.  Click the pic or click HERE to check out the tour dates.  The guys at Rhymesayers will also be selling this cd at the merch booth, but I highly recommend you get this album right now on itunes HERE.  These 2 Ep's put together make it look like an official album with the amount of quality across this album.  There are some artists that never put out bad material and Atmosphere can be one of the elite few who can hold that moniker.

posts.title: Rhythm Of The Rain ft. Thee Tom Hardy
posts.writeup: Kno of Cunninlynguists has taken an hiatus on rapping on the last 2 Cunninlynguists albums (A Piece Of Strange & Dirty Acres) to "focus" on the production.  But on Oct 12th Kno will be releasing his first solo album, Death Is Silent that will feature him on the raps and the production.  Kno has always been a talent on the mic and has one of my favorite all time verses on 'Brain Cell' from 2005's A Piece Of Strange.  Featured on this beautiful track is the always dope Tunji and Thee Tom Hardy.  Pre-order's for Death Is Silent will go up on Qn5's site later today.

posts.title: The Thing That Helps Me Get Through
posts.writeup: Franti  has grown into one of the best musicians of our generation with his  mass appeal for people from all walks of life.  If you ever look around  at a Franti show you will see old, young people, hip-hop heads,  hipsters, rockers etc...  There is something about the simplicity in his  music that makes it so easy to enjoy.  Franti's style has come a long  way since the hip-hop influenced albums back in the late nineties to now  with his more alternative "poppy" sound.  One thing has always remained  with Franti and that is the music has always held its meaning through  content that hits you on a sub-conscious level. The Sound Of Sunshine  will be another stepping stone for Michael Franti to stretch his music  out as far as he can with those good vibrations we call music.  The Sound Of Sunshine will hit itunes on Sept 21st.  You can pre-order the album HERE.

posts.title: Letting Life Pass By
posts.writeup: If  you have been an avid follower of underground hip-hop since the early  2000's then you should be familiar with the Chicago based label  Galapagos4.  The G4 label represents the duo of Qwel & Maker and  also showcase other dope emcees like Robust, Denizen Kane, Typical Cats +  many more.  Last year Qwel and Maker dropped a solid album in So Be It and now only a year later they are back with more material.  Owl drops on Sept 14 so check for it on itunes soon.

posts.title: Diamond Snares
posts.writeup: Sapient   is the hip-hop version of slithering reptile with his slippery beats   and forked tongue raps that attack your reptilian brain through the deep dark pads and thick grimy synths.  The production style Sape possesses   is in a league of its own and will always be recognizable.  Sape is  also  one of the most prominent members of Sandpeople and more recently  with  The Prime with Luckyiam of the Living Legends.  Barrels For Feathers is his latest endeavour that features Eligh, Pigeon John and LMNO. Click HERE to cop this via itunes.

posts.title: Nothing Be
posts.writeup: Crown & Nefast Beatmakers - Just Listen - 2010(Hip-Hop/Jazz/Soul)You   can bet every corner of the world has talent when it comes to hip-hop production and these 2 cats from France will further more shine some   light on the hip-hop scene in France to a worldwide audience by linking   up with some quality emcees from around the globe. You will see the likes of Vancouver's Sonreal, San Diego's Main Flow, Portland's Braille,   UK's Mc Killa,  New York's Raekwon and Mr. Metaphor plus resident   french emcees spitting en francais.  As far as I am concerned the beats   all over this album are as good as it gets and perfectly exemplify the beauty of hip-hop production and its juxtaposition of genres. You can get this album via Amazon HERE.

posts.title: Country-Of-Loving
posts.writeup: Max  Tannone has been a busy man on the internets lately by feeding the  masses with his remix projects.  First It was the Jaydiohead mashup of  Jay-Z and Radiohead and just recently it was the well received Mos Dub  remix album.  Dub Kweli will for sure see the same adoration as his  previous projects with Talib Kweli over classic reggae tracks.  You can  get this for FREE HERE and download his other projects while your at it.  My favorite tracks are posted below with 'Your Gospel' taking the instrumentals from Scotty 'Draw Your Breaks' x Keith & Tex  'Stop That Train' over the lyrics from 'Hostile Gospel Pt. 1' on Eardrum.  'Country Of Living' takes the lyrics from 'Country Cousins' from Talib's latest release over the dub from Scientist 'Ghost of Frankenstein' x Michael Prophet 'Sweet Loving'.  And finally with 'Words High' which is a remix of the classic Get Em High from Ye's debut College Dropout over the dub instrumental of Jah Marcus 'Words'.  For a complete list of all the samples check the file included in the .zip folder when you download it.

posts.title: Hook Them
posts.writeup: In 2008 Skit Slam and Cymarshall Law lit us up with their debut album (Freedom) that sparked a niche style and sound with production from the Swedish duo: The Beatniks.  Freedom was an instant hit and was well received within music critics and tastemakers on the internets.  Fast forward to now and we have the same setup its just 2 years later and the stakes have been pushed up a few levels.  The production on Freedom II is an absolute standout with every track having a an encompassing feeling that allows the talent that Cy and Skit possess to make this album thick with quality.  Do your musical mind a favor and get this album NOW: via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Itssoeezzz (Shin-Ski Remix)
posts.writeup: Shin-Ski is a Japanese producer with heaps of talent in hip-hop production from the land of the musically gifted.  Shin-ski is best known for his work with American emcees Insight (Shinsight Trio), Othello, The Procussions, and Time Machine.  This was ripped from a Bootleg LP so if you ever want to grab this in vinyl you better get digging or get your hands on this digitally.  Shin-Ski absolutely kills it with the beat on his re-take on the classic Itssoeezzz from De La Soul, peep it below.

posts.title: Dear John
posts.writeup: Potholes In My Blog and Prefix Magazine have put together a free mixtape showcasing some new talent as well as some already established artists.  Featured on this project is Intuition, Hezekiah, Buff1, Moe Green, Slum Village, Cymarshall Law + many more.  Intuition easily captures my tastes with Dear John and the way he raps about his laziness in the third person over layered acoustic/electric guitars, harmonicas and a yukalaylee?.  Get this album HERE.

posts.title: A Lady Named Summer Ft Dillon
posts.writeup: Batsauce  is a producer from Jacksonville, FL who brings a organically funked out  sound with his compositions on Summertime that feature some note worthy  emcees.  My favorite track on the album is the short but sweet song  with Dillon that screams classic.  The vocal sample is really weird as  it drags on and on but when it drops out the song takes on its headnodic  feel and you will want to play this one over again.  Download this  album for free HERE.

posts.title: Stop, Look....Listen To Your Heart
posts.writeup: Circle Research are Nik and Gil who are 2 veteran producers/DJ's who have been kicking it in the Toronto music scene since the early 90's.  They recently released their newest album 'Gardiner Express' that you can grab via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Boy
posts.writeup: >Here at Hillydilly we try to bring you good music across all spectrums of musical genres and after having these 2 songs stuck on my play list for the last week, its time to pass them on to you guys.    Ra Ra Riot remind me a lot of Vampire Weekend with their bass lines and vocals but their guitar chord progressions are uniquely catchy.  The Orchard can be purchased via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Our Song Ft Seth Sentry
posts.writeup: Solo, Seth and Adit have let loose this brand new track via twitter and facebook in light up their upcoming Walk You Home Tour around Australia.  Check for the dates HERE.  Horrorshow have been making a name for themselves in the past couple of years with superior production from Adit and smooth raps from emcee Solo.  If you have never heard Horrorshow's last album, Inside Story, I suggest you take a listen now to one of the best hip-hop acts from Australia.  Seth Sentry is also a notable talent who works with Matik (who produces for Pez).  He dropped a very dope EP in 2008 called The Waiter Minute EP that featured the gems: Simple Game and The Waitress Song.  You can get Horrorshow's Inside Story HERE and Seth Sentry's The Waiter Minute HERE.

posts.title: Echoes
posts.writeup: Klaxons are a London based indie rock band and Surfing The Void is their latest album that was just recently released.  Echoes was my favorite track off this album and deserves a look from music heads who check out this site!  Get this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Fundamentals Ft Dl Incognito
posts.writeup: Vernon, BC emcee Tassnata will be releasing this free download project on Sept 1st that features stellar production from Tone Mason, Tzarizm, Northern Profit, Vokab Beats, Crown Beats, Nick Brongers and features emcees SonReal, Black Milk, Elzhi, Dl Incognito + many more.  The idea behind Between Planets is about how our generation is the transitional generation i.e: we are the last generation born into the pre-computer era, we are the era that will experience the most change, and how media/technology has played such a big roll in our personalities and decisions on a daily basis.  Between Planets is a great offering from Tassnata who will no doubt make a name for himself with this free project.  Standout favorites for me include Fundamentals, Pieces, On My Way, Dream and The EverWonder.  Click HERE to become a fan on Facebook and grab the bandcamp link on Sept 1st when it drops!

posts.title: Team Player #1
posts.writeup: The beatsmith for AML, 14KT recently released this unreleased track via twitter a few days ago.  Its the original version of Team Player #2 that was on AML's 2004 release Jungle Gym Jungle.  It was never released because of sample clearance issues but now is seeing the light of day.  For those who have never heard of AML they are a quintessential hip-hop group from Michigan consisting of emcees with large amounts of talent.  Members include the very notable Buff1, Sonny Star, Texture, Vital, Grand Cee, 14KT and DJ Haircut.  AML plan to release a brand new album this year so you should be excited because these dudes make good ass hip-hop.  I have included the AML classic 'Birth Control' from Jungle Gym Jungle to give you more of a taste of some classic flavor and get you in the know of this group.  Get Jungle Gym Jungle via itunes and their other albums HERE.

posts.title: Go To Sleep
posts.writeup: Here is the latest leak from Lupe's new album that has been put on hold by Atlantic Records against the wishes of Lupe.  The album was originally called Lasers but is now being titled: Food & Liquor 2: The Great American Rap Album.  This track is produced by 1500 or Nothin.

posts.title: Wheres There A Moon That Is Mine
posts.writeup: Japan has always had a lock on the hip-hop as far as production goes and Japanese producer Shin-ski (& DJ Ryow) have taken the skilled Insight along for another ride as they collab for the second time with their group Shinsight, bringing us their newest project since Shallow Nights Blurry Moon.  Get this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Mandatory
posts.writeup: The Five One have their musical style on random because every time these guys release anything its like I am listening to a completely different band.  The Five One grabbed my attention originally via their debut album The Green Complex from 2008 and since then the band has been putting in work releasing various remixes and now gearing up to release their new full-length album.  Mandatory is the first single they are giving away for free.  I really dig the intro and first verse of this song but find the autotune in the middle of the song to be a bit unnecessary. The autotune still doesn't stop me from liking Mandatory so go ahead and take a listen for yourself and let me know what you think.  Keep a lookout for The Five One's new full length album coming soon.  You will see it on here for sure.

posts.title: Resist
posts.writeup: Australia's Blue King Brown have taken the elevator to the top floor when it comes to their new double disc album Worldwize that features some international flavor with appearances from Sly and Robbie, Queen Ifrica, Jah Mason, Elliot Martin (JBB) plus many more.  The album is split into 2 discs with North being the bulk of the album with all the original material and the South disc being a dub disc that further elevates this album's experience.  BKB have also been co-signed by the likes of Carlos Santana through some strong words: "Blue King Brown are the voice of the street & the band of the future".  My favorite track on the album is 'Resist' with its powerful lyrics and vocals led by the talented lead female vocalist, Natalie.  Blue King Brown bring honest revolutionary content with great instrumentation to take this band to the ears of the people of the world who demand a change in their music and reality.  Worldwize will hit digital outlets August 20th!

posts.title: Soldiers Of Sound
posts.writeup: Some bands can capture that reggae roots sound we all love and some don't but with this eight piece band from Boston they capture that sound with pleasure and ease.  Soldiers of Sound is their first full length album that has turned some heads (including mine) in the music industry and should make their way to your ears.  Make sure to also check out my other favorites Voodoo Spell, I Define Me and The Golden Age.  Get this album via Bandcamp HERE.

posts.title: To All My Friends
posts.writeup: Atmosphere have released this promo single to kick off some attention for the new US tour Atmosphere is about to embark on with Grieves + Budo, Blueprint and DJ Rare Groove.  Check HERE for the show listings.  Sorry I can't give this out for free, you gotta buy it via itunes HERE.

posts.title: The Way That I Rhyme
posts.writeup: J-Live has been one of the best emcees + writers of the last 10 years with his unformidable substance, wordplay and content within his lyrics.  J-Live is the rapper of all rappers in my opinion.  If I was to show anyone what a real emcee sounds like it would be J-live.  The Way That I Rhyme was released along with the b-side of Poetry In Motion in the hype for the his new album.  You can get this single digitally HERE for 2 bucks or the Vinyl Single HERE.

posts.title: Mr. Rager
posts.writeup: Mr. Rager is the latest leak from the new album from Cudder, Man On The Moon II: The Legend Of Mr. Rager.  The original release date was for Sept 14th but has now been pushed back to Oct 26th.  Mr. Rager is a fine example of how creative Cudi is, so go ahead and take a listen to this slow moving epic ballad.

posts.title: Dub Dub Inc
posts.writeup: The way Umberto Echo's beats sound you would think this guy invented the dub sound because his music is like nothing I have ever heard.  He is so clever with his reverb and delays through putting massive amounts on verb on snare hits, side sticks and what sounds like a pipe hitting a cement wall on Dem Gone Dub.  It is pretty damn cool.  Dub The World features Umberto giving his dub treatment to songs from the likes of Sly And Robbie, Steel Pulse, Damian Marley, Katchafire, Buju Banton, Seed, Cee-Lo, The Red Eyes plus many more.  Umberto Echo is from Munich, Germany.  You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: The Lonely Ones
posts.writeup: K-Os has never been the one to release mixtapes so hearing about this new Anchorman Mixtape that uses sound bites from the classic movie is a great surprise.  K-Os has consistently been an artist that always makes quality music when he drops anything and this mixtape will continue that expectation along.  Fellow Toronto rapper Drake is on one track but gets out shined by K-Os by far!  Get this for free HERE.

posts.title: Sooncome
posts.writeup: This '12 headed' band from Belgium really do bring pura vida (pure life) to your ears through one of the best sounding reggae roots acts I have ever heard.  Pura Vida have been a project many years in the making with the band first jamming together in 2006.  Over the course of the 4 years leading up to now they have been perfecting their craft and forging their own sound that refuses to be anything but spectacular.  Their debut, Struggle In The City is above and beyond average with a sound that cannot be matched.  You will need to hear the rest of this album, trust me.  Get it HERE

posts.title: The-Dream-Machine.
posts.writeup: Astrological composes heavily layered beats that scintillate you through the melodic melodies of the various instrumentation.  Each song takes on a life of its own with its constant flow of progression that gives each song a sense of movement along a musical plane.  I find myself drifting away in thought when I let this album play, there really are some beautiful compositions on this free album.  Astrological currently resides in Vancouver and is also on the vancity based label Jellyfish Recordings.

posts.title: Twisted Love
posts.writeup: Seedless are another reggae.rock band emerging from Orange County, CA.  They have garnered themselves some internet hype via The Pier.org and through this free EP they released on band camp HERE..  The Twisted Roots track best exemplifies this bands sound and should provide enough grounds to add these guys to your watch list as they prepare to drop their debut full length that is due to hit stores on Aug 28th.

posts.title: Dub To Go (Ancient Astronauts Remix)
posts.writeup: Kabanjak will hijack your attention with some out of this world production that sounds like it was composed on Pluto.  He composes beats that are fit for a dream world full of wonderful sights, sounds and emotions.  While the original (the title track) is pretty damn good, the remix that label mates Ancient Astronauts did takes the song and pushes it over the edge into the must listen category.  Wishcraft is also a must hear with its highly infectious dub's, synths, drum rhythm's and all around 'badassness'. This EP is only 3 tracks deep and can be purchased via the soundcloud player embedded below.

posts.title: Jamaica ft. Zig Zag
posts.writeup: If you ever want to relieve some stress or tension just take a seat and pop some Molecule on and let his hypnotic music massage your mind.  Molecule is a DJ/beatmaker from France and this is his second album that showcases his production with the vocal assistance of Flox, Zig Zag, Martina Topley, + more.

posts.title: Work ft. Slug
posts.writeup: Surprises are always nice and when they come in the form of music you snap out of your thoughts to focus on what this MPLS rapper brings to our ears.  Don't be fooled by the Russian hockey player name the dude can rap and sing.  Krane is known as Eyedea's BFF but with an album like this no one will care about that.  The production on Picking Flowers Next To Road Kill is held up by the Kristoff and the production duo of Strange Matter.   Along with this album he just released another album in Hunting For Father you can get this album HERE and Hunting For Father via bandcamp HERE.  That synth melody on Work is soooooo gravy and when you add the legendary slug on some verses you get a neck snapping gem.

posts.title: The War
posts.writeup: More About Nothing is the second installment of the Seinfeld inspired free mixtape series that Wale has started. It seems to be the trend with some rappers these days that put out better free mixtapes than their major label albums because More About Nothing is pretty damn good. Its probably not very good for the record exec's but for the music fan we could care less. Wale is as hungry as ever, his confidence and drive are dripping all over his raps on this mixtape.

posts.title: Dont Give It Away
posts.writeup: If you're like me and just finished watching the latest episode of Entourage, you were probably wondering what that song was at the end credit.  The song is by a five piece band from Memphis who have caught my attention with Don't Give It Away.  The track starts off with a crackling beat with dark pads, erie synths and a classic southern drawl rap.  The song encompasses a gangster rap feel with soulful signing and ends off with some epic drums.   The music supervisor responsible for Entourage (Scott Vener) let loose this track via his blog.  Free Sol are new to me, I suggest we both hit up their latest free mixtape HERE.

posts.title: Foolish ft. Reks
posts.writeup: Moe Pope has been a journey man in hip-hop starting out in the Bay Area with Mission (now known as Crown City Rockers) in 2001 with their debut LP One.  He would later move to Boston and link up with Insight and form up the 5 man crew Electric Co. who released a a great album in 2003 with Life's a Struggle.  Breaking off from the Electric Co,  Moe Pope joined up with Raheem Jamal and Annonymous to form Project Move.  They released a solid album in Love Gone Wrong/Butterfly Theory in 2006 that featured the classic 'Yao Ming' track.  Moe Pope's last effort saw him re-link up with beatsmith Headnodic of the Crown City Rockers as they released Megaphone in 2008 to some critical acclaim.  In 2010 Pope has linked up with newcomer Rain to drop an absolute gem of an hip-hop album that has equal amounts of quality on the raps and the production.  The relationship between the beats and rhymes on Life After God is like a match made in heaven for hip-hop junkies.  Rain has made his mark with the production on this album and will definitely be finding himself some more work in the future.  Get this album HERE.

posts.title: Addicted
posts.writeup: I will never forget the first time I heard Flying Colors and the many months after of enjoying that amazing album.  Its now 2 years later and after 6 months of isolated recording in the Victorian Bush we get the follow up album that has been hyped and marketed very nicely over the past few months.  Running On Air is a three headed beast of an hip-hop album with huge production, intricate lyrics and voices that demand your attention.  Running On Air is lead by soul cracking production from the likes of M-Phazes, Matik (the beatsmith responsible for Pez' A Mind Of My Own) and Hattori Hunzo.  The variety of beats range from gritty boom bap to acoustic, electric and sitar driven melodies.  Lyrically MC Bliss and Eso carry the vibration of our collective consciousness through their down to earth, thought provoking lyrics that spark positive emotions.  The two emcees attack each track with wizardry taking the listener's attention and bolting their ears to the ground.  The album has 19 tracks with 14 of them having replay value.  You can bet this album will be stuck on your playlist for a few months.  BnE have produced an album that will push Aussie hip-hop to new levels that will stretch their fan base to all corners of this planet.  You can get this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Scheming ft J Dilla, Posdnuos and Phife
posts.writeup: Slum Village carry a heavy respect behind their name through their classic work throughout the years.  And no matter the obstacle the group faces, the music always prevails.  Slum Village originally started out with rappers Baatin (deceased in July 2009), T3 and J Dilla (deceased Feb 2006).  It wasn't until 2002 with Trinity (Past, Present, and Future) that we saw Elzhi jump into the crew via T3.  Villa Manifesto is the groups sixth and final album that is being released on E1 Records.  The album serves as a reunion/memorial album that features posthumous work from J Dilla and Baatin with new verses from Elzhi, T3 and J Dilla's brother Illa J. Guest appearances include Little Brother, Dwele, Phife, De La Soul + more.  Get this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: And The Camera ft. Dessa
posts.writeup: Paper Tiger is a producer from the awesome Doomtree collective who brings an eclectic style to our ears throughout Made Like Us.  MLU is 11 tracks deep that is mostly instrumentals with  fellow Doomtree member Dessa and Maggie Morrison (of Lookbook) on the only 3 tracks with featured vocals (Dessa is on 2 tracks).  Paper Tiger's beats feature many different types of instrumentation from strings, keys to tight drum samples and a vast array of vocal samples that allow the instrumentals to hold their own.  Get this album right now from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Bastian Bones
posts.writeup: The Aussie hip-hop scene keeps pushing out good music with a generation of artists who grew up on good hip-hop and artists like Skryptcha are continuing that form through their music.  The Sydney emcee turned heads in '09 when he dropped his debut EP, Left To Write on Obese Records.  The Numbers is the emcees first full length that features production from M-Phazes, Chasm, Domingo, Jase and Ante Escobar.  Get this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: Maximizng Love
posts.writeup: Finley Quaye has always been a musician in the reggae scene that has seemed to always fly under the radar in North America.  He created a huge buzz with his debut in '97 with Maverick A Strike then again with Vanaguard in 2001 and Much More Than Love in 2002.  He then dropped this EP in '05 and another EP, Pound For Pound in 2009.  As you will tell from these songs the man is a great singer and we can only hope for a new album soon with some proper promo behind it.

posts.title: Time Wont Tell
posts.writeup: EL-P has always been a well respected man within the music industry through his work running the Def Jux label and releasing quality albums.  Growing up being a music scoundrel I would always listen to EL-P's albums but never really came away with any favorites.  After going through his latest mix I came across Time Won't Tell and I now I think my past opinion my be a bit skewed.  My opinion aside El-P fans will have already picked this up but if you're like me giving this song a spin might turn you onto some EL-P.  Get this album HERE.

posts.title: Dont Let Love Pass You By
posts.writeup: Last Resort is a compilation put together by many great producers and emcees that is described as a last resort for true music heads for an escape from the mundane music world dominated by crap on tv and the radio.  I'd say claiming that this compilation is a 'Last Resort' may be a bit overkill but the message/inspiration is in the right place.  On Last Resort is production from Freddie Joachim, Seoul Avenger, Othello, Deedot and Dibiase.  With vocals from Freddie Joachim, Raashan Ahmad, Vigilant, Othello, Jacewon, Navery, Dibiase, Addullessence, Braille, Ohmega Watts +more.  The track below is a melodic soulful instrumental that caught my ear right away and will most likely catch yours.  Get this album via bandcamp HERE.

posts.title: I Am Legend
posts.writeup: Rass Kass is a class example of how skill will overcome any obstacle and always result in longevity in the land of the 'disposable art'.  Ras Kass has seen his ups and downs with his personal life but with the music it has always been up.  A.D.I.D.A.S. has some really standout tracks that remind us why we loved Ras Kass back in the late 90's/early 2000's.  I Am Legend leads off this mixtape with its best track that boasts Ras Kass hungry as ever over the perfect beat to let him drop some amazing raps. A.D.I.D.A.S. features production from The Alchemist, Mr. Porter, DJ Cannon, DJ Babu, DJ Rhettmatic + more. Get this album via bandcamp HERE.

posts.title: My Shoes
posts.writeup: Hoffmaestro & Chraa are an eclectic group from Sweden who can't be pinned down with a one dimensional sound from their head turning music shown on their debut from 2008. The potential can be seen with this band very easily and when they do hit the mark the result is very good. Take a peek at the tracks I am posting up here to get your best example of Hoffmaestro & Chraa. Lets hope they drop a new album soon because I assume it will top this album.

posts.title: Golden Years
posts.writeup: Today Bliss N Eso released a new track from their forthcoming album Running On Air along with part 2 of the documentary series about the whole recording process in the Victorian bush that you can see below. From all the audio teasers on the documentary its easy to hear that this album is shaping up to be HUGE. I really love the direction BnE are heading towards and there is no doubt in my mind that Running On Air will be one of the years best. For now enjoy Golden Years and count down the days until July 30th.

posts.title: Herbal Remedy
posts.writeup: Clear Conscience dropped one of the best reggae/rock albums last year with On The Rocks and a quick year later we get a new CC album. This album's focus has shifted away from the feel of On The Rocks with most of songs on Herbal Remedy showcasing a slew of international reggae singers and emcees. On Herbal Remedy you will see Hani from Katchafire, Toko Tasi, Josh Heinrichs, Dub Passenjah of Chapter 11, No Needz, Mike of Orange Grove, P-dub, Wildlife + many more. All proceeds from this album are going towards Feedthechildren.org so hit up iTunes for the album by going HERE.

posts.title: Fast Forward
posts.writeup: The German reggae artist Martin Jondo is back with his new album that was just released on July 16th. Sky Rider contains positive reggae music with enough good vibes for all you reggae rockers out there. Get this album from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Mezcal
posts.writeup: Rakaa of Dilated Peoples is releasing his new solo album on July 20th that features some great production from the likes of Illmind, Evidence, Exile, Babu, DJ Honda, DJ Rhettmatic, The Alchemist and El-P. Illmind gets my vote for the best beat on this album with the banger 'Mezcal'. Give the song a few seconds and you will see what I am talking about. You can also find Aloe Blacc, KRS-One, Krondon, Chali 2na, Evidence + many more on Crown of Thorns.

posts.title: Paving The Way Ft Okay City, Shad & David Morin
posts.writeup: 10 years ago the Canadian hip-hop scene was pretty bleak as far as depth goes. As the years have gone on more and more beat makers/emcees have been popping out of everywhere. Take Incise for example who is from a suburb in Hamilton, Ontario. Incise hit the radar in 2008 with his debut album Nobody's Story and now we have his follow up. His production has taken on a signature sound with some really great sounding synths that carry on beautifully with the melodies. The tracks I have posted below are a great examples of Incise stepping up his game. Daily Methods features Shad, Dan Johns, Tunji, The 49ers, + many more. Get this album now HERE.

posts.title: Drama Free
posts.writeup: Sweatshop Union have built up a solid reputation for putting out quality music and has allowed side projects like Dirty Circus to exist and bless us with some true-school hip-hop. Dirty Circus differ from the Sweatshop sound with a more raw production style that exhibits a more classic hip-hop sound that works well with the two talented emcees. Dirty Circus is a side project from the members of Sweatshop Union with Metty The Dert Merchant and Mos Eisley on the raps. Alive and Well features production from Rob The Viking, Slahka, The Beatchild + Dj Rip One. The vocal features are held down by Shad, Geologic of Blue Scholars, Moka Only, The Pharcyde, Blame One, Dynas, + more. This album is out now go ahead and get it HERE.

posts.title: Aint Nothin Gonna Change Ft Barfly & Heresey Mae
posts.writeup: Factor is who from Saskatoon, Saskatchewan is one of Canada's unsung hip-hop producers who has been putting in work for the last ten years working with the likes of Swollen Members, Awol One, Busdriver, Aesop Rock and Xzibit. Lawson Graham sees Factor heading up the production with some vocal assistance from Ceschi, Josh Martinez, Sole, Def3, Moka Only, Pigeon John, Myka 9, Cars + Trains, + many more. Lawson Graham will put Factor up a few notches in the eyes of music fans across the world with a sound that deserves to be heard. Get this album via iTunes HERE.

posts.title: It Takes A Muscle
posts.writeup: MAYA is the new album from M.I.A who has become a household name for her massive hit Paper Planes from her last album Kala. M.I.A is known for her progressive style of music and MAYA will continue that trend with some out of this world material. Most of this album goes over my head but there are some gems including Space, Tell Me Why, Internet Connection and the track below It Takes A Muscle. MAYA will hit itunes and stores on July 13th. On MAYA, M.I.A worked with her longtime collaborators Switch & Diplo with additional production from Blaqstarr, Rusko and Derek E. Miller of Sleigh Bells.

posts.title: Rise and Shine Ft Inverse
posts.writeup: Cook Classics is an up and coming producer from LA who has the advantage of working with some great talent (along with his talent, of course) on Recharged from the likes of Tunji, Buff1, Emilio Rojas, Sean Price, Shawn Jackson, Aloe Blacc + more. The track I have here one my favorites off the album that sees a piano driven beat led by the always impressive Tunji with harmonies from Deacon The Villan. You can get this album now HERE. I recently blogged about this Cook Classics dude in March with that epic track Towards The Future w/ Tunji & Deacon. The problem was I had no idea who this Cook artist was because there was no info on this album. All I had was the cover from this so called album titled "Cook - Towards The Future". Turns out it was probably bootlegged/released before the intention of both artists because Towards The Future was only released a few weeks ago by the artists themselves. Weird.

posts.title: Julius Final Song
posts.writeup: This album has been stuck on my playlist due to a unique style and sound that is produced from rapper/singer Ceschi (pronounced Chess-key) who hails from New Haven, Connecticut. Its hard to dismiss his music as too abstract or progressive because there are way to many styles mixed in for it not to strike you as interesting. My first listen I ignorantly pegged this as nerd rap but the more I get into it the more I respect the juxtaposition of styles incorporated into his compositions. His singing reminds me of the Beatles and his fast rapping is incomprehensible but impressive nonetheless. I don't think Ceschi expects people to comprehend what he is saying in his raps. Its more of an art that is better left untouched so don't bother hating on him for that. This album is entirely produced by DJ Scientist. You can get this album via iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Fallen Angels (Prod. P-Money)
posts.writeup: Fallen Angel was the very first official single that the Deceptikonz released back in 2001. I can see why this song shot these guys into fame back in the day because this track is pretty good. This track is from their debut from 2002, Elimination. Any New Zealand hip-hop head would of heard this years ago and will probably want to slap anyone who has never heard this track. So yea take a listen.

posts.title: The Sound Of Sunshine
posts.writeup: Franti will return with a new album on August 24th titled The Sound Of Sunshine. Anyone that is a fan of Franti knows that every album he puts out usually meets expectations. Check iTunes for the other single that is already out that features Lady Saw. Click HERE to check it out.

posts.title: Legacy
posts.writeup: The Deceptikonz have been holding down the New Zealand hip-hop scene since their inception back in 1996 by emulating that true-school hip-hop and staying true to making quality hip-hop. I first came across the Deceptikonz a few years ago via one fourth of the group in the incredible Mareko. If you have never heard his classic album White Sunday then I suggest you get in the know! The track 'Legacy' is an absolute classic from that album. Peep it below. The other 3 emcees not to be left out are Devolo, Savage and Alphrisk who all deserve respect for holding it down for New Zealand. They have a brand new album dropping July 5th with the same title as this lead single. Evolution is a guitar/string driven single that allow the 4 Maori emcees to rough it up Terror Squad circa Big Pun era style. So hit up iTunes for that and look for a full review when I get my hands on it. Mareko is the emcee on the second verse on Evolution with the great line at the end of the verse with "the only thing moving backwards is my hair line" Check the visuals for the vid!

posts.title: Hold Yuh (Major Lazer Remix)
posts.writeup: Diplo and Switch aka Major Lazer are back at it again with another remix on Hold Yuh that was also remixed on the Lazerproof mixtape with La Roux that you can get for free HERE. This remix is highly likely to get you out of your chair and dancing. I would love to jam out to this track inside a club, that is of course I still actually went out clubbing! But regardless this remix is an upbeat happy track that will suit any party atmosphere.

posts.title: We Could Forever
posts.writeup: Bonobo is another prime reason why I will never try to compose my own beats.  Why even bother when you have musicians like Bonobo making wonderful compositions like we see on Black Sands?  Black Sands has hit critical acclaim on many respected sites and been a success in 2010.  You can get the rest of the album from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Root To The Fruit Ft Wildchild
posts.writeup: ASM are three emcees (Green-T, FP &  Fade) that are backed by a 6 piece band who incorporate the reggae and funk with the hippity hop.  Root To The Fruit was the most notable track off this album that features Wildchild complimenting this track nicely with a solid verse.  You can listen to the rest of their album via their Bandcamp page.

posts.title: Nothing Left
posts.writeup: The good news is Luckyiam from the Living Legends will be dropping some new material this fall with 2 new projects.  The first being the next installment of Luckyiam x Sapient as The Prime and Lucky's new solo album (I Heart Haters) that will feature production from Kruse.   Nothing Left is the first leak from the album.

posts.title: So Basic
posts.writeup: Marco Polo is a beat maker from Toronto who has stormed onto the scene with some very solid work over the past few years.  Marco Polo caters towards that classic boom bap sound that fits nicely with emcees who can rise up to the level that Polo is giving them.  Surreal is one of those rappers who can take a beat from Polo and bring it to a level that makes it seem all 'so basic' with an undeniably raw style that oozes with confidence.  Also on this project is Elzhi, Royce Da 5'9, Large Professor, Supastition, Oddisee, + many more.  You can get this HERE.

posts.title: Wake Up
posts.writeup: Controlled Substance/Silverback and The Pier have just let loose the latest sampler from the best label (Silverback) pushing out reggae/rock right now.
