The sampler includes new/old/unreleased material from Slightly Stoopid, The Expendables, Outlaw Nation, Passafire, Pepper, Fishbone, Rebelution, Iration, The Beautiful Girls and The Aggrolites.  Head over to The Pier now to get yourself a copy.  You have to sign up through their online store and eventually you will get the link.  Featured here is the best track off the sampler with a taste of some new Pepper that may or may not be from their forthcoming new album.  I'd say this song deserves to make the new album.  The track sounds unfinished to my ears from the vocals sounding a bit dry, but it still is a pretty damn good track.

posts.title: Right On ft. Joanna Newsom
posts.writeup: The Roots have always been that hip-hop group you can always be proud to be a fan of. Every time The Roots drop a new album they receive well deserved hype and How I Got Over sees the same treatment from a worldwide hip-hop fan base that always loves a new Roots album. On June 22nd How I Got Over will hit stores and digital outlets. I didn't get into the Roots until 1999 when they dropped Things Fall Apart and since that time I have really enjoyed each album with The Tipping Point and Phrenology being my favorites. The Roots last effort (Rising Down) did not ever receive much play in my ears and sadly How I Got Over is going to fall into that same category. I hate not having many positive things to say about one of the most respected groups in hip-hop but I have to keep it honest from my personal point of view. The Fire is clearly the best track off this album and I can see Right On, Now Or Never and This Day getting some quality spins. Maybe I am being too harsh but with The Roots I expect a bit more from a talented group like that. The Roots are high profile enough that this review won't stop you from buying this album or downloading it, so as always make sure you listen for your self! For fans of Dujeous, Common, Jurassic 5.

posts.title: California Girl ft. Shwayze
posts.writeup: Cisco Adler is a singer/songwriter/producer who is one half of the alternative hip-hop group Shwayze.  Cisco is bringing us a free mixtape that sees the same formula that we see on Shwayze records with that signature 'California' sound.  The free mixtape features Shwayze, Del The Funky Homosapien, The Knux, TQ, Mickey Avalon + many more. (seen at thissongisick.com)  Get it HERE.

posts.title: REVOFEV
posts.writeup: REVOFEV is Kid Cudi's way of saying Revolution Of Evolution for short. Not sure if that works when you try to say it but its Kid Cudi and I am pretty sure he doesn't care. REV OF EV is the lead single from Cudi's new album Man On The Moon II The Legend Of Mr. Rager. After the highly addictive debut from '09 the expectations have been set high and it will be great to see what Cudi will have in store. Look for the album Late Summer/Fall.

posts.title: The Perfect Occasion
posts.writeup: On June 22 RJD2 will be releasing the instrumentals from his last album Colossus plus 7 new instrumentals.  RJD2 is known for always releasing the instrumentals from his albums but Colossus was half instrumentals and half featured vocal preformances.  So instead of re-releasing the instrumentals with no vocals he just added 7 new songs and re-named the album.  The Perfect Occasion is one of the new instrumentals, its pretty banging.

posts.title: Good Enough Ft Collie Buddz and Lindi Ortega
posts.writeup: Major Lazer is a fictional musical character from the minds of DJ/Producer(s) Diplo and Switch who are known best with their work with M.I.A.  Diplo was responsible for producing M.I.A's smash hit Paper Planes.  The fusion of electronic and reggae works very well on this track especially with that synth (the one playing the melody) draped in reverb to give this production style a change up from the norm.  Collie Buddz is autotune-less and sounds great on the chorus. Look for the new album this year titled Lazers Never D.I.E.  Major Lazer's debut album can be purchased HERE.

posts.title: Back Home
posts.writeup: Watermelon Chicken & Grits was a monumental album back in the early 2000's and shot Nappy Roots to commercial success at a fast rate. It has been a slow decent for the band since WCG and it seems like every time they release a new album we all wonder if Nappy can bring it back. Nappy Roots have never touched WCG with their new efforts but have always had some gems on their albums.  In The Pursuit Of Nappyness unfortunately takes on the same trend as previous albums with not much of a feeling of a cohesive high quality album.  But when the songs are good you tend to forget about any negatives about the rest of the album because everyone falls in love with Nappy Roots and their country-folk style of hip-hop.  Back Home and All For U best represent this album, so take a listen and remember why you like Nappy Roots.  This album is out now get it HERE.

posts.title: Skewed Empathy
posts.artist: Crystal Castles x Outkast x Raekwon
posts.writeup: Skratch Bastid has brilliantly combined one of the best tracks off of Crystal Castles new album with the classic Skew It On The Bar B from Aquemini.  The more I listen to that new Crystal Castles the more I enjoy it and that is a cool thing for me because I never would of imagined I could like their style.  Oh the joys of loving all types of music!

posts.title: Chick Pop
posts.writeup: Amp Live has shown through the years that he has been experimenting with the whole electronic/hip-hop vibe with his group Zion I. Murder At The Discotech is Amp-live's musical expression of his new found hobby of making some incredible electronic beats with mind blasting synths. Featured vocalists include Zumbi, K. Flay, The Grouch, Eligh + more. This album is out now HERE.

posts.title: Elevation ft. Opio
posts.writeup: Chosen Few are 2 emcees: Cold Showda, Charles "Chop" Cooper and Unjust on the beats. They carry the respect of being one of the few groups signed to the Hiero Imperium label ran by Domino and the Hiero crew.  New World Symphony is their debut that also features Del, Pep Love, Phat Kat, Tajai + more. You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: Need You
posts.writeup: Travis McCoy is the lead vocalist from the Gym Class Heroes and this is his debut album.  Lazarus covers a wide range of musical styles and features a few tracks with some great production.  The album falls short on too much autotune and too many poppy songs for my tastes.  The album should fair well with his target market so I am sure this album will do well for Travis McCoy, but for us 'seasoned music appreciators' we can take it or leave it.  Need You was my favorite off the album  You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: The Fire Ft John Legend
posts.writeup: Philly residents can always turn to The Roots and know they are always putting out championship material and allow their Flyer fans to keep their mind off the loss in the Stanley Cup Finals with a head nodding single from the new album.  The 3rd leak from the new album has turned up the anticipation a few notches around here.  How I Got Over hits stores June 22nd.

posts.title: To Love To Miss Ft Wordsworth & Illustrate
posts.writeup: It has been 7 years since I have heard of Tzarizm with his album 'Dirty Work' that had a old time favorite track 'Yes To Day' that I had totally forgot about until now.  The Heart & Brain Mbodiment is a new project with emcee Vis Major and Tzarizm on the production and raps.  This album leaks out at the seems with true-school hip-hop vibes, just check out the track I am posting here and you will know what I am talking about.  Featured on this album is Supasition, Wordsworth, Akrobatik, Butta Verses + many more.  Get this album now HERE.

posts.title: Space Bound
posts.writeup: Recovery is a very good start towards the right direction for Em.  The attitude and lyrical explosions are back with a bit more anger on Recovery.  Em is at his best when he puts everything on the table in his raps and you can expect to hear Em lay it all out from the past few years of his life on this album.  The third verse on 'Talkin To Myself' Eminem apologizes to all the fans like us who have gave up on him and promises to return to form:"......this time around its different, the last 2 albums didn't count, Encore I was on drugs, Relapse, I was flushing them out. I've come to make it up to you, now no more fu*king around. I've got something to prove to fans cause I feel like I let em down. So please accept my apology"All will be forgotten if Eminem can stay off the drugs and keep on making good music.  Recovery is still his best album since The Eminem Show but I am still searching for more.  Personally I think the production found on Recovery does not match Eminem, there is something not right about those cheesy synth drums that does not fit well with em's lyrical style.  I am only talking about the tracks that I felt missed the mark.  Early favorites include, Talkin' To Myself, Cold Wind Blows, Space Bound and Love The Way You Lie.  All constructive negatives aside I am pleased with Eminem's latest and has renewed my faith in one of my favorite artists from the last 15 years.

posts.title: I Got You
posts.writeup: Dyme Def are a throwback hip-hop style that I would of loved when I was younger for the days when I was more into rap than hip-hop.  The Seattle trio have a commercial appeal in their music and I can easily see them getting some attention.  Their production is full of heavy hitting drums and tons of cool synths to compliment their style.  You can listen to the whole album from their bandcamp page HERE.

posts.title: Night Train ft. Goodie Mob
posts.writeup: Cee-lo is back on his solo work after a recent successful stint with Dangermouse as the Gnarls Barkley duo.  Stray Bullets is the hype mixtape for his new album Lady Killer that will be released later this year.  You can get this mixtape HERE.

posts.title: Fine
posts.writeup: Yu is a member of the Diamond District crew who are fresh off of garnering some attention for their last album, In The Ruff. Yu just recently released his solo album that showcases his version of boom-bap hip-hop with that Virginia/Maryland sound that Oddisee has put on the map. Oddisee was responsible for producing the lead single, Natives. The majority of the album is produced and mixed by Yu and Slimkat of the 1978ers. In the tracks below I have included - Fine : a very fresh sample based beat with the catchy sing along phrase of "we'll be fine, we'll be fine, we'll be fine, we'll be find....etc." The other track showcases the best example of what you can expect for the rest of the album. Get this album HERE.

posts.title: Set You Free Ft Masta Ace, Wordsworth, Nathan Flutebox Lee
posts.writeup: Skitz has been a main stay on the UK hip-hop scene since the mid 80's through his production work, DJ gigs, and his weekly radio show on the BBC. Sticksman is Skitz newest album after a 10 year hiatus after his original release Countryman that was released back in 2001. Sticksman features an international guest list from the likes of Masta Ace, Wordsworth, Kardinal Offishal, Rodney P, Brotherman, plus many more. The album tackles the classic hip-hop sound with tinges of dancehall reggae. You can get this album via iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Crazy Things
posts.writeup: Collective Efforts are a hip-hop trio from Atlanta who have been making music since their debut from 2003, Visions Of Things To Come, also in 2005 with Trail Mix, and their last album from 2006, Medicine. Freezing World is their newest album and judging from this album I am shaking my head as to why I have never heard of these guys. Collective Efforts are 3 emcees MC J Mil, Ben Hameen, and Bambu de Asiatic. Their music is a positive listen with their unique style of soulful raps over quality production complete with some sweet percussion giving the album its own pedestal to stand out above the rest. You can get this album and previous albums from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Immersion
posts.writeup: My roomate kicked me onto these guys last week and I have had that 'Set Me On Fire' track stuck on my  playlist ever since I went through Pendulum's newest album, Immersion.   It's quite the epic composition with some X files type synths combined  with some dirty pads over some spacey drums.  I have not really gotten  into anything Pendulum has done but this track deserves a look from the  readers of this blog.  Check for Immersion on iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Hype2night Ft Ang 13
posts.writeup: Picture Chali 2na rapping through a block of ice before the mic because the man is cool as hell with his flow, lyrics, voice and style. Fish Market 2 is Chali's follow up mixtape to the original Fish Market mixtape from 2004. Featured on the part 2 is Akil (Jurassic 5), J-Live, Elzhi, Planet Asia + more. Fish Market 2 will be released June 8th on iTunes any many other digital retailers.

posts.title: Feelings Gone
posts.writeup: >The Cat Empire - Feeling's Gone - (Single) - 2010(Reggae/Ska/Jazz/Funk/Rock)The Cat Empire are one of the best musical bands in the world in my opinion.  Whether it be in the live setting or full albums, these guys bring everything but the kitchen sink to the table.  Their new album, Cinema, is going to be released on July 6th.  Feeling's Gone is the first single from the album and has got me super excited because Feeling's Gone is amazing!  The new album is 11 tracks deep and will no doubt be a musical buffet for us rabid consuming music fans to digest slowly.

posts.title: Its The Chemicals Ft Scarub
posts.writeup: Inspired Flight are that promising duo who released the Push, Pull, Let Go w/ Eligh a few months ago and here we are with the new album that has deserved itself a good look. The duo stir up multiple genre's with great verses from guest vocals from Scarub + Eligh of The Living Legends, Inspectah Deck and Rugged Monk. The production provided by Open Optics and Chavez are melodic compositions with copious amounts of harmonies and beautiful timbre's from the various instrumentation throughout each track. Beside my personal favorite track (posted below) check out player below take a listen to 'Jackie's Song' and you can get a quick representation of what you can expect from this album. This album drops tomorrow (June 1st) and will be available for download HERE.

posts.title: I Rock
posts.writeup: Open Mike Eagle is an emcee from the Project Blowed (Abstract Rude, Myka 9, Aceyalone) camp from LA and Unapologetic Art Rap is his debut album.  His brand of hip-hip is self described as 'art rap' and is really quite entertaining.  The music is progressive and pushes the boundaries on the creative front on hip-hop.  My favorite track off the album features an amazingly flipped sample that creates a instrumental that fits perfectly with Open Mike Eagle's style.  Get this album on iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Across The Map Ft J-Live
posts.writeup: On June 8th Chali 2na will be releasing a his new mixtape , Fish Market 2, that follows up the Fish Market mixtape from 2004.  Across The Map is one of the leaked tracks from the mixtape with the always impressive J-Live on the hook and one verse at the end.

posts.title: I F*cking Hate Rappers
posts.writeup: I absolutely love the title and theme to Pack FM's new album because its about time someone dedicated a whole album to the subject of today's out of control rapper.  But don't get it twisted that this is all hate because its not.  I F*cking Hate Rappers is out today on iTunes and you can get it HERE.  It features production from Tonedeff, Domingo, Deacon The Villain, J-Zone and Marco Polo.  The single released today is produced by Tonedeff.

posts.title: Like You (Grieves + Budo Remix)
posts.writeup: Grieves and Budo pay homage to the latest in the Felt series with their own twist on the project with 4 remixes.  Don't get your hopes up and think Grieves has verses on these tracks because he doesn't (Sadly).  I know Budo is a BEAST on the beats with his chord progressions so I just assume Grieves did all the patches for the lead synths......? or not.  The remixes give a whole new life to these songs and make it as if I have never heard these tracks because the original beats were not my style.  You can get this remix EP for free HERE (or click the pic).

posts.title: Put It On Wax
posts.writeup: In case you haven't noticed lately here at Hillydilly we recognize that Australia pumps out some of the best music from all kinds of genre's.  The hip-hop scene in oz has always been rooted in amazing production with quality raps.  Back in the day I was always turned off by aussie hip-hop because of the accent, but that was a short lived opinion because now a days its plain to hear that the hip-hop coming out of Australia rivals any hip-hop in the world.  In my opinion Australia and the Northwest of the US are the two hot spots for great hip-hop these days.  Ozi Batla is one of the emcees from The Herd and Wild Colonial is his debut solo album that is produced by Sandro and features cuts by DJ Bonez.  Wild Colonial has the underlying theme of true school hip-hop with boom bap beats and lyrical substance that will keep your ears glued to the sonic story book that Ozi Batla depicts throughout his impressive debut.  Ozi Batla's accent is as thick as it comes but if you can throw out your ethnocentric point of view on accents its plain to hear that this dude can rap.  Wild Colonial is out on iTunes now so go HERE to grab it.

posts.title: Running
posts.writeup: Everybody's favorite reggae/rock music export out of Australia are releasing their 4th album on May 21st.  Mat Mchugh (Lead Vocalist) has said in the press release for this album that he has spent the better part of a year slaving away to craft this album down from 30 songs into a well conformed album that will further push the success of the band.  Spooks is not a big step away from the usual for TBG, but you can expect some new elements consisting of dubbed out keys with tons of reverb, a dancehall vibe on some tracks and horns that rival the horns of John Brown's Body.  Spooks will easily please TBG fans and will continue to attract more fans from all types of listeners because good music is good music and TBG fit right into that with ease.

posts.title: Showtime ft. Solo
posts.writeup: Exile is Spit Syndicate's second album that has seen some steady hype from the Aussie hip-hop scene in the last few months, so it was nice to see this released today. Spit Syndicate are 2 emcees Just Enuf and Nick Lupi who are at the front of the pack when it comes to new hip-hop groups from Australia. The production on Exile is very very solid, you can mail out your thank you cards to M-Phazes, A-Dit (Horrorshow), JSquared and Cam Bluff. The lyrical features includes Solo of Horrorshow, Sarah Corry and Joyride. Exile is one of the best albums to come out of Australia this year and will go down as one of this years best from the land of OZ. Get the album from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: A Good Name
posts.writeup: Shad has made quite the stir in the hip-hop scene in Canada from his very impressive second album The Old Prince from 2007. Shad is an emcee that you respect from the art form he clearly lays down in his music. The production on this album is definitely spectacular and when you have quality beats with an emcee like Shad its going mesh together like a cup of coffee and a joint. On the production tip is Classified, Kondrat, LaManga, Rich Kidd and features Broken Social Scene’s Lisa Lobsinger and Brendan Canning, Justin Nozuka, Relic the Oddity and Kamau. Shad's lyrics come off as effortless thoughts placed down neatly on beautifully crafted beats evoking smiles and satisfaction for fans of GOOD music.  This album is beyond good and will be steady on your play list when you get your hands on it.  You can preview the whole album HERE, look for this awesome album to hit stores and digital outlets on May 25th.

posts.title: On The Lookout
posts.writeup: Kae Sun is a Toronto via Ghana based musician who expresses many different styles in his music. Lion On A Leash is his debut album that dropped back in October 2009. Its easy to hear the talent in his music and this is one album I recommend for people looking for some original music with some international flavor. Take a listen to Free, its an upbeat funk hip-hop record that showcases Kae Sun at his best. You can get this album HERE. Shouts to Mr. Lapp for giving me the heads up via Twitter.

posts.title: XXXO
posts.writeup: The always good M.I.A returns this year with her follow up to the very successful Kala from 2007.  XXXO is the lead single from the new album that is produced by Blaqstarr and Rusko.  The new album is titled ///Y/ and will hit stores July 13th.  Prepare to hear this song all  over the radio and clubs soon.

posts.title: My Brain
posts.writeup: Debaser are Sapient and Ethic on the raps and features production from Sapient.  Anytime you get Sapient on the production you're going to get an ear pleasing dose of musical wizardry.  Peerless is another notch on the hip-hop belt for the duo from Portland.  You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: Natural Selection
posts.writeup: Panacea are every hip-hop fans oasis from the mundane of mediocrity .  K-Murdock and Raw Poetic skyrocket you away from boredom with their jazzy-soulful upbeat music that will induce head nodding and prompt you to open up your eyes to the music.  12 Step Program continues Panacea's impressive track record of quality hip-hop with another must have album.  You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: Bare Feast
posts.writeup: When it comes to being absolutely mind blowing RATATAT fits right into that description like a glove . RATATAT pack an endless array of mind numbing patches that incorporate just about every electronic modulation you could think of.  I'd hate to see these patches inside Reaktor!  LP4 is the brand new album with 12 new tracks to take you to a musical place that is very unfamiliar, yet highly ear pleasing and entertaining.  LP4 hits stores May 17th.

posts.title: Resurrect Me
posts.writeup: Weapon Aid is a collection of unreleased songs that Braille wrote in the summer of 2009. So this album is not being pushed as an official new album but more of a present for the fans to get a taste of Braille again before he drops the official new album, Audibly Enhanced Dreams, this year on Syntax Records. The majority of this album is produced by Big Jus of Unknown Prophets. This album is out now on iTunes and you can get it HERE.

posts.title: Street Lights
posts.writeup: Natty has officially established himself as one of the best roots/reggae musicians in the world with 2008's Man Like I and now with the Things I've Done Ep.   The new Ep gives us a peak into some of the new material Natty has been working on with the minimal feel of just bass, guitar and percussion.  It doesn't take a full band or special production for Natty to make great music.  Back on April 15th this EP was released with a new way of giving fans exclusive access to the recording process of Natty's new album.  Just go HERE and sign up to Pledge music and check out all the different packages that you can get with the EP.  If you live in the UK make sure to check him out as his is about to embark on a mini tour across the UK.  Click the pic for his myspace.

posts.title: Damian Marley + Nas – "Africa Must Wake Up" Ft K’Naan
posts.artist: Damian Marley + Nas – "Africa Must Wake Up" Ft K’Naan
posts.writeup: They continue to butcher the whole experience of hearing this album in its entirety by leaking another track from Distant Relatives.  There is no way I am going to not listen to these songs, and I am sure you're in the same boat as me.  Africa Must Wake Up is one of the songs from the album that I was looking forward to the most due to hype from a friend who saw this song at Rock The Bells last summer.   African Must Wake up clocks in around 6 minutes of Nas with 2 verses, Damian on the choruses with one verse and K'Naan who does a great verse in his native language (Somalian).  Distant Relatives will hit stores May 18th.

posts.title: Know About Us
posts.writeup: The Let Go are ready to release their follow up to 2008's Tomorrow Handles That on May 2nd.  I did not have any nice things to say about their last album but I can honestly say positive things about the new album this time around.  So I apologize to TLG for being so harsh back in 08.  Morning Comes is a big step in the right direction for The Let Go who have really produced a solid album with more that one or two good songs.  You can't get away with skimming through this album without stopping on every song to let it play out.  Enlisted for some vocal help on the album is the likes of P.O.S, Soulcrate Music, Eyedea, Pigeon John and JFK of Grayskul.  Check for this on iTunes May 2nd.

posts.title: Ganja Train Ft. Kyle Stoopid & Moxley
posts.writeup: C-Money of Slightly Stoopid has finally released his long awaited solo album that features the likes of Kyle from Slightly Stoopid, G-Love, Elliot Martin of John Brown's Body, Kevin Kinsella of 10 Ft Ganja Plant, Angelo Moore and Dre Gipson of Fishbone.  C-Money has been a journey man in the reggae scene for years originally starting out with JBB and working with 10ft Ganja Plant.  To now playing the trumpet for Slightly Stoopid.  Every time I see this guy live he looks like he is having a blast on stage.  Family Business is a fun record with great vocal performances complimented by melodic horns and savvy bass lines that you would expect from a music lover like C-Money.  The horns on Sly are brilliant.  The same can be said with B37H with its beautiful pads, synths, bass and horns.  The musical arrangement of Family Business is thick and plentiful that will keep listeners like us happy.  You can get this from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Never Be The Same
posts.writeup: Back in '09 High Tide hit the studio to record an Acoustic EP but ended up scrapping the idea and made it into a full album, with 9 new tracks. And here we are with The Rooted that is being released today! High Tide are one of the best bands in the reggae/rock scene lead by the awesome vocals of Ed Barrena and instrumentation lead by horns with awesome acoustic guitar riffs. You can take a listen to the whole thing HERE. After you go through it you can see why its worth the buy.

posts.title: People Talk ft. 14Kt
posts.writeup: &Devin The Dude has always been consistent throughout his entire career and is now back with his 6th album, aptly titled Suite #420 for a release date on 420.  People Talk was my favorite from the album, Devin rips it.  If you want to hear the rest of the album you can check it at your local iTunes store.

posts.title: Tighten Up
posts.writeup: Brothers is the 6th album for The Black Keys who have been building up steady hype in the music world with the very impressive first single, Tighten Up.  Its easy to say that the album will live up to the hype after a few listens through.  The Guitar's all over this album will be a big stand out as far as musical feel goes due to them dominating the album with awesome riff's and guitar tones to die for.  It makes no sense for me to describe this album anymore because this is where The Black Key's step in and let the music do the talking.  Brothers will hit stores and iTunes on May 18th.

posts.title: Still Keep Diggin Ft Jane Tyrrell
posts.writeup: Urthboy is about to embark on the Snequel Tour with Jane Tyrrell and Elgusto all around Australia for the next 3 months so if you live in Australia you have no excuses to miss these shows!  Sadly I live in Vancouver so I will not be able to make it.  Urthboy recently sent this track out to all his fans on his email list stating the single be spread far a wide.  The email does not state the producer but one can only assume its Elgusto.  The production features  Sabzi'esque soft synths and tight snare's + kick's that are topped off by the amazing vocals of Jane Tyrrell.  Jane is probably my favorite female vocalist out there, everything she sings on turns to gold.  Click the pic for tour dates.

posts.title: The Promised Land Ft Dennis Brown
posts.writeup: The Promised Land is the third leak from the Nas + Damian collab album that every music fan in the world has been waiting for ever since we heard of its inception. Stephen Marley heads up the production on this one with a heavy dubbed out riddim.  Every leaked track from this album has been good so you can expect this album to be one of the years best.  Distant Relatives drops May 18th.

posts.title: Welcome To Hell
posts.writeup: The Defamation Of Strickland Banks does a complete 180 from Plan B's debut album by showcasing his singing abilities more over his rapping.  Through a first glance this album could pass under the radar from all the singing due to your expectations of what Plan B used to sound like.  If you take the time to let the songs play all the through you will be pleasantly surprised.  That is of course you like the new direction Plan B is heading in.  Personally I am down with the new sound.  The track What You Gonna Do was the most rapped on song on the album and was my favorite.  Welcome To Hell is one track where Plan B showcases his singing style over the anthemic soulful ballad.  Other 2 great tracks were The Recluse and Darkest Place  You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: Sleep Awake
posts.writeup: Soulcrate music are 3 dudes (2 emcees Def & Dirt Dee, and producer/DJ Absolute) from Sioux Falls, Dakota. Their brand of hip-hop is exactly what you are looking for. The two songs I am posting here will set the precedent for you to add these guys to your music radar. It took me a few listens before I was hooked, so go ahead and get acquainted with this dope group! You can get this album from Itunes HERE.

posts.title: Macklemore & Ryan Lewis w/ SonReal & Giraffe Aftermath @ The Media Club [April 10/10]
posts.artist: Macklemore & Ryan Lewis w/ SonReal & Giraffe Aftermath @ The Media Club [April 10/10]

posts.title: Flawless Crowns
posts.writeup: This song is a few years old but has recently been given the 7 inch vinyl single treatment that is being sold as a collectors item, due to only 300 being made.  Producer Mondee lays down a classic sound for one of the best to rip it.  You can get this 7 inch HERE.

posts.title: Dont Sleep ft. Grouch
posts.writeup: Sapient and Ethic are the 2 members of Debaser who are also members of Sandpeople.  They will be releasing a new album titled Peerless on May 4th that features the likes of The Grouch, Eyedea & Cage.  The production will be handled by Sapient (of course) so you can expect the production to be one of the highlights of the album.  It will be available for purchase from their website HERE.  Peep the first track off the album with the always dope Grouch.

posts.title: Armada Latina Ft Pitbull + Marc Anthony
posts.writeup: It has been 6 years since the last Cypress Hill album so the expectations are bit low coming from my personal view but after a once over of this album I can say I am impressed.  Get It Anyway is a great example of a great first impression of Rise Up with banging instrumentation with classic B-Real raps draped all over the beat.  Rise Up features Mike Shinoda (Linkin Park), Everlast, Evidence, The Alchemist + more. This album will hit stores and digital outlets April 20th!  Click the cover art to hit up their website to stay on top of all the newest news from the group.

posts.title: Circus Hounds (Superfly Gettin High)
posts.writeup: As Common Market has been on hiatus with no plans for a follow up to the Hillydilly certified classic Tobacco Road, the emcee of the group has been in the lab with producer MTK to bring us the alter ego of RA Scion, superhero Victor Shade.  This album is a digital only release and you can grab it HERE. Circus Hounds was easily my favorite track off the album.  If you want some visuals for the lead single off the album check the video for 'Soothsayer' HERE.

posts.title: I Need A Dollar
posts.writeup: Its time to give this track its just due on Hillydilly because it has finally grown on me. Aloe Blacc has always been on point with his work with Exile where he raps and sings in the group Emanon. With I Need A Dollar Aloe has really stepped up his game with some amazing falsetto's. The track sounds like it is straight from the glory days of Miles Davis, Coltrane and such. Aloe has a new album dropping soon on Stones Throw, titled Good Things. You can get this track within a mini EP with 1 other track + instrumentals HERE. This track is also the theme song to the new HBO show How to Make It In America.

posts.title: New People (Empire Way Remix)
posts.writeup: Saba and Geo have been letting loose new music on a weekly basis and I couldn't be more pleased.  This week is the actual original mix of New People that was supposed to be on the OOF! EP but due to obvious sample clearing issues and the style of this song being a bit off from the flow of OOF! it was shelved.  Until now of course so go ahead and grab it!

posts.title: Shoe Box Ft Def 3
posts.writeup: Ron Contour (Moka Only's alter ego) teams up with Saskatoon producer Factor to drop a full album collaboration that doubles as Ron Contour's 3rd album.  Everywhere I read about this album people keep up that joke that Ron is Moka's cousin so I will spare you on that note.  The reality is that Moka over Factor's production is highly favorable and you should make a point to give this album a listen.  You can purchase it HERE.

posts.title: When The Night
posts.writeup: Aaron Nazrul is one of those artists that has a great voice along with lyrical material that paints a picture of one mans journey through this thing we call life. Aaron is known as 'Butterfly Man' which is known in SE Asia cultures as a man with a restless soul. So much can be said about our generation and how we are travelling so much and are tired of staying stagnant in our lives. Aaron's music is a direct reflection of the new generation of people who feel that need for more authenticity in their lives. Aaron is now working with a new 10 piece band and they call themselves The Boom Booms. They are currently recording a new album so keep a lookout for that one around late summer. You can bet you will see that album on here! You can grab this album from CDbaby HERE.

posts.title: Whatever
posts.writeup: Opensouls are an 9 piece band from New Zealand and Kaleidoscope is one album that deserves a look from every music lover out there. Opensouls are lead by one female vocalist Tyra Hammond and emcee Bjorn. They are backed by Isaac Aesili (Trumpet), Cameron Allen (Sax), Julien Dyne (Drums), Chip Matthews (Bass), Jeremy Toy (Guitar), Godfrey De Grut (Keys), and Harlin Davey (MPC). The combination of Tyra and Bjorn with the incredibly funked out beats will have your musical attention in seconds. The beat on Whatever is awesome.

posts.title: Unconditional Love Feat Am Radio
posts.writeup: We all became familiar with Marv's music earlier this year when he dropped the very impressive Mental Picture Machine but what needs to be brought to attention is his sophmore album from 2008 that flew under the radar.  Dream Catcher Juice is equally impressive with a great mixture of styles from singing or rapping over jazzy funked out beats that are always ear pleasing.  You can get this album from CDbaby HERE.  His first album can also be found on CDbaby.

posts.title: Suicide Girl (feat. Planetary, Doap Nixon & Apathy)
posts.writeup: AOTP are back with a new full length LP that sees all members from the original album contributing on The Unholy Terror.  These guys need no introduction and if you're a fan of these dudes you probably already have this and are wondering what took me so long to post up a track from it.  Well here you go, this track was my favorite off the album.  This album drops March 30th, you can pre-order it HERE.

posts.title: Captivate Dub
posts.writeup: Mista Savona (not pictured above) is a dub specialist from Melbourne, Austrailia who works with a 10 piece band in collaborating with top notch reggae artists from all over the world. After the highly acclaimed, Melbourne Meets Kingston, the man is back at it again with his amazing musical arrangements, keeping the dub progressive and never predictable. Featured on this album are reggae heavyweights Sizzla, Capleton, Alton Ellis, Horace Andy, Prince Alla + many more. Chant Rasta Sound was Alton Ellis' last recording before he died in October of 2008. You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: Revolution
posts.writeup: On March 26th (April 6th in Canada and US) we are going to be treated to a new John Butler Trio album that sees two new members and new material that is being quoted as " my best work to date" by John Butler. JBT have always topped my list as one of my favorite bands that not only bring a musical element that is second to none but a social awareness of lyrics that represent reality. John Butler's lyrics lay out a musical cinema for everyone to enjoy in their own way. If you were to describe music to anyone who has never heard of such a thing, April Uprising would be that album you passed to that person. It has the complete package of substance with sweet melodies, vocal brilliance, wicked guitar work, and a sense of euphoria that every great album has. JBT fans are going to sick their teeth into with what I think is John Butler's most complete album to date. Standout favorites include Revolution, I'd Do Anything, Johnny's Gone, I'm A Fool, Look Like You, Steal It, Mystery Man... anymore and I might as well name the whole album. You can Pre-order the album HERE and hit up their website by clicking the album art to preview snippets of the album. Sadly I am not allowed to stream any songs but you can just trust me that its going to be one of the best albums of 2010. To hold you over till the album here is a live version (acoustic) of the albums title track One Way Road.

posts.title: Tighten up
posts.writeup: The Black Keys plan to release their newest album this May titled Brothers. Tighten up is a great track that will turn heads and get people excited for the new album. Dangermouse lends out his hand for the production on this awesome track complete with some amazing guitar work, especially when they kick in those 16th note riffs at the end of the track.

posts.title: Ride
posts.writeup: Decon Records released this must have compilation back in '09 with a guest list that will rival any compilation I have ever came across. Its packed with new material from Chali 2na + Anthony Hamilton, Zion I, Jurassic 5, Aceyalone, Skillz, Casual, Dilated Peoples, Del the Funky Homosapien + many more. Do your self a favor and get this from itunes HERE. The Chali and Anthony Hamilton track is so FRESH.

posts.title: Towards the Future f. Deacon the Villain (Prod. Cook Classics/saxophone by J.Molloy)
posts.writeup: Towards The Future is a project from Japanese Producer Cook that features a nice package of emcees from Deacon The Villain of Cunninlynguists, Tunji, Inverse, Outasight, Nieve, Aloe Blacc, Sene + More. Information on this album is pretty scarce so bare with my insight into this album. But do yourself a favor and take a listen to the best track off the album with Tunji tearing up his verses and Deacon doing his singing.

posts.title: Vibes
posts.writeup: I am as green as that album cover when it comes to Sinizen but that green will turn to black after a quick musical blaze up with this FREE album they just released! Click the pic or click HERE to begin your download and get acquainted with Sinizen from Huntington Beach, CA. The songs I have posted here are my 2 favorites. I love the guitar on Shoot Em Down and on Vibes that acoustic guitar with the synth gets my head bopping.

posts.title: Paul Valery
posts.writeup: The always dope Blue Scholars have just let loose a new track that is about how the future used to be so bright but now its so bleak with all the apocalyptic thoughts and 2012 ish.  If you want to read the exact words from the band about the track go to their site HERE.  The track samples Owl City's Fireflies.

posts.title: You Dont Know (feat. Tunji)
posts.writeup: The Iration camp are releasing You Don't Know as a promo single for free download off of one of the best albums of 2010, Time Bomb! The track features one of underground hip-hop's best, Tunji, over the signature sound of Iration. Time Bombs is available on itunes right now! Click HERE to grab it and watch it climb the charts on itunes.

posts.title: Its Easy
posts.writeup: Truthlive's lyrical content is heavy on substance with subject matter for the music fan who is down with listening to someone with a good sense of reality depicted in their music. The production on Patience (handled by Jake One) leaves the listener with a raw underground feel that stains your mind with the huge kicks and snare hits. Patience gets a thumbs up from me and will be my underground hip-hop fix for the next month. This album will hit stores April 20th, you can pre-order hit HERE.

posts.title: Big Appetite
posts.writeup: >ALO - Man Of The World - 2010(Folk/Acoustic/Rock)Zach Gill and company are back this year to give us a new album full of goodness from the  gravy sounds of ALO.  You can get Man Of The World from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Unity ft. Matisyahu
posts.writeup: Trevor Hall's vocals come equipped with a deep soothing undertone that will grip your mellow moods with ease through a positive message that will bring a smile to your day. Mr. Hall has been doing the music thing since his debut in 2004 with Lace Up Your Shoes and with albums also released in '06 and 2 albums in '08. I have not heard any of his previous albums so I can't compare this one to any of them but after one listen through this album I can bet his discography is going to be quality. Click HERE to get this album on iTunes.

posts.title: Time Bomb
posts.writeup: There comes a time when a rising band drops an album that shoots them into a whole new level of success, and for Iration, Time Bomb is going to blow them into that stratosphere! Iration have been bubbling on the reggae/rock scene for the last few years with quality releases like the Sample This EP and their first full album, No Time For Rest. But now they are all ready to release the album that has that professional sound along with the promotional backing of being on LAW Records. I have been rocking out to this album for the last few days and it seems like every time I listen to it I keep finding new songs that I like. The title track (Time Bomb) is still easily my favorite with its awesome sing along melodies from Micah and the layered guitars that will having me playing the crap out of this song for the next month. Another stand out track is the positive emotion enducing 'Change My Mind', the B section grabs me every time I hear it. Track 3 'Let Me Inside', will also make your list of favorites from the very cool guitar picks, wah's and distortion. The guitar work all over this album really adds a great dimension to the overall sound and production value. Other favorites include, Turn Around, All In You, Coming Your Way, Love/Hate and The End. Basically every track on this album has replay value thus making this album a must have! Time Bomb will hit itunes and stores March 16th, you can pre-order it now just click HERE.

posts.title: The Pushers Party
posts.writeup: Portugal. The Man have just released their newest album on March 2nd that you can get HERE. If you prefer the physical copy with some goodies attached you can get it directly from the band HERE. American Ghetto is an album that touches on many different styles and keeps up a flow of creativity from front to back. It is an enjoyable listen that will keep your mind busy with the progressive instrumentation and vocal melodies.

posts.title: Smile For The Blade
posts.writeup: Chicago Il, emcee Grieves has been making waves recently by touring extensively on the Every Never Is Now Tour w/ Dessa and P.O.S and now just being added to the roster of the indie hip-hop powerhouse Rhymesayers. Grieves' also just had his last album, 88 Keys And Counting with Budo re-released on the Rhymesayers imprint that you can get HERE. You can get this previously unreleased EP from itunes for 7 bucks HERE. Confessions of Mr. Modest is an impressive EP that also features a track with the Cunninlynguists, get it now!.

posts.title: Day By Day
posts.writeup: Its amazing what good production can do for emcees. Toni Shift has allowed Theory Hazit to elevate his game musically to a point where I will be reaching for repeat listens. I have never heard Theory Hazit over quality production but after Modern Marvels I will be putting Theory Hazit in a whole new light. Toni Shift will be on your hip-hop radar after you get a taste of this guy's beats. Make sure you check this one out. You can purchase this album HERE.

posts.title: Rhyme Games Ft Gift Of Gab & Whosane
posts.writeup: The L.A beatmaker Elusive has dropped his latest production compilation album that features the likes of Gift Of Gab (Blackalicious), Royce Da 5'9, Rass Kass, Planet Asia, El Da Sensei, + many more. On my favorite track 'Rhyme Games', Gift Of Gab brings his A game with the quick verbal mastery represented over the sample driven beat from Elusive. Whosane ends off the track with a very solid verse of his own. You can get this HERE.

posts.title: Pull, Push, Let Go Ft Eligh
posts.writeup: Inspired Flight are a new duo of turntablist OpenOptics and guitarist/songwriter Chavez. They will be dropping their debut album May 1st that will see the likes of Scarub and Rugged Monk from The Wu-Tang Black Knights featured on the album. The first single is a dope collab with Eligh from the Living Legends.

posts.title: Three Legged Dog
posts.writeup: Santa Cruz's Wooster will be responsible for soothing any mood with edgy vocals from lead singer Brian Gallagher and sweet harmonies from female vocalist Caroline Kuspa. You can get their debut album from CDbaby HERE. I actually met Caroline on my recent trip to Central America so its cool to see her making good music and having it end up on my site 2 years later!

posts.title: Remedy
posts.writeup: Dominic Bali & The Roots Republic will fill your musical prescription to the brim with a diverse array of vocal melodies over funked out bass lines with some tasty instrumentation. Dominic Bali will sound like Matisyahu one second then sound like Jay from The Movement and sometimes I even hear a bit of Krayzie Bone when he busts out his quick verbals. If your a fan of this genre you need to get this album added to your ipod asap. Get it HERE. Huge shouts to Dirk for the heads up!

posts.title: Remember Ft Black Milk, Promise & Liya
posts.writeup: Vernon, BC emcee Tassnata will be releasing his brand new album, Between Planets due for a Spring 2010 release. Remember is the first leak that features the one and only Black Milk over the soulful sample driven beat from Northern Profit. Photo courtesy of LMW Photo.

posts.title: Soldier Of Love
posts.writeup: I finally got around to listening to the new Sade and was blown away by the title track, 'Soldier of Love". The heavy drums and guitar give this song a definite hip-hop feel. It has been 10 years since her last album and I am sure a lot of us forgot how good this woman can sing. This album is out now, you can get it HERE.

posts.title: Solar Bowls
posts.writeup: The NorthWest has been responsible for some of the best hip-hop in recent memory and after listening to Marv Ellis you can add another dope emcee to that ever growing list of top notch hip-hop acts from the NW. The Eugene/Portland native, Marv Ellis will sing and rap into your mind creating a musical mental picture of a a man's art depicted on funk-soul infused beats. You can get this album HERE. The artwork was done by Hanif Wonder. Mucho Gusto Marv Ellis.

posts.title: Michelle Malkin
posts.writeup: Geo recently let loose some inspiration after watching the neo-con Michelle Malkin on Fox news. Geo flipped the Sade song Feel No Pain by flipping the beat and sampling the 'get a job' line. The result is a very dope track that will have your head bopping and listening along to what the man has on his mind. This is my kind of hip-hop!!

posts.title: A Fathers Son
posts.writeup: The Rainwater LP is Cope's first independent release after being tied down in a record contract for the last 10 years. With this new album you can tell that Citizen Cope is back to making the amazing music he was born to do. Being free of the dreaded record contract has released the shackles that may of hindered the musical creation from Cope, but all that will be forgotten when you take a listen to the Rainwater LP. Get this album on Itunes HERE.

posts.title: We Will Make A Song Destroy
posts.writeup: Regardless of what kind of genre(s) you listen to on a regular basis you can always detect good music from NOT good music, right? Well this album is a good example of when a certain genre may not be you're forte. After one listen to this album you will find it undeniably good from the instrumentation to the vocal melody's. Rogue Wave is on Jack Johnson's Brushfire Records label. This album will hit digital outlets March 2nd! Click HERE to pre-order.

posts.title: Burn One
posts.writeup: The 7 piece Fortunate Youth bring a breath of fresh air with their brand of reggae/dub from South Bay, CA. This EP is their debut and judging from this EP I will be adding these guys to my list of good bands from the reggae/dub genre to keep an ear on. You gotta love the heavy keys and the sick guitar solos that these guys bust out all over the EP, it makes a solid listen from front to back. To get this album you have to contact them via their manager HERE.

posts.title: Highs N Lows
posts.writeup: Dot Da Genius recently let loose this track that was originally recorded for Cudi's demo before his career shot off. This was produced by Dot Da Genius who also produced Day N Nite. Look for Cudi's second album Cudder due this year.

posts.title: Ya Feel Big?
posts.writeup: I am not sure just how hidden this track is, but if your a fan of the Hoods you need to check out this track. Ya Feel Big is an ode to below par rappers and anyone that does not represent the truth. Its songs like these that allow us to get some frustration out from fake artists through a lyrical tirade from the always dope Hilltop Hoods. This track can be found by pressing rewind on a cd player, on track 1 from 2006's The Hard Road. If you're in Canada you should check their myspace and look for a venue near you. (Click the pic for their myspace).

posts.title: Fast Forward Rewind
posts.writeup: Vancouver's Sonreal has been on a steady rise for the last few years with the release of his debut from 2008, Good Morning and now with his sophmore release, The Stroll. With the new album you really do 'stroll' through the many different styles Sonreal can express over the course of the album whether it be a hip-hop banger or an acoustic hip-hop affair. Sonreal easily gets your attention with his on point lyrics and delivery. Combine that with some solid production and you got yourself an album that is worth your attention!. You can grab this album via Itunes or CDBaby.

posts.title: Old Man Vs Himself
posts.writeup: This compilation from 1999 features a rare track that has Citizen Cope rapping over a huge snare lead drum kit. Its really cool to hear Citizen Cope in his early form with a style that I have never heard before. The production value of this track is pretty crazy considering how HUGE those drums sound with that long ass reverb time on that snare. Cope's voice is also EQ'd like a band pass to give it that early 90's feel.

posts.title: Stay Too Long
posts.writeup: Plan B is back in 2010 with a totally revamped style, for the people who became fans from Who Needs Actions When You Got Words and The Paint It Blacker Mixtape, will be a bit surprised. We all knew the guy can sing but he takes it to a new level with Stay Too Long. His new album, The Defamation Of Strickland Banks will be dropping this March!

posts.title: Spectacular ft. Sharon Jones
posts.writeup: Dujeous are that band you were looking for to renew your faith in hip-hop and you can expect their new album 'Day In Day Out', dropping sometime this year, to re-up your faith. Spectacular is the second single from the forthcoming album. There is also a very nice b-side to this single that you can get on itunes HERE.

posts.title: Goin Down Ft Terryman
posts.writeup: Add this album to your must hear hip-hop albums list of 2010 right now because the production on this album is on some Stoupe meets Hermitude style that will please you beyond expectations. The Q4 are a group of 3 producers from The Netherlands (Arts The Beatdoctor, Sense & STW). The album is entirely produced by the group with a combo of brain melting instrumentals with featured musicians that seem to give it the world music touch. When you listen to 'One Of These Days' you will find yourself immersed in the beautiful melodics of all the wonderful sounds that put you into another world. You can listen to the whole thing and order the album HERE.

posts.title: Buzzkill feat. Slug (Prod. By Equalibrum)
posts.writeup: Girls Like Me is the newest album from the LA native, Intuition. The standout track on the album with Slug passes the litmus test with flying colors because that B section is going to be in your head all day. Slug kills it.

posts.title: NOBODY KNOWS
posts.writeup: The reggae.rock scene has been on idle for awhile so its nice to see some more good music from the genre. This is Natural Incense's newest album that is self proclaimed as their best work to date. You can grab this album from CDbaby HERE. The song I am posting here is very cool, its lead by killer bassline with a simple 2 chord guitar change with a 50ms delay that goes left and right. Light one up for this one.

posts.title: Strong Will Continue
posts.writeup: I haven't been more excited for an album in my entire life. This album WILL be dropping this year so its only a matter of time before we get blessed with what is for sure to be a classic. This will most likely be the first single from the album, Distant Relatives. Keep your ears open for news for a release date.

posts.title: Dutch – A Bright Cold Day
posts.writeup: Dutch is a collaboration with the mastery of Stoupe (Jedi Mind Tricks) combined with female vocalist Liz Fullerton. The unlikely duo originally hooked up back in 2006 on the classic Razorblade Salvation and now we see them with a whole album of material. The production handled by Stoupe lays down a red carpet for Liz to make the unique collaboration a success. There is no release date for the album yet so check their myspace page (click the cover art) for better updates on that front.

posts.title: Matches To Paper Dolls
posts.writeup: The wonderful Dessa from the Doomtree collective has a brand new album dropping on January 19th. Dessa is in a league of her own with her amazing voice and emcee skills, other aspiring female emcees take note, the bar has been set. A Badly Broken Code features production from Paper Tiger, Lazerbeak, Cecil Otter, MK Larada, and Big Jess. Click HERE to pre-order the album.

posts.title: Happy Days (Prod. By Tonedeff)
posts.writeup: Thankfully we have Pack FM to ring out the crap from all the shitty rappers out there with a brand new single that makes me so happy to hear truth and realism in these days and times. Because seriously the world is going down the shitter, for instance we have a Nobel Peace Prize winner sending more troops into war and we got these rappers who are still not speaking the truth. Thank god for truthful artists like Pack FM. Go ahead and listen to the lyrics and get that refreshing feeling of no bullshit. For the video on this click HERE.

posts.title: 40 Day Dream
posts.writeup: Here goes a switch up from the norm on Hillydilly as we branch out to some very cool music that has just been brought to my attention. While this album has made many indie blogs top 10 albums of the year, over here we are not on top of the indie scene as we should be (apparently). But don't fret if your not into the indie scene! Just expect a more diverse presentation of the wonderful world of music that is out there! This album is available right now from itunes and you can get it HERE. To check out more from the LA based band check out their myspace by clicking the cover art. (a shout out goes to one of the Jersey Shore's finest)

posts.title: Paint In Black (Cover)
posts.writeup: The Dirty Heads let loose a cover of The Rolling Stones classic Paint It Black today via their Twitter. The guys also just posted up a remix of Check The Level Ft Slash & M Shadows on their myspace. Click the pic or click HERE to be directed to their page.

posts.title: The High Road
posts.writeup: Broken Bells is the new collaboration of Dangermouse and the lead singer of The Shins, James Mercer. Anything Dangermouse touches always ends up being quality music, so this album is one to keep a look out for. The full album will be dropping March 9th. Click HERE to pre-order the album.

posts.title: Fruits (feat. Malbec)
posts.writeup: The Knux have just released a free EP via their twitter earlier today. The F You EP contains 6 new tracks that you can download for FREE from HERE. This EP reminds you to keep an eye on The Knux when they release a follow up to one of 2008's best albums in Remind Me In 3 Days.

posts.title: The Rain
posts.writeup: If you are down for some good ol reggae dub tracks with some tasty vibes then you need to check out Predator Dub Assassins right NOW. Fully Qualified is their newest album you can grab from CDbaby HERE. And if you want to find out more click the cover art to hit up their myspace.

posts.title: Stay This Way (Remixed By Jazzy Jeff) ft. Phil Nash, Big Pooh, & Chaundon
posts.artist: Mick Boogie & Peter Bjorn and John
posts.writeup: The hip-hop world has taken over PBJ's latest album and added a talented list of producers and emcees to rework the album. Emcess include Talib Kweli, GZA, Black Milk, Wale, Bun B, and Big Pooh of Little Brother. The remixes were done by Jazzy Jeff, 6th sense, The Kickdrums, Marco Polo + more. This was released for free back in September, so go ahead and grab this if you haven't already. Click the pic for the link or click HERE.

posts.title: OMG!
posts.writeup: Number 15 in the False Hopes saga brought to you by the Doomtree collective is available for 7 bucks from HERE or click the pic. False Hopes XV features tracks from Sims, Mike Mictlan, P.O.S, Dessa, Cecil Otter and features production from Lazerbeak, Paper Tiger, Cecil Otter and Rich Rok. The track I am posting here features Mike Mictlan over a nice piano laced beat that adds a great touch to the track from the beautiful harmonics of those keys.

posts.title: Weight Of The World
posts.writeup: The Grouch has one of the most honest voices in hip-hop and has always been a refreshing listen. You can always count on The Grouch to have the ego in check and just be a rapper you aren't embarrassed to like. Take a listen to the standout track off the album with classic hip-hop strings filled in with a catchy synth. This album production was handled by DJ Fresh. Get the album from itunes. HERE. (Recognize that symbol the grouch has made with the eye on his forehead?)

posts.title: Exposed Sampler
posts.writeup: The Pier keeps holding it down for the reggae/rock scene by putting together a free exposed sampler that has 30 tracks from 30 bands. The likes of The Expendables, Passafire, Dirty Heads, The B Foundation, John Brown's Body and Ballyhoo lent their tastes to compile it. The compilation showcases some more bands to keep an eye on including new Top Shelf that has the catchiest guitar progression since Iration's Wait And See. The long beach trio of Seth Davis finally deliver a much needed banger to get their ball rolling with some more fans as they prepare to drop a EP. If you have not heard of Seth Davis yet then I suggest to hit up their myspace and take a listen to some of their tracks. Seth Davis are a deadly combo of raps and reggae skanks, they make music to get hyped about. Go ahead and get this already and expect to find some new bands on your way. Click HERE to be directed to ThePier.org's site to get it.

posts.title: Im Ready
posts.writeup: The always good JBT will be releasing a brand new album dropping in 2010 titled 'April Rising'. One Way Road is the first single from the new album and is being packaged on a EP with a live version of One Way Road, a B-side and a unreleased demo. For all the truthers out there if you take a closer look at the cover art you can see the letters NWO (New World Order) shown vertically filled with images the falling Amercian currency, the useless wars waging and a image hinting to our society hurdling towards a police state. Its really good to see a musician bring this to light. You can pre-order the EP HERE and if you are in NZ or AUS check your itunes and feel lucky that only you guys can purchase this right now.

posts.title: Payroll Ft Yellow
posts.writeup: If your down for some of that classic 70's sounding reggae then your going to need to put P-Dub on your music radar and get in the know. These guys have 2 albums released already from '05, '07, and will be releasing a new one soon. You can grab these albums from CDbaby HERE. I have not heard either of the albums but from the songs on his myspace its sounding like a purchase for the albums will be in order. P-Dub has also posted up a link for a collection of tracks he was featured on, click HERE to download.

posts.title: Missing The Feeling
posts.writeup: Eligh is a wizard on the beats and the last installment in the GBM series exemplifies why Eligh is one of my favorites. Its sonic mastery that seams itself to your thoughts and catches your mind for the 3 minutes that it plays. Get this album with over an hour of music from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Root Trees
posts.writeup: Here is a must hear track from Clutchy and Shawn's newest collaboration, Fascinating Fingers. Root Tree is a very cool track that is undeniably funky! Get this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Cudderisback (Feat. Vampire Weekend)
posts.writeup: Kid Cudi recently leaked a new freestyle over Vampire Weekend's Ottoman. Cudi has officially shot up to one of my favorite artists in any genre. The Man On The Moon is a classic in my eyes. If this dude rolls through your town GO SEE HIM.

posts.title: Runaway
posts.writeup: Emilio Rojas has been doing this thing for a few years now in the underground scene and it seems with this new release that he is going to break back on to the scene again. He has gone through many name changes as well, from Sycorax One to Raks One and now he uses his surname. Although this is not the official debut from Rojas it still has some standout tracks. Hopefully we can see a full album from this guy sooner than later. This mixtape is available for free download click HERE to get it!

posts.title: Pink Lemonade Ft Apt
posts.writeup: Halifax native, Ghettosocks reps a classic boom bap style over head nodding production that will get the attention of hip-hop fans worldwide . Treat Of The Day is Ghettosocks' newest album that features Edgar Allen Floe, Cesar Commanche, Muneshine, D-Sisive, Timbuktu + more. Grab the album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Seasons Change
posts.writeup: Today (Nov. 24th) GPGDS is releasing a selection of high quality live recordings of previously unreleased tracks. The album is 11 tracks deep with selections from 5 concerts between 2008-2009. The band has trimmed down to a 4 piece recently and are in the works of working on a new record. GPGDS are renowned for their live act and LIVE UP! will embrace the rumors and hype with mutual agreement amongst fans. Get this album from iTunes HERE.

posts.title: Throw it Away
posts.writeup: Kyprios is one talented artist and 12:12 showcases a man in his prime musically. Its hard not to enjoy Kyprios' music with the beautiful word play that he brilliantly combines with his melodic choruses. Add this album to your must hear list right now and let the music speak for itself. Get 12:12 from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Otherside
posts.writeup: Macklemore and Ryan Lewis are teaming up this fall to release a new EP called V.S. that will sample songs from groups that some hip-hop fans may not be familiar with (not hillydilly readers!) The EP will cover tracks from Beirut, The Killers, Arcade Fire, Red Hot Chilli Peppers + more. The first leak samples the classic RHCP - Otherside with orchestral strings, heavy drums, clever synths and incredible raps from the always dope Macklemore. V.S will drop Nov 27th and will be available to buy through a donation. Check back later for a full review and link to get this album.

posts.title: Crazy For You
posts.writeup: Shwayze's music has a universal feel to it that will take you on a ride through many genres, its catchy and worth checking out. This is my personal virgin listen to Shwayze thanks to a friend (shouts to DOLAN). So in case you have never heard of this dude here is a great track from the new album (Crazy For You) and from his first self-tilted album (Buzzin'). Click the cover art for his myspace. Get the new album from itunes

posts.title: G.I Joesephine
posts.writeup: The favorable duo of Murs and Slug keep it going in the Felt series with the brand new addition. This time around we get Aesop Rock's production to stir up 'murs-n-slug' and bring out a whole new sound that is totally different from the past 2 Felt's. I have never given Aesop's production a good chance or thought, but after this album I will be checking for his work. Felt 3 will hit itunes Nov 17th, click HERE to pre-order.

posts.title: Aint Nothing Like You (Hoochie Coo) Ft. Mos Def and Jim Jones - from YouTube by Offlib
posts.writeup: On November 27th we are going to be treated with a Hip-Hop Rock mashup album with The Black Keys putting down the instrumentals for emcees like Mos Def, Raekwon, Jim Jones, RZA. Billy Danze, Ludacris, Pharoah Monche, ODB + more. There are many videos of the studio sessions floating around the net right now and they are stoking up the masses for what is to be sure a SICK album. Here is the first taste with Mos Def on the chorus and Jim Jones slick talking on 2 verses. You can pre-order this album HERE.

posts.title: Stay Humble
posts.writeup: Here is a heads up to all the music heads out there that are looking for new music with a fresh sound. The Lytics have what your looking for with their own brand of hip-hop from Winnipeg, Canada. According to their twitter they are currently recording their new album so keep on the look for that! Click the cover art to hit up their myspace. GET THE EP HERE!

posts.title: Count
posts.writeup: Dodger are back with another impressive offering of 5 brand new tracks. Its easy to hear the commercial appeal these guys have so I definitely see good things for this band. Their music is more on the harder side of rock than reggae, its a nice switch up from your regular reggae/rock group. These guys are from Hamilton, Ontario. Another band to add to our ever growing list of Canadian acts that we can be proud of! Unfortunately you can't purchase this yet but click the pic for their myspace and you will find out when you can buy this EP.

posts.title: Livin It
posts.writeup: The one man army of the reggae dubs, Stick Figure is back to end off 2009 with the irie vibes and put us in a warm place as old man winter rolls through. Smoke Stack is a well rounded album that flows well from track to track, you can let this one play right through without worrying. The dubs are really creative on Smoke Stack with the classic stabs on the keys combined with clever organ fills. Stick Figure's music is must have for fans of the reggae/dub and chances are you are already familiar with this guy's work. This album is currently #1 on itunes reggae charts, so congrats! Grab it from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Slumdog Millionare Ft Cyndi Lauper
posts.writeup: Wyclef is back with this brand new mixtape hosted injunction with DJ Drama with all new songs. In my opinion this mixtape is much better than Clef's last album. You could get away with thinking this is an album with the quality. This mixtape will be available Nov 10th but you can pre-order it HERE.

posts.title: The Park (Blue Sky Black Death Remix)
posts.writeup: Vol 2 of the Strange Journey series is finally here for Cunnin' fans to feast on. On Vol 2 we see new tracks with remixes and the live version of Nothing To Give with Club Dub. The features include Sean P, E-40, Evidence, Jermside, Freddie Gibbs, Witchdoctor and the track I am posting up here features some of Seattle's best emcees (Macklemore, Geologic of Blue Scholars and Grieves). You can grab this right now from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Light Years
posts.writeup: Gift of Gab is back with a new solo album that is not about aliens or Mars but more about if we keep treating out planet the way we do, we may have to escape to Mars! You can stream the whole album via his myspace by clicking the cover art. Here are my 2 favorite tracks off the album. Make sure to cop this album as it hits itunes and stores tomorrow (Nov 3rd).

posts.title: Dont Wait
posts.writeup: Wow this track is sick, you gotta love when you listen to a song and 10 seconds in you are hooked and bopping your head. This is the first single TBG have released for free download from their brand new album that will be dropping May 2010. TBG have steadily evolved their sound through each album and I expect the new album to follow the same trend. Don't Wait is a brilliant first taste of new music from the guys.

posts.title: Shad K ft Jermiside & Destruments - The Calling (Prod Danny Diggs)
posts.writeup: Danny Diggs is a beatmaker form Galway, Ireland and this is his debut release that you can get for free! HERE. DD really lays down some nice beats for some great emcees like Shad K and Akrobatik. Put this guy on your radar and check out his myspace by clicking on the picture Introducing also features Soulstice and a crew from the UK (Maverick Sabre) that I think sound pretty good on the Lost Boys (posted below) .

posts.title: To Be For Real
posts.writeup: Here goes the next leak from the new Cunninlynguists album Strange Journey Vol 2 due Nov 3rd. (Thats this Tuesday!) You can pre-order the album HERE.

posts.title: The Town
posts.writeup:
Before "Thrift Shop," before the Grammys, before any of it, there was Macklemore grinding in Seattle with nothing but a microphone and a point of view. The Unplanned Mixtape is where we first took notice — a raw, earnest project from a rapper who was clearly operating on a different frequency than his peers.

What stood out immediately was the writing. Macklemore wasn't rapping about what rappers were supposed to rap about. He was processing his life on record — addiction, ambition, faith, doubt — with a directness that felt genuinely rare in 2009. The production was scrappy but it didn't matter. The words carried everything.

"The Town" is the track that made it undeniable. A love letter to Seattle that doubles as a thesis statement for everything Macklemore would go on to do.

posts.title: In My Haze ft. Jane Tyrrell
posts.writeup: Horrorshow waste no time to release their second album after their debut 'The Grey Space" from 2008. Inside Story boasts emcee Solo rapping and singing over the impressive production of Adit. I find this album an enjoyable listen especially when you roll up to track 8 that features the beautiful voice of Jane Tyrrell (The Herd). This album will cement these guys on a list of ever growing acts from Australia that deserve international recognition. You can grab the album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Itll Be You
posts.writeup: Mr. Wesley is back with a brand new album that us Vancouverites have been anticipating for a while. D.W. has been on a steady rise in the last few years by releasing quality music that resonates with a solid base of music fans from Canada and worldwide. The new record sees Wesley spending a cool 5 months to record this album, a far cry from the days of recording an album in 5 days (as stated on his previous 3 albums). Its great to see success reach a good musician like Wesley. With all the accomplishments Wesley was able to hire a Producer (Dave Genn) for this album, allowing him to focus purely on the music and it shows! This self-titled comes off as his best album with a deep playlist that will have you checking out every song with a cool intent of playing this album over again. Grab this must have album on itunes HERE.

posts.title: HI 808 (Milkbag Rainbow Remix)
posts.writeup: So when I originally listened to this compilation I completely missed the remix. OOPS! I am at fault for being pessimistic towards remixes, just from the shear fact that 99% of remixes suck. But of course that is just my opinion. But when you have Sabzi behind the remix you know he will up the original. This mixtape showcases Sabzi's selections of some Hawaiian reggae flavor (dubbed as Jawaiian for short) you can download this for free -> HERE.

posts.title: Justice Keeperz
posts.writeup: Louisiana reggae rockers, Outlaw Nation are here to bring us their debut album on Stoopid Records that dropped Oct 13th. Outlaw Nation is Christian Simeon (Vocals, Guitar, Keys), Danny Woolen (Bass), Jackson White (Guitar) and JB "Da Drum Killa" on the Drums. I really dig the instrumentation on this album, it seems this album was put together with no fillers. Every track has its own distinct sound which gives the album more substance for the listener. After my usual quick listen I am left with 6 tracks that I will be analyzing over the next week. I suggest picking this album up because its simply worth it. Get the album HERE.

posts.title: The War
posts.writeup: It easy to say that this album is a must hear with the pure originality of style and sound. This man is better known as the third member of the Galpagos4 group Typical Cats. This is my virgin listen to this guy and I'm pretty stoked on what I am listening to right now. Kane's albums are voiced through 4 different characters who are all brothers. This album is the voice of the youngest brother, Min, who represents "the most tragic young, gifted, and dead - the suicide". He is also working on short stories to back up the stories of the Kane brothers. You can get this must have album HERE.

posts.title: My Kingdom
posts.writeup: I posted up a blurb on these guys a few months ago with their newest album, Rude World. But I am back today to hit you up with their debut EP from 2005 that has 3 tracks with 2 dubs. This EP is a must have! Soothing vocals eminate from behind the sultry instrumentation of the dub sound that will get in the groove within seconds. Look for a new album from these guys soon!

posts.title: Running Wild Ft E-40 & Evidence
posts.writeup: November 3rd will see the release of Strange Journey Vol. 2 featuring the top notch production of Kno and with a few tracks produced by Blue Sky Black Death and J-Zone. The second and final part in the Strange Journey series features Geologic of Blue Scholars, Tonedeff, Sean Price, Substantial, Poison Pen and Freddie Gibbs. Running Wild is the first released single from the album that sees Kno using some wicked keys that add a dark feeling to the track to compliment the subject matter. Click HERE to pre-order Strange Journey Vol 2.

posts.title: Urthboy- Tour CD
posts.writeup: Urthboy is currently touring Australia promoting the wicked new album Spitshine. He is selling this Limited Edition CD at the merch table complete with unreleased tracks, remixes, b-sides and the acoustic version of Ready To Go w/ Jane Tyrell. Click the pic to check his myspace out for venue and city details. If you haven't checked out Spitshine yet then I suggest you take a listen NOW.

posts.title: DQMOT
posts.writeup: The LA underground legends are back with their 7th album packed with Thes One and Double K's signature flows over the classic production of Thes One. I must say that this album is impressive and makes a fan like me very happy that these guys are still making good music. Carried Away hits stores Oct 13th. Click the pic for the myspace page.

posts.title: The Weed
posts.writeup: Mysitc MC is an Gambian Born artist who currently lives in Dennmark. The Weed is a new song along with a few others that have been released to his myspace recently. I assume these tracks will be off his new album. Its hard to find info on Mystic MC's past albums so If you can direct me to some more info on his past material please do! You can grab a bunch of his singles from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Nobody Listening
posts.writeup: Wait For Green is a 4 piece band from LA consisting of Drew Dockerill (Lead Vocals/Guitar), Rafael Britto (Lead Guitar), AJ Larson (Bass/Vocals), and Chris Shalchi (Drums). In Motion is their second release that you can grab from CDBaby HERE. Their first album 'All In' can also be grabbed from CDBaby HERE.

posts.title: American Nightmare Ft. Brother Ali & Scarface
posts.writeup: BK-One is Brother Ali's touring DJ and this is his compilation he put together full of his production that features a stacked guest list. The Brother Ali and Scarface track anchors this album with 2 great verses from the two emcees who link up for the unlikely collaboration. Radio Do Canibal features Slug, Raekwon, Black Thought, The Grouch, Brother Ali, Scarface, Aceyalone, Abstract Rude, Phonte + many more. You can pre-order the album from Fifth Element HERE. The album will be released Oct 6th.

posts.title: Da Math Ft Consequence & Insight
posts.writeup: Boycott Blues is a Trinidadian-American emcee who resides in Boston. This is his debut album that is garnering him some attention through the old school vibe of the album that is overseen by Electric Co.'s Insight. Grab this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Put It Down
posts.writeup: This is the third album I have featured in the last month from Wookiefoot. As you can see I have been doing my homework on Wookiefoot and I must say it is worth the time to do up a post for each album. Wookiefoot is Mark Murphy (vocals, guitar), Jojo Lash (vocals), Aimee Willmoth (vocals), Scooter Holtzinger (guitar, drums), Matt Cartee (drums), Larry (bass), Marcus Schmid (keyboards), Joe Mechtenberg (saxophone, flute), Emily Star (vocals), Alyssa Kluver (vocals), Nicole Paul (vocals). You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: I'm A Demigod
posts.writeup: This album is great and I am very impressed with the quality in the beats and the lyrical content. I always love retreating to some real hip-hop that is represented with pure class and skill. Apathy is in a class of his own with his in your face honest style. One my favorite tracks (content wise) 'Anyday' strikes up raps on our current situation in the world with some truthful words. I love hearing people speak the truth and actually know what they are talking about. Wanna Sunggle will hit stores and itunes Oct 6th.

posts.title: Guitar Sound
posts.writeup: Mr. Jenkees is back with more of his free flowing music. The guy is a well known youtube phenomenon with videos of him jamming on his keyboard. His music comes off as fun and ends up being a great listen. This is his newest album that has been anticipated for a year now.
