Mr Jenkees also raps on a few songs but I think he should stick to the keyboard! But I am sure he just raps because its fun for him, so who am I to hate on that? You can get this album right now HERE.

posts.title: Monkey See Monkey Do
posts.writeup: Wookiefoot are a band that combine great music with lyrical content that can be taken seriously. Take the cover as a great example for the type of vibe these guys get across. Wookiefoot are an 11 piece band from Minneapolis. Be Fearless And Play is their newest album and you can order it HERE.

posts.title: Welcome Home
posts.writeup: Yes it is necessary to have all those descriptions because Wookiefoot cover so many styles, its really quite amazing. Wookiefoot posses you with heavy drums and intense instrumentation one song and then switch it up completely from track to track. You find yourself caught off guard with each song, it adds a euphoria to the music. I think that is pretty rare and adds to the coolness of Wookiefoot's music. Check the myspace for more info and to listen to more songs off their new album. Look for a review on the new album soon. If you have the patience to let Dead In The Eyes play out you will be treated @ 3:00.

posts.title: Tight Rope
posts.writeup: When you see the name you immediately know that your going to hear 100% quality lyrically and muscially with the backing of the coolest cigarette smoking producer on the planet, ANT. It would be unrealistic to be able to review this album after two listens. But I will say that Us is jam packed with songs that are full of substance, so get ready to take the time to soak up this album. Us will hit itunes and stores Sept 22nd and make sure to see him on the Fresh Air tour with Evidence of Dilated Peoples. Click the pic to check out the tour dates to see if he will be near your city.

posts.title: here i am ft. marley rory and eric of rebelution
posts.writeup: SOJA (Soldiers of Jah Army) have progressively gotten bigger through releasing quality music and touring extensively for the past few years. And now we see them release this new album with many ears, old and new, anticipating the new material. I really enjoy the melodies of this album, its something that you can throw on and just vibe out to. If your in the mood to lay on the couch and go up up and away then I suggest taking a listen. You can grab this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: People People People
posts.writeup: Here is a sneak peak at a nice new track from the upcoming Giraffe Aftermath album. This song will have your head bopping in seconds with infectious guitar skanks combined with tasty acoustic licks, dubbed organ stabs and a dope bass line. Look for the new album soon! Check back later for more info on a release date and a full review! And if you want to hear the other released track click the pic to head over to their myspace.

posts.title: Sweet Love
posts.artist: Aaron Kamm And The One Drops
posts.writeup: Check out some original material from the trio who reside in Edwardsville, Illinois.. AKATOD is led by the impressive vocals of Aaron Kamm who also plays the lead guitar for the group and bassist Andy Dorms and drummer Sean Ralia. You can grab this album via CDbaby HERE.

posts.title: Youre Here
posts.writeup: Passafire return to our ears fresh off of garnering mass appeal from their successful sophomore release, Submersible. From the last stellar album fans will be anticipating Everyone On Everynight and rightfully so! Don't expect to be disappointed with this album, through my first listen I was forced to start it all over again! When September 15th rolls around make sure to get this album on itunes. You can click the pic to head over to their myspace and listen to more tracks off of Everyone On Everynight.

posts.title: Soundtrack 2 My Life
posts.writeup: Innovative, clever and re-freshing are words that best describe the stellar debut album from Kid Cudi. Its good to see new music coming out that comes from a place of creativity that is not just another carbon copy of whats hot right now. Cudi's music flows great from the verses to choruses through his singing rap style. You can bet you will be singing the words to these songs once you get to know them. This album sprung a early leak to the internet today so make sure to support when the official release date comes on Sept 15th. And make sure you see this guys live show! Man On The Moon:The End Of Day features production from Kanye West, RATATAT, Emile, Dot Da Genius, Jeff Bhasker, Free School, Plain Pat, Crada and Illfonics.. The album also features the guests MGMT, Kanye West, Common, Billy Cravens, and Chip Tha Ripper.

posts.title: Da Da Da
posts.writeup: If you are the mood for some sample based hip-hop instrumentals with tinges of jazz and funk then you need to check out this album. You can grab this album HERE.

posts.title: Lunch Money
posts.writeup: The last we heard these two together was 2004 with 'The Harvest'. I will always remember the amazing track 'A Little Something' from that release. Its good to see these two talented dudes back together again with new material. Qwel is the MC and Maker is the producer. These guys have been around for a while with their successful Chicago based label Galapagos4. Qwel has teamed up with many producers but they never seem to stack up to Qwel w/ Maker. Maker really sets the bar high with his work on this album. Its easy to come to that conclusion after listening to these 2 songs from 'So Be It'. Qwel's flow is very impressive over the reggae skank influenced 'Back Stage Pass'. You can get this album right now from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Here I am Ft Phonte, Brother Ali & The Grouch
posts.writeup: BK-One is Brother Ali's touring DJ and he has joined forces with producer Benzilla to form a compilation album inspired by their travels in Latin America. Radio Do Canibal features a stacked guest list of emcees including Slug, Brother Ali, Scarface, Black Thought, Raekwon, Murs, P.O.S, Blueprint, The Grouch + many more. You can pre-order the album HERE. This album will be released Oct 6th.

posts.title: Morning Rain
posts.writeup: I have been dying to hear this album since 2006!! I remember bugging these guys back then asking them when the next album was coming out, and now its 2009 and here it is!! Vinnie said in 2006 that he was switching up the format by using a live band and straying away from strictly hip-hop. That is sure the case with The Oslo Agreement as it sees them dabbing in different genres. The album sounds nothing like previous work, but I am not complaining as its going to take some serious listening sessions to dissect this album. Through a first listen I am not blown away like I was with previous albums but I have a feeling the album will grown on me ( and you). Make sure you hear this album! The Oslo Agreement will be released in Norway Aug 31st.

posts.title: Youths Dem Ball
posts.writeup: Hor!zen bring heavy heavy neck snapping dirty south dub from the lands of Florida. These guys are responsible for one of my all time personal fav reggae tracks 'The Life'. This EP is just a little taste of whats to come from this up and coming group that will be sure to make waves in the reggae/rock scene with their self proclaimed dirty south dub rock. Bottom Out will see a release date of Sept 18th.

posts.title: No Justice
posts.writeup: Colorado's P-Nuckle will be releasing "Stand Up And Bitch" September 18th and will see them build on a bigger fan base with this solid effort. The album has the backing of a well recorded/mixed album that clearly allows the listener to focus on the music. "Stand Up And Bitch" contains relevant subject matter in the lyrics behind the great instrumentation and dubbed out keys. Also its worth noting that the vocalist for P-Nuckle has a good voice for their style. Click the cover art to head over to their myspace.

posts.title: Lay Me Down ft. Rome
posts.writeup: The premier site for reggage/rock has officially released their first official compilation featuring a plethora of artists that represent this relatively new genre. Featuring unreleased songs from Dirty Heads, The Movement, John Brown's Body, The Black Seeds, B Foundation, Rome, Can't Hang, RAC, plus many many more. If you are a fan of the scene make sure to pick this up from their store HERE.

posts.title: Cruz
posts.writeup: Sabzi and Geo have taken another big step forward musically with OOF!. The EP covers many styles and sees the music taken to new levels with effortless skill. This EP will easily blaze up your headphones that will be worth telling your friends about. Hopefully these songs will inspire you to buy the album as I just did! Click HERE to get it.

posts.title: Top Of The World
posts.writeup: The guys from Louisiana will be releasing their first album (New Day) through Stoopid Records and 'Top Of The World' is our first taste of the album. Look for the album to hit stores Sept 26th! Top Of The World features a catchy beat with a dubious lead synth that fits great with the dub stabs of those keys. And the vocals seem to be EQ'd like a band pass filter, it trips me out, but it works!

posts.title: Today (Zero 7 Remix)
posts.writeup: For this remix The Five One have added a little insight of their own into the already solid song with a great verse that just pushes the song over the top to deserve a post. Check out Zero 7's music HERE. And click the cover art to get your self up to date on The Five One, click HERE to listen and download tracks from their last solid album, The Green Complex.

posts.title: Mary Collie Weed Ft Slightly Stoopid
posts.writeup: The original 'Bad Boys' of reggae music are back in 2009 to release the new album 'State Of The World' for a release date on August 25th. The album features a nice lineup of Damian Marley, Stephen Marley, Slightly Stoopid, Gramps Morgan (Morgan Heritage), Steel Pulse and more. Check for this album on your favortie online dealer on Aug 25th!

posts.title: People Of The Dust
posts.writeup: Botanics are a group from Sydney that you need to keep your ear on. I became a fan when I heard their solid debut album Release The Peace. The good news is the band is releasing a new album this year and People Of The Dust is the first single we get. Botanics are 2 emcees that flow over live drums, bass, keys and sick guitars. You can click the cover art to head over their myspace and check out the recently released track What The Deal Is. Check back later for a review of the new album!

posts.title: Necta (Butterfly)
posts.writeup: Sashamon's music is peacefully soothing and gives you the irie feelings of some Hawaii island flavor. This album started out as a demo but with word of mouth the album spread like wildfire in Hawaii. That was 2003 and in 2009 there is no new album, but the man is still doing his thing with the shows in Hawaii and around California. You can check him out this summer at the West Beach Festival in Santa Barbara HERE. And click HERE to get yourself a copy of 'One Day Maybe'

posts.title: Protagonist
posts.writeup: Yup its Murs and Slug back again with the third installment of the FELT series. They just leaked this track through myspace. Rumors be it that this is dedicated to Sarah Palin and Aesop Rock will be handling the production instead of Ant. Check back later for some solid info on this sure to be dope album.

posts.title: Redemption
posts.writeup: Diafrix bring a fresh style of truth in their music through their experiences of growing up in Africa. They now live in Melbourne and have just released their anticipated debut album that combine the roots of Africa with some singy hip-hop flair. You can get this album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: Gun Slinger
posts.writeup: The Crest make quality hip-hop that will find the ears of music lovers through word of mouth. That's what happens when you blow people away with ill rapping styles that hug the incredible production. Its good to see these guys still making good music and proves that having love for something will always produce positive things as it translates into another solid album Jack Cracker and AD can be proud of. Cheerful Robots features notables One Be Lo, Pigeon John, plus new comers (to my ears) Stephanie Rearick, Mr. Freeze and New MC. The scratching and production is handled by DJ Skrabble and Jayson Blare. Grab the album HERE.

posts.title: The Day I Died
posts.writeup: Its all about mashing up genre's these days and the man from the UK, Just Jack appeals to many tastes across the broad spectrum of musical universe. Whether it be the epic strings of 'Embers' that hits your chord or the happy ballad of 'The Day I Died' that is instantly recognizable as good song or the epic, roller coaster of an instrumental to end the album off with 'Basement'. His vocals border the lines of singing with tinges of rap flows, it comes off nicely and works well with his unique style of music. All Night Cinema will be on itunes France, Germany, Swiss, and Netherlands right now but won't hit UK itunes till August 31st. The sad thing is I have no idea if and when we be able to purchase this album in North America, but here is a great song to hold you over.

posts.title: Kings & Queens
posts.writeup: Its a very exciting time to be living these days if your a music lover because there is sooo much good music out there. Every corner of the world can hold pockets of amazing music that needs to be heard. Its pretty overwhelming and it makes my goal in life to get this music out a very daunting task. Soon one day the music industry will change for the good and you will be able to hear bands like this one from the Gold Coast of Australia on a daily basis through some sort of media. But for now I guess you can rely on places like this to keep you up to date. TCCC is a 7 piece band and I definitely digg this album and will assure you that spending 5 bucks on this EP is better than a over priced mocha frappuchino from starbucks. Get album on itunes HERE.

posts.title: White Daisy
posts.writeup: Orange Grove is a band that eminate rump shaking reggae groves that you can hear coming over the pond from The Netherlands. Its hard to pick a favorite song through my first 2 listens. These guys are lead from the strong lead vocals of Micheal Maidwell and backed by Roel Calister (drums), Touche' (bass), Danny Fuljhari (guitar) and Bruce Harms (guitar). This album screams out quality and should be not taken lightly! Click HERE to get the album via itunes and go HERE to listen to some songs from their first album. Their myspace says it perfectly "Musical goodness for the body and mind"

posts.title: New Girl
posts.writeup: Alpha Noise is 2 emcee's Jay Qwest and Kel with DJ/Producer Scizzahandz who hail from Atlanta. New Girl is the best example to give you guys a taste of what you can expect from these dudes. The song boasts a dope rhythm guitar with both emcees trading verses complimented by the soulful sample. Get the album via CDbaby HERE.

posts.title: Localize It
posts.writeup: New Zealand once again represents with some classic reggae from the debut album of newcomers Sweet & Irie. If your itching for some reggae/roots with that Marley rootsy feel then you need to get this album asap. Just from listening to these three songs I guarantee that whatever mood you were in before will change after a therapeutic listen through these songs! Click the cover art for their myspace to peep more songs. If you are in New Zealand hit up itunes for this album! I can't access the link for the album on itunes because its not on Canadian itunes but if your not from Canada just search for it!

posts.title: Good Times
posts.writeup: Dodger is a band with tons of potential from Toronto, Canada. I just got my digital hands on this album and the music is great but the recording is compressed so much that its brick walled. Other than the brutal compression on everything I dig what these guys have to offer. I would love to get these guys in the studio and re-record this! You can grab this album via itunes HERE.  Good Times is a great song to introduce you guys to that is not brick walled!.

posts.title: More Than Ever
posts.writeup: One of reggae/rock's fastest rising groups is back with the follow up to 2007's successful debut, Courage To Grow, with 12 brand new tracks to fill out the rest of your summer with some reggae jams. Bright Side Of Life picks up where Courage To Grow left us with the up beat dubs and the signature guitar chord progressions that is easy to enjoy. So when you head to the beach this summer make sure to have this album within ears reach of you and the sun. Look for the album in stores and digital outlets August 4th.

posts.title: Out Of Range
posts.writeup: Chris Velan is a singer/songwriter from Montreal, Quebec. His music is pretty laid back and its something that I could see being played on the radio. Never the less I do think its worthy of letting people know about his music so give the songs a listen! Grab the album from itunes HERE

posts.title: Coo?
posts.writeup: Great news from one of the best hip-hop acts in music right now is the news of the new EP 'OOF!' due August 25th and the full length due sometime in 2010. The guys recently left Rawkus to sign a better record deal with Duck Down Records and Cafe Vita, so a congrats goes out to them. They are giving 'Coo?' away for free download on their site just click HERE to sign up and get your copy of the track. This track will jump right up in my steady play of songs on the summer playlist as it is a pretty damn dope track!

posts.title: Bag O Tricks
posts.writeup: The Bredrin Daddy's will either dub you out with their infectious reggae or they will kick your face off with the intense metal. The album is pretty much spilt in half between the reggae and the hard metal, so if you got someone who is into the metal you could be pleasing yourself and someone else with different tastes at the same time. As you can tell from the songs I am posting I am a fan of the reggae/rock twist a bit more than the screaming. The Bredrin Daddy's hail from Long Beach, CA and contain 6 members. You can get this album on CDbaby HERE and itunes HERE.

posts.title: Back In
posts.writeup: Bridging the gap is an collaboration album between the golden era raps of Chubb Rock and the new school raps of Baltimore's Wordsmith. The beat on Back In boasts cadence on the keys with a melodic sample that will keep you listening untill the end. Get this album on itunes HERE.

posts.title: Anybody Else
posts.writeup: It has been a long 4 years between albums for Audible Mainframe who have made their way out to L.A via Boston a year ago. Transients is a refreshing upgrade to the memory that reminds me why I was so stoked on them back when I first heard Exposition's classic album 'The Metro'. Highlight tracks include the reggae influenced 'Ice Cold' that picks up during the chorus with the sweet guitar skanks that will get your neck bobbing and the smooth moving 'Anybody Else' that lets you know you are listening to some original music. Another standout that I don't have posted here is the up-lifiting track called 'Subi Alto' (Climb High) that boasts a choir of kids on chorus. The album tackles relevant topics from financial problems to the sad ways of the music industry, so don't worry about being bombarded with annoying rap ego's. Audible Mainframe is lead by the lead raps of MC Exposition and backed by the live instrumentation of Johnny (Drums), Lethal D (Guitar), Miller (Keys, Trumpet, MPC), Walt (Bass), and Special Blend on the 1's and 2's. Look for the album to hit digital outlets August 25th!

posts.title: Mirando
posts.writeup: Yea I know I am a tad bit late on this but I can't help but vibe out to this epic ride of a song. This track is best described as a composition of sounds that will blow your mind. This 12' was released awhile back but if you have not heard it then get to it! On the A side is the original and the Yacht Remix and on the B-side is the Animal Collective remix. You can get Ratatat's newest album LP3 from 2008 HERE.

posts.title: Wasting Time
posts.writeup: Inf is a crate digger/beat maker/emcee from Utrecht, Netherlands. You can get this album via itunes HERE. The Go Round is entirely just a instrumental album that can hold its own with out any vocals. Click the cover art for his myspace to listen to more tracks.

posts.title: Save Me
posts.writeup: The female representation in the reggae area is pretty scarce and its only a matter of time before lady musicians get in the act. I would love to hear Aimee collaborate with lots of reggae/rock bands, her voice is smooth and sultry and would light up any song with a verse or chorus. A Little Happiness will be released on July 21st, you can pre-order it HERE and you can get the lead single 'On Vacation' from itunes HERE.

posts.title: My Volvo (Produced by Ill Pill)
posts.writeup: You are about to listen to 2 very dope hip-hop tracks from an mc who will make your list for must listen in 2009. Because I must say these days even downloading new rapper albums is getting pretty brutal. So its good for the hip-hop heads to hear a dude like this. His rhymes come out clear and precise with relateable content that is not overblown with ego. The production on Smoke and Mirrors hits you with an captivating beat that instantly touches on your soul with its brilliance. Featured on the Chemistry EP is RA Scion of Common Market, Geologic of Blue Scholars, One Be Lo and Tunji. The best news of all is that you can download the whole thing for free HERE. Grych also has a full album you can get on itunes HERE.

posts.title: Things Change
posts.writeup: Josh Heinrichs is the former lead singer of the recently disbanded JahRoots band and this is his first solo venture. In Josh's case change is a good thing as this EP really shows up the soulness of his music allowing the listener to chill out to the irie vibes that Josh's voice exudes. You can get this EP exclusively on ThePier right now just click HERE. Keep on the lookout for the 'Reggae Favorites' album that Josh is doing with Cas Haley. Peep some of the studio bedtrack/overdub video's HERE. Reggae Favorites is slated for a late summer release so check back for a review and more info later!

posts.title: Water For Mars
posts.writeup: We can finally order the stellar new CYNE album with its special North American release with 3 new tracks that are not featured on the Japan retail. Usually its the other way around, so take that Japan! The album is being released on the Hometapes label and they are offering a special deal giving you the album + a t-shirt + a sticker for 25 bucks (includes postage). And its to be noted that you will get a link to download the album for those that can't wait. I can't stress enough about the quality of this album so please support! Take a listen to my favorite track off the album. Click HERE to order!

posts.title: Cease Fire ft. Dudu Stinks
posts.writeup: Natty Nation dropped a gem of a album back in 2008 that switches from roots/reggae to hard hitting hip-hop influenced tracks all throughout the album. Natty Nation currently resides in Madison, Wisconsin. You can grab this album HERE for 5 bucks!

posts.title: Too Damn Tough
posts.writeup: Souvenirs, Novelties and Party Tricks is The B Foundation's highly anticipated 3rd album that will hit stores and digital outlets July 7th! The steady hype built up over this album was not exaggerated to say the least. From a fan of the reggae/rock scene I am very pleased with what I can say is B Foundation's best work to date! You can order a hard copy from CDbaby HERE and you can get this album via itunes HERE.

posts.title: The Ropes
posts.writeup: You gotta love a group like Atmosphere who just drop a free EP with no warning at all that is better than most MC's albums. The music has been flowing effortlessly from Ant and Slug and from a fans point of view, it couldn't get any better. To get yourself a copy of this EP just simply click HERE and the EP to your cart and checkout!

posts.title: Young Girl
posts.writeup: Orange Grove is an reggae/roots band that first started out in 2001 in Sint Maarten (West Indies) but now are based out of Holland. Members include Micheal Maidwell (vox), Roel Calister (drums), Touche' (bass), Danny Fuljhari (guitar) and Bruce Harms (guitar). Their music evokes positive vibes and carribean breezes. This is their debut album that you can get HERE and they just released the new album 'Fingerprint' that you can get HERE. Look for a review on Fingerprint soon!

posts.title: True Jewels
posts.writeup: Hailing from San Antonio is the duo of Easy Lee and Tre that form up Mo'Joe. According to their website they took 7 years to craft this album! This album oozes with soul, dope raps and great instrumentation that lays a backdrop for these guys to get their unique brand of music out to the masses. You can get the album HERE and be on the lookout for the new album.

posts.title: The Getaway
posts.writeup: The Finnish MC Redrama is back with his follow up of Street Music after a 4 year hiatus. Everytime he drops an album it always catches me off guard so its always a pleasent surprise. On this new album you will hear more synthy beats, singy hooks and a tad bit of autotune. If you are familar with his past (really good) albums you can expect your expectations to be filled because this album will fit nicely with his previous work. Redrama also re-recorded 'Slow Down'. Get the album HERE.

posts.title: Taking Over Me
posts.writeup: Oregon's Rootdown are releasing a new EP in a timely fashion with the start of the summer that will mesh perfectly with your summer activities . Instantly you can feel the summer breeze hitting your face through the visuals on the cover (pictured above). Rootdown start off by grabbing your ears through the smooth vocals of lead singer Paul and finish you off with catchy hooks and breezy guitar riffs. Rootdown has all the right things going for them and hopefully we can see this band keep on pushing out that good music. Look for this must have EP on itunes within a week. Click the cover art to hit up their myspace

posts.title: Got Nuffin
posts.writeup: The guitars on this track captivate you through catchy riffs that get you hyped from a slow build up right to the end of the song. Combined with the great vocals from the lead singer Britt Daniel. Got Nuffin is the band's 6th EP, click HERE to get it.

posts.title: March Of The Caterpillar
posts.writeup: This is really quite a unique album, as soon as the album starts off you find yourself going to a musical place that is unfamiliar yet it will intrigue you to keep a keen ear on what you are listening too. In lehman's this is some good original music that people will find an affection for. Madala is the sixth full length album from the Bandits that is slated to hit stores July 28th. RX bandits are a 4 piece band from Seal Beach, California. Check for this on itunes and digital outlets July 28th. For now you can preview more tracks on their myspace, just click the cover art.

posts.title: Peace Of Mind
posts.writeup: The Dub Pistols are an London based band consisting of 6 members. By no means are they a new band on the scene but personally this is my first taste and its hard not to like a sound that these guys represent. I remember giving this album a listen when it first came out but slept on it and never gave it a re-listen but thanks to buddy who posted on the c-box to remind me! You can get this album here and previous albums HERE.

posts.title: Rightful Place
posts.writeup: Uwe Banton is an reggae artist from Bielefeld, Germany and this is his second album. If you are down with that classic roots reggae sound your going to digg this guy's music. So you might as well get the whole album on itunes HERE.

posts.title: Ridiculous
posts.writeup: DJ Js-1 is back again with another compilation album full of a who's who in hip-hop. The album boasts a hefty guest list full of some long awaited pairings. For starters there is a track with CL Smooth and Brother Ali paired together and a dream team lineup of Akrobatik, J-Live, Supastition and Pack FM all on one song. My favorite track ended up being the combo of O.C (D.I.T.C) and Pharoah Monche over a classic hiphop vibe brought to you by Js-1. Check out accesshiphop's page to see the guestlist I don't want to spend 20 minutes typing their names out, oh and you can buy this album HERE.

posts.title: Do You Remember
posts.writeup: I remember originally hearing this song back in '05 on Last Emp's Hidden Treasures. But the song was cut short and skipped at the end. I finally got my hands on the full version and I think you guys should hear this classic song. If you like the song click the pic to check out his myspace page and info to get his music.

posts.title: 4 Be Be Ft Ming Xia
posts.writeup: The man that everyone knows who it is when they hear the voice is finally dropping the long delayed solo album to be released July 7th on Decon Records. This album sprung a early leak today so I will pretend I was given out the promo copy for a review. After a in-depth listen through the whole album on my way to school today I was kind of disappointed. I did have high hopes after hearing the first sickness of the DJ Nu-Mark assisted 'Comin' Thru' But I guess I was looking for Chali over some feel good hip-hop tracks. This is all coming from a place of high expectations so don't get it twisted that this album is not worth your money. Highlight tracks like 'When Will I See You Again' boast Chali and Elzhi trading verses over a mellow rnb vibe. And on another standout is the beautiful voice of Ming Xia from the legendary SPOOKS lending out her vocals in assisting Chali on a heartfelt track backed by a melodic guitar about the death of a friend and that 2000 bus crash. Fish Outta Water features the likes of Damien Marley, Stephen Marley, Elzhi, Talib Kweli, Anthony Hamilton, Ming Xia (Spooks) + many more. And on the production tip is Jake One, DJ Nu-mark, Scott Storch and Vitamin D.

posts.title: Roq Roq
posts.writeup: Times Neue Roman is a group on a steady rise from Toronto who bring a dope style to the masses through self proclaimed nintendo raps. This is the track in my opinion that will be turning heads and making people take TNR very seriously. I mean go ahead and listen to this track, I guarantee you will play it again right after your first listen. Its hella catchy, 'look it way, look it the way, look it the way, that I rock rock..... Can you picture yourself letting loose to this track if you heard it in a club? I know I can and I barely even hit the clubs anymore. 'Roq Roq' will be featured on the soon to be released EA Sports game 'Fight Night 4' due to hit stores June 30th. You can also grab this single via itunes (June 16th) and you can also grab their debut EP HERE. Click the pic for more info and more tracks to listen to!

posts.title: One Day
posts.writeup: This is the heartfelt first single from Matisyahu's highly anticipated album 'Light' due to hit stores and digital outlets August 25th. You can grab this single on itunes June 16th! Sorry no download link for this one.

posts.title: Ready For Whatever
posts.writeup: Portland's own Sapient has just released a new solo album that you can grab on itunes HERE. If you are familiar with his work you can expect more of his signature over-driven/dirty synth'd out beats that weave perfectly with his rhyming style. Rumor has it that Sapient is supposed to drop a album full of him on the singing tip, one can only hope that album drops! Keep your ears open for that one.

posts.title: O The Night
posts.writeup: This is the debut EP from the promising group out of Melbourne. I instantly became a fan of Sybiosis in 2008 when I first heard "Rise Up" from their self-titled second album. If you have not heard that amazing song then go HERE and listen up! You can grab this EP via itunes. According to the bands myspace they will be taking some time apart from band activites for 2009. I can only hope this band makes some more music in the future. If they do you, you can bet I will be all over it.

posts.title: The Harvest
posts.writeup: The Tribal Seeds really produced a great album with their 3rd release, 'The Harvest' Its an album that blends well and contains many reggae jams that are easy to please. Grab this quality album from itunes HERE.

posts.title: 10 Ft Ganja Plant And Weed Ft Kyle McDonald
posts.writeup: On July 28th we will be blessed with the newest release from one of my favorite roots/reggae acts. Bushrock stays true to the classic 70's roots sound that has been prevelant on their past albums. Featured on one track is the infectious vocals of Kyle from Slightly Stoopid. Bushrock is a must have for any reggae fan! So when the 28th rolls around, you know what to do.

posts.title: We Fear Not
posts.writeup: Black Canvas is Mr. Melody, Rider Shafique, and King Chubby J who all hail from Gloucester, England. I don't see the album for sale yet on itunes but you can get the EP that features this track plus remixes HERE. Click the pic to hear more tracks and find out more info.

posts.title: Flow ft. Mr Woodnote & Flower Fairy
posts.writeup: Dub Fx is not your ordinary one man band to say the least. Originally starting out as a street performer who would tour all over the world showcasing his beat boxing talents (check out his youtube vids). He creates all his music by looping his voice/beat boxing through a series of EFX pedals. Then of course he adds tastefull amounts of reverb and delay to suffice his style. Another cool thing he does is when he switches his voices between his deep 'Moonsoon' voice to the higher pitch 'Lyric' voice by setting up a pitch shifter on a foot pedal. I would stop dead in my tracks if I saw this guy performing on the street. This is his sophmore album that you can get directly from him HERE. This album is SICK!!

posts.title: Rude World
posts.writeup: The Red Eyes are a 10 piece band from the music epi-center that is Melbourne, Australia. Lead vocalist Yoda sets a mellow tone with his smooth voice over the classic roots/dub sound that The Red Eyes represent. Pick up the album HERE.

posts.title: Kutsat
posts.writeup: FSB aka Formation Studio Balkantone aka Free Sailing Band are a band from Sofia, Bulgaria who first started out in 1976. There is little to none info on these guys on the net, its crazy. The only reason I heard about this track is because a reader hit me up with the suggestion. Thanks!

posts.title: Relax
posts.writeup: The guitar work all over this album captivates you, leaving you in full attention from track to track. From the myspace bio I have learned that this is their 5th album...For me this is my virgin listen to POT (oh shit I just realized that haha) and I must say I am impressed. This is the type of album you have to study for awhile to figure out your favorites. That is usually a positive. You can pick up this worthy album on itunes HERE.

posts.title: No Foolin Me
posts.writeup: Fatty Down is an emcee from Vancouver, BC and his newest offering, Soup, is now available for free download via his myspace page (just click the cover art). Peep my favorite track off the album with the sweet acoustic guitar.

posts.title: We Are To Answer
posts.writeup: One of the best things about music is that out of nowhere dope releases just fall into your lap. Case and point with this solid debut effort from these 2 producers who currently reside in Cologne, Germany. Ancient Astronauts are Kabanjak and Dogu. The album features The Pharcyde, Raashan Ahmad (Crown City Rockers), Tippie Irie + many more. Check out the highlight of the album with Raashan's joyful mastery. We Are To Answer will be released June 9th on ESL music. Pick up the album HERE.

posts.title: Lies
posts.writeup: Hailing from the haven for reggae/rock (Santa Cruz, CA) , is the 4 piece band Ribsy's Nickel. I am digging this album and I'm sure most of you will too. The album is currently out of stock from CDbaby right now. So here are 2 songs to hold you over. I suggest hitting up the band and see if you can buy the album directly from them (If its still in print).

posts.title: The King Had A Dream
posts.writeup: This track is a heart felt dedication song to Obama's Inauguration. I love the song, but I have to disagree with his faith in Obama. The message is good but I like to judge someone on their actions over their words.

posts.title: I Dont Wanna Wait
posts.writeup: I know that has been out for awhile now. The way I look at is that the die hard fans (like me) have already bought this. So this is for the new fans who are not familiar with one of the best reggae groups doing it. Look for the new album to drop this Summer! You can grab this single on itunes HERE.

posts.title: Young and Able
posts.writeup: This is Current Swell's new album that has been available at their shows since January. If you were lucky enough to see them, you probally already have this album. The band is working out the kinks to get this album on itunes at the moment. But to hold you over check out this highlight track.

posts.title: Comin Thru
posts.writeup: Chali 2na from the legendary Jurrasic 5 is finally dropping a solo album! This is great news and is sure to be a great album. Chali has always been a fan favorite with his signature voice and confident flows. You can grab the 'Lock Shit Down EP' right now on itunes that features Talib Kweli, a unreleased track and the acapella/instrumental of the title track. Click HERE to buy the EP. And look for the new album (Fish Outta Water) to hit stores and digital outlets July 7th.

posts.title: Suicide King Ft Pigeon John
posts.writeup: Its been a long 4 years since the last CREST album. But this summer the guys from Madison, Wisconsin will follow up 2005's Skeptik. Personally I am happy to hear this because I have not heard much from these guys in awhile. The new album 'Cheerful Robots' will be released July 7th! Keep posted for more info for an album you will need to buy.

posts.title: The Dapper Mob
posts.writeup: Are you sure you a ready for a song like this? The Dapper Mob is a elegant track in ways that please me with every twist and turn this track takes me on. Once again its Sapient on the production and singing vocals. This track may take a few listens for it to sink in. The instrumental outro at 4:14 is musically brilliant and had my head bobbin' in amazement. Long Story Short is out on itunes right now, click HERE to get it. Saaaaaaaaaaaandpeople....

posts.title: I Play My Kazoo
posts.writeup: Grand Analog give us a new album that is full of grimey guitar riffs complimented by ear pleasing "baritone" raps from Odario. 'Metropolis Is Burning' will be available in stores and digital outlets May 26th.

posts.title: Wake Up
posts.writeup: One Drop is a reggae band from San Diego consisting of James Alger (Vocals, Guitar), Bart Negalia (Guitar, Back Up Vocals), Art Gener (Bass) and Jim Hathaway on the drums.You can pick up this EP and the new full length via CDbaby HERE.

posts.title: On A Roll
posts.writeup: This EP was recorded as a promo release for High Tide's 2007 album 'Play Me Reggae'. Ed B's vocals are very smooth over the stripped down sound of the acoustic guitar and percussion drums. I find this EP very easy to enjoy. You can grab this EP on CDbaby for 5 bucks HERE. Look for a new acoustic Ep to be released very soon.

posts.title: Hell No Way
posts.writeup: Santanero is a 8 piece band from Utrecht, Netherlands. You can grab the debut album for 8 bucks from CDbaby HERE.

posts.title: I Came Running
posts.writeup: The Ancient Astronauts are Kabanjak and Dogu from Cologne, Germany. The album drops June 9th on ESL music. Featured on this album is Raashan Ahmad (Crown City Rockers), The Pharcyde, Tippa Irie, Azeem + more. Check back later for a review on this promising album.

posts.title: Open Up Their Eyes ft. Jeru The Damaja
posts.writeup: This is the first producer I have ever heard out of Italia, but it does not come as a surprise. Fabio Musta has been in the Italian hip-hop scene since the early 90's and now has a record deal with the indie powerhouse Babygrande. Passport has an pretty dope array of featured emcees from USA to the local Italian artists. Featured on this album is Akrobatik, Termanology, Slum Village, Strong Arm Steady, Substantial, Elias, Reks + many many more. Pick up the album on itunes HERE.

posts.title: No More Weapons
posts.writeup: I have never really checked for Steel Pulse but after this album I feel pretty dumb for not. Steel Pulse is the biggest Reggae outfit out of the UK. I'm sure they don't need a introduction. So please don't hate on me for never hearing any Steel Pulse. Hit up itunes for this album HERE.

posts.title: Old School Vibe
posts.writeup: Hailing from Detroit, Michigan are The Regiment consisting of 2 emcee's IseQold, OSI and the DJ, The Arch-E-Tect . The hard hitting beats lay the backdrop for these guys to bring that classic boom bap hip-hop that everyone loves. Hit up CDbaby right now to buy the Mp3's just click the album art.

posts.title: Whats Real ft. Emanon
posts.writeup: Jazz Lib are already back at it with the follow up to last years impressive 'Clin d'oeill' This album is not like Clin d'oeill in the fact that this album contains mostly instrumentals. The emcee's featured are Mos Def, Aloe Blacc (Eamanon), Declaime, Fat Lip, Dela + more. You can grab the new album from itunes right HERE.

posts.title: Mellow Stages
posts.writeup: Check out these guys from Melbourne, Australia. The sad thing is this band just recently broke up. Its too bad because their music is good. You can get this album on itunes right now.

posts.title: Sunshine
posts.writeup: Tahuna Breaks is another great band from New Zealand. My favorite aspect of their music that catches my ear right away is the overall feeling you get from the instrumentation. Each track on Reflections has a different clever arrangement of skanks and tasty break downs. Its just simply easy to say that this band is good and you should probally make the effort to hear this album. Tahuna Breaks will be releasing a brand new album 'Black, Brown and White' that is due to hit us in August. So as always check back later for that. You can get 'Reflections via itunes right now.

posts.title: Chase That Feeling
posts.writeup: This is the first single from the new Hilltop Hoods album 'State of The Art' due to hit stores and digital outlets June 12th. The first single boasts a nice piano loop with the backing string section courtesy of the Adelaide Symphony. The track I am posting here is of lower quality, I would love to buy the track off of itunes but you can only purchase it from the Aussie itunes store. Another reason to hate on itunes. BOOOO!

posts.title: Pretty Apollo
posts.writeup: I have always had an affliction for Cyne's music ever since I first heard their music back in '03. In my eyes they have dropped two classic abums in 'Time Being' and 'Evolution Flight'. Their last 2 albums (Starship Utopia and Pretty Dark Things) did not hit the same for me, but with the new album, 'Water For Mars' , Cyne is back to their classic form. This album is going to go down as one of the best of the year for me. This album is thick with substance, quality beats and dope rhymes. Check back when I find out when we can buy this over here. This album is out in Japan right now on P-Vine records. For fans of Panacea, Cunninlynguists, Nujabes.

posts.title: Outro
posts.writeup: Reggae vibes, heavy guitar solo's and good vocals is what your going to get with Audiodub's new album. I love the build up of the 'Save Your Soul' track, its easily my favorite track off the album. After that track check out the sick guitars on the 'Outro'. To hear the rest of this album make sure to pick it up May 16th on itunes and all other digital retailers.

posts.title: Cry Cry Cry ft. Jack Johnson & Paula Figa
posts.writeup: Ziggy is back with a new album that is directed towards the little ones. Its a happy album full of ballads and songs that are not just for the kids. I am a big fan of anything Marley related and its hard not to have a soft spot for this album. It makes me wanna hit up the park with my kids (if I had kids) and just play around. This album is on itunes right now, it features Jack Johnson, Willie Nelson, Judah Marley, Rita Marley, plus many more. Go check it out!

posts.title: High Tide
posts.writeup: Thanks to one of our beloved readers for passing on the tip to check these guys out! Still Time is lead by the strong vocals of Dan and backed by Paul on the bass, John on the drums and Haircut on the guitar. You can grab this album on itunes right now. And if your just tuning into these guys (like me) you will be glad to know that they are releasing a brand new album that you can pre-order HERE. So get your ears out and grab these 2 albums.

posts.title: Love Is Endless
posts.writeup: Here goes some easy going funky vibes from The Lunar Funk Theory who currently reside in Sioux Falls, South Dakota. Click HERE to buy the album from CDbaby and click the pic to hear more songs from them!

posts.title: Illusions
posts.writeup: High Ceiling is a 4 piece band from Shelton, WA. These guys have been making music since 2004, so this is their second album. You can get this album and their first album 'Angle Of Response' from CDbaby, just click HERE.

posts.title: Drum & Bass
posts.writeup: This is the first album from Flox released back in '06. After hearing the new album I had to go check out this one. Drum and Bass is easily my favorite tune off of this. You can get the rest of this album and the new album 'The Words' on itunes right now.

posts.title: Dont Stop The Record
posts.writeup: This man has been blowing up my speakers for the last few months with his verses on the new Clear Conscience album and the work in '08 he did with Close Enough. Now its time for NoNeedz to rep a new solo album with 13 new tracks. I was originally introduced to NoNeedz music through his vocals in the Close Enough band, and I must say I really miss the reggae/rock mixed in with his music. But after hearing this album it's easy to see that Noneedz can excel with his music in any element. His subject matter is very down to earth and its something I can personally relate to. This album is NoNeedz best solo album to date and Its worth the 10 bucks to pre-order. Just click the Buy Now below (all you need is a visa). The album will be shipped out in June, click the pic for his myspace.

posts.title: The Words
posts.writeup: Here is some headnodic reggae/dub music from Flox, who hails from France. Check for this on itunes right now.

posts.title: King Tunders
posts.writeup: As you can see I have been doing my homework on the Sandpeople roster. Sapient is quickly becoming one of my favorites from the group but not from his raps but the tracks where he sings. I am not saying he's not a good emcee but I really enjoy Sapient the most when its just him, a acoustic guitar and his production. All my favorite Sandpeople tracks are from Sapient in his singing style. They always put the Sapient singing track at the end, maybe they are self conscious about it? Maybe I have wierd taste, but whatever it is I would love to hear more. Listen to my favorite Sapient sangin tracks 'Gladys' and 'Synthetique Princess' You can get this album HERE.

posts.title: Whatever ft. Sapient
posts.writeup: Its hard to scroll through a album like this one because every beat grabs your attention and your forced to listen to each song carefully. Expect to give this a in-depth listen, this is not your average insta-delete album. Immaculate is from the Sandpeople, who have been pushing out some good music from all members lately. You can preview the album via CdBaby and purchase it there.

posts.title: Never Let Go
posts.writeup: This is a compilation album produced and recorded by Jesus Wore Dickies. On this album is Half Pint, Slightly Stoopid, Toko Tasi, Jack Maness, plus many more reggae/rock bands. Now go ahead and listen to one of the best voices.

posts.title: Cant Breath (Hermitude Remix)
posts.writeup: Elephant Traks is a record label in Australia that is home to some of the best artists/groups in aussie hiphop. Included in the roster is Urthboy, Ozi Batla, Hermitude, Chasm, The Herd, Astronomy Class and many more. Check out their site HERE. This album is full of remixes of past songs from the likes of Hermitude, Unkle Ho, Plutonic Lab, CountBounce (TZU) and more. Now that I am on the topic of Elephant Traks, you can be on the lookout for a new Urthboy album Spitshine and a new Astronomy Class album Pursuit Of Happiness' due May 2nd.

posts.title: Money Is On Their Minds
posts.writeup: The 10 deep collective Sandpeople are releasing a new EP "Long Story Short" on May 26th. These guys have been on my radar for awhile now so I expect the EP to be stellar.

posts.title: Ark of the Covenant
posts.writeup: Corey Harris is a reggae musician who currently resides in Virginia. This is his last album that he released, check out his discography here. Click the cover art for his myspace.

posts.title: First Mate
posts.writeup: This is my first taste of RAC and it does not disappoint! RAC is a 4 piece band from Glendale, Arizona. I just bought this off of itunes and so should you. Just listen to 'First Mate' and you will be forced to buy it.

posts.title: Us Against The Crown
posts.writeup: I am not sure how well known these guys are due to just coming across them in '09. State Radio is lead by the former singer of Dispatch (Chad Urmstom). State Radio has quite the discography dating back to their debut EP Flag Of The Shiners from 2002. You can bet its worth to take the time to check these guys out. Click the pic for the myspace and links to get your hands on their music. For fans of Three Legged Fox, John Butler Trio, The Beautiful Girls.

posts.title: Persistence ft. Bliss N Eso
posts.writeup: Its about that time for some good new aussie hiphop. This time around its Phrase from Melbourne with a album that boasts good production with quality vocals. If you live in Australia you can pre-order this album HERE, it will be released on itunes April 24th. Clockwork features Bliss N Eso, Jackson Jackson, Wendy Matthews, and Daniel Merriweather.

posts.title: Let The Music Play
posts.writeup: Top Shelf hails from the Northern California Area consisting of Carter Lane (Vocals), Drew Nicoll (Drums), Chad Goodlin (Bass), and Sean Erickson (Guitar). This is my first time hearing Top Shelf and I am happy with the end result of this album. I especially like Carter's vocals. You can grab this impressive debut album from CDbaby HERE and click the cover art to hit up their myspace.

posts.title: Growing Up On The Road ft Cosmic
posts.writeup: The always on point Swedish emcee Promoe is back with his monthly release. April's track gives us another beat produced by Astma & Rockwell. If you happen to be a reader from Sweden you can pre-order his new album HERE. The album is only in Swedish, but its ok for us English folk because every month Promoe drops heat, in English!

posts.title: Reggae Avenue
posts.writeup: Yea I know that is a lot of descriptions for the genre but its needed to describe Mojo Morgan's music. This is Mojo Morgan's first solo album branching out from his band, Morgan Heritage. This album hits digital outlets April 7th.

posts.title: New Life
posts.writeup: Here are some sweet reggae tunes for a hazy Sunday afternoon. Black Ghandi is from Barcelona, Spain. They have songs in English and Español. Get this album right now from itunes.

posts.title: Playground
posts.writeup: Its always good to hear original music that combine all different genres of music into a melting pot of free flowing creativity. You can pick up this album via itunes, just click HERE. Click the appealing cover art to peep some more songs from their myspace.

posts.title: Friday
posts.writeup: Representing Springfield, Missouri is the 3 piece band 'The Seed'. This is their debut album and you can get it now from CDbaby for 8 bucks. Like I always say at the end of the posts if your a fan of The Beautiful Girls your going to like this album plain and simple. The album is a easy listen, great for a lazy beach day. Check out their myspace page HERE.

posts.title: Bad Luck
posts.writeup: This limited edition CD showcases Mr. Pinto in a stripped down element with his acoustic guitar. You will find acoustic versions of past favorites such as Bill's Song, Temptation, Backburner and many more goodies. You can pre-order the album via CDbaby, just click HERE.

posts.title: Remember ft. Toko Tasi
posts.writeup: I have always admired the Japanese music scene for how big it is for such a small land mass. And the fact that you are usually big in Japan before anywhere else. Japan has a big reputation for loving music, there is a huge following for jazzy hip-hop and most of it never gets released over here. So thank the world for the internet. (Note to big brother: If you ever shut the internet down, LOOK OUT!) But yea anyways recently I found out (thanks Pier.org!) there was a following of reggae/rock forming in Japan with new bands and fans alike popping up. So the guys at One Big Family Records were nice enough to reach out and give us the album. On this compilation is artists from Long Beach (Toko Tasi, Phileano, Jack Maness, LBDA) Live@subs from Australia and Japan bands (Seven Beach Dub Taste, Pampas Field Ass Kickers, Sunset Bus). The compilation is very diverse and gives you a taste of whats to come for the Japanese reggae/rock scene. So the next time your in Japan go buy this and say whatup to Satoshi for me!

posts.title: Sensi Time
posts.writeup: Clear Conscience is coming out with a brand new album that you can pre-order right now and get a bonus track that will NOT be on the itunes version. Simply click the cover art to hit up their myspace to pre-order. On The Rocks features Half Pint, Daddy Scotty (Chapter 11), NoNeedz, and Giraffe Aftermath. Don't miss out on this dope album!

posts.title: Big Loud (Instrumental)
posts.writeup: Its beats like this one that make you wish an emcee was rapping over this beat. Mr. Green's production is hard hitting and I love it. Peep his amazing skills with the chop during the bridge . For the past month Mr. Green has been selling beats for 200$ a piece. His myspace says the deal ends April 10th, so get at him asap if you need beats. I'm pretty sure if I was a rapper I would be buying his beats. Click the pic for his myspace.

posts.title: Hey Now Ft Project Move
posts.writeup: Soulution is an beat maker from the Netherlands and this is his debut album. Shine Through has a good selection of emcee's like Supastition, Talib Kweli, Asheru, Project Move and many more. The track I am posting here is actually a remix of the original version from Project Move's 2005's Love Gone Wrong:The Butterfly Theory.

posts.title: Under Da Sun
posts.writeup: Here is another band worth checking out from Huntington Beach, CA. This nice EP is for sale on CDbaby and itunes right now. Look for a double disc new album slated to drop at the end of the summer. Check back for a review on that and for now go pick this up for 5 bucks. Click the pic for Zutra's myspace page.

posts.title: One Day Ft Ky-Mani Marley
posts.writeup: I am a big Ky-mani fan and its hard to pass up on this track, so don't be fooled by the album cover.

posts.title: Documentarian (Prod Exile)
posts.writeup: Blame One is an emcee from the San Diego area. Blame said himself that this album is his best work to date and he was right! You can get this on itunes or get a hard copy HERE. Days Chasing Days is a concept album ".....about us all forming routines and patterns in our life and trying to go outside of that to accomplish something near to our own hearts" The album features production from Black Milk, Exile, Blu, Kankick. And on the vocal tip is Sean Price, Blu, and Aloe Blacc. Oh and Blame One, if your reading this (in regards to your lyrics in Blame Me), understand that good music like yours WILL be heard. The industry is changing and its my goal to start that change with this blog.

posts.title: Burning Bridges
posts.writeup: The anticipated follow up to 2006's Atlantis:Hymns For Disco is the brand new album Yes! Its available right NOW on itunes so go get it. This album is good from front to back, I am definitely stoked to have this on the play list for the next month!

posts.title: Ill City Breezy Snippet feat. Strange Fruit Project.
posts.writeup: Japan once again represents with the jazz infused hip-hop production that they are the best known for. I can't find much info on this album but I can tell you that it features CL Smooth, Strange Fruit Project, and Pase Rock.

posts.title: All In ft Pigeon John & Gift Of Gab
posts.writeup: This track is a great start to the new G&E album 'Say G&E' due to hit stores and digital outlets April 7th. This album is shaping up to be a banger!

posts.title: Chronic Song
posts.writeup: Well its pretty clear that after one listen through Stay Gold that this is easily my favorite reggae/rock album of the year so far. This album prompts you to realize how good the reggae/rock scene has been of late. With groups like the Holdup and The Movement, the future is looking pretty good for the budding scene (you know what scene I'm talking about). Just listen to Chronic Song and BOOM your a fan of The Holdup, yes its that easy. Grab the digtal album off of CDbaby right now for 10 bucks.

posts.title: Hypnotized Ft Pack FM, Club Dub
posts.writeup: Yes its good, of course it is. Cunninlynguists represent 100% quality every time they release anything. If you haven't pre-ordered yet your going to have to wait for the digital release on April 14th. This is Vol 1 of the Strange Journey albums that Cunninlynguists will be releasing with Vol 2 due to be released in Fall of 2009. Strange Journey Vol 1. features Suffa and Pressure (Hilltop Hoods), Promoe and Supreme (Looptroop Rockers), Slug (Atmosphere), Inverse, Substantial, Pack FM, Tonedeff, Nappy Roots and more.

posts.title: Lets Just Be Friends Ft Tuomo
posts.writeup: The DJ/Producer/Emcee from the Bay Area is back with his new album 'Early Believers' due out April 7th. Early Believers is a easy flowing album with jazzy beats and smooth vocals that will ease out your mind. The albums features the likes of Ohmega Watts, Ben Westbeech and the 'Stevie Wonder of Finland' Tuomo.

posts.title: It Don't Move Me
posts.writeup: PBJ are back with their newest offering dropping this Tuesday. After a quick listen through, Lay It Down is still my favorite track off of the new album. Nevertheless Its still a solid album so I suggest you pick it up.

posts.title: With A Little Help From My Friends Ft Luciano
posts.writeup: The guys that brought you the very dope Dub Side Of The Moon (2003) and Radiodread (2005) are back putting the reggae twist on the classic Beatles album. Guest vocals on this project include Matisyahu, Steel Pulse, Luciano, Micheal Rose (Black Uhuru) and many more. Look for this album to hit itunes April 14th!

posts.title: Spread Love
posts.writeup: Here goes some more flavor from New Zealand. Rhombus is a very diverse group that brings the hiphop element crossed with some soul, electronica and that good ol rub a dub style. Rhombus consists of 2 producers (Simon Rycroft and Thomas Voyce) and MC Imon Star. This is Rhombus' second album, also released is 'Bass Player' from 2002 and the self titled album just recently released last year. Click the cover art to check out their myspace and info to purchase their albums!

posts.title: Camel Song
posts.writeup: Medicine man is the newest album from the New Zealand based group dDub.

posts.title: Magnolia
posts.writeup: Sometimes I stare blankly at my screen and wonder if I have sucked the internet dry of good music. That thought is always a short lived because somehow the good music just seems to show itself when you least expect it. Take for example this hiphop group from Sydney who bring a original sound with the live instrumentation to make some really dope music. These guys are soon to release a new album this year so untill that drops I will be having this album to listen over. Check back later for a review on their album.

posts.title: Darling This Scene Must End
posts.writeup: The voice behind The Beautiful Girls released a solo album back in '08 for those that missed it. I had a crappy version posted of 'Darling This Scene Must End' last year but I feel the need to show you guys a good version of the best song off this album. You can get this album on itunes right now.

posts.title: Electric City Ft Shad
posts.writeup: This is the first released track (courtesy of URBNET records) taken from Grand Analog's new album, 'Metropolis Burning', slated for release on May 26th. Grand Analog is Odario on the vocals, DJ Ofield on the 1's and 2's, Catalist on the keys, Warren Bray on the bass, and Damon Mitchell on the guitar. With a solid track like this I will be looking forward to hearing this album. Check back later for a review on this album. For now click the pic to check out Grand Analog's myspace.

posts.title: Right There (Ruff Mix)
posts.writeup: This was just leaked via The Pier. Look for a acoustic album, The Rooted, from High Tide with 9 tracks later this year. Click the cover art to check out more tracks off of High Tide's latest album.

posts.title: Help Im Alive
posts.writeup: The more I get sick of rappers the more I start to dab into other genres. A couple years ago I would of never seen myself liking some Metric. Pack FM is right. This album hits itunes April 14th.

posts.title: Junior
posts.writeup: Can't say I have heard of Royksopp before but as soon as I heard this beat It was destined for a post. These guys are from Bergen, Norway. I'm pretty sure these guys are going to regret that album cover in a few years. This album is on itunes right now.

posts.title: Jah Gringo
posts.writeup: Just a couple of days ago I posted the new acoustic album from the German reggae singer. This was his first album from 2006 that sees him in a better element than the acoustic album in my opinion. I hate to make the comparison in voices but he sounds like Akon without the autotune. I can see why some people would not like his voice but I like it so here it is.

posts.title: Escaping Arkham
posts.writeup: I had no idea that one of my favorite hip hop acts had a new EP out!. This EP has 5 new tracks with the last track being a very very sick Sabzi instrumental. You can preview the EP HERE and once you hear it you will want to go cop it on itunes ASAP. Once again RA and Sabzi bring high quality hiphop for the masses.

posts.title: Embers
posts.writeup: This is the first single from the UK artists new album 'All Night Cinema' due March 30th. I really like the strings in this track along with the overlapping of his vocals.

posts.title: Little Closer (Don Krutscho Mix)
posts.writeup: Martin is a singer/songwriter from Berlin, Germany. I have not heard of Jondo's previous albums but judging from this smooth acoustic album I will be looking to get a hold of his discography. Click the cover art to check out more songs from Martin Jondo. This album is on itunes now.

posts.title: On My Own
posts.writeup: Josh Martinez and Sleep are back to follow up 2006's When Pigs Fly with 7 new songs. You can order the album HERE.

posts.title: Alarms
posts.writeup: Its a rarity when a band hits you up and wants you to check out their stuff and it is actually not terrible. So its good when you finally get a band that is worth checking out. The Pilots are a 5 piece band from South London, they have no official album out but you can check out more tracks on their myspace by clicking the pic.

posts.title: Vibe
posts.writeup: JWATP is a 4 piece band Based out of Minneapolis. You can get the album right now for 10 bucks on CDbaby. This is a pretty good debut album if you ask me and will put these guys on my map. Click the cover art for the bands myspace and a show near you.

posts.title: Woah
posts.writeup: Scarub (Living Legends) and Very aka Afro Classics are back to follow up 2001's self titled album. To be honest I can't even remember what the last album sounded like, but judging from the tracks off of this EP I will be giving it a re-listen. The Beats are instant ear grabbers, Scarub's flow is superb and Very easily holds his own. Look for this to hit itunes March 10th.

posts.title: Real As Can Be
posts.writeup: Ali is one of the best emcees doing it right now and this is brand new EP due this tuesday. The EP features Nine tracks with 2 rare b-sides and one track with Slug. You can get the album on itunes this tuesday or pre-order HERE.

posts.title: Safe Ft Slug and Pigeon John
posts.writeup: Eligh and His Mother (Jo Wilkison) give us a mother/son/hiphop/folk album that does a great job in crossing the two genres. I think its really cool that they did this album together, it makes me want to do a album with my mom, haha. The album features The Grouch, Pigeon John, and Slug of Atmosphere. Hit up itunes on March 10th or if you prefer a hard copy go HERE.

posts.title: The Sky Might Fall
posts.writeup: I have seen Kid Cudi all over the blogs for the last few months but have been reluctant to check him out due to the overflow of so much crap out there. But I heard this track on the new Transformers trailer and liked what I heard. Kanye is back in his 808 style production, that snare sounds pretty dope if you ask me. Cudi's anticipated album ' Man on The Moon: The Guardians' is due this summer.

posts.title: Good Times
posts.writeup: Diego Roots is Chris Mcconnaughey (vocals), Ryan Kelley (guitar), Josiah Panella (drums), and Brandon Jensen (bass). These guys rep a chilled out sound for the under the influence listener.

posts.title: 70s, 80s
posts.writeup: Nightmares On Wax is a DJ/Musician, George Evelyn, also known as DJ E.A.S.E from the UK. At 1:25 this song just kills it.

posts.title: Send A Message
posts.writeup: This cd is a collection of reggae artists from all parts of the world. I can't seem to find any info on this compilation due to the fact that any info on this is only in french, so I am assuming this was released in France. This album is packed with some really good artists like The Black Seeds, Natty, Katchafire, Easy Star All Stars....Although the tracks that are featured on this are not new. Except I have never heard most of it, for example this Black Seeds song I have never heard. And same goes for the 340ml who are a very cool sounding band from Mozambique, Africa. 340ml only have one album out from 2004 called 'Moving', you can bet I will be on the lookout for it. Check back later for more on 310ml.

posts.title: Wait And See
posts.writeup: This EP in my opinion is a step in the right direction for Iration. With this EP they are starting to define their own sound which is a must in a genre where the music can sound very similar. Iration have been laying down beds for the last month or so with overdubs and mixing to go, I would expect the album to drop around summertime. This EP is available right now on itunes. I love that rythm guitar on 'Wait and See'.

posts.title: Evil Doers Beware
posts.writeup: Edu Leedz is a well known promoter from Boston and this is his compilation that he put together. It features Exposition, Pacewon, Casual, Last Emperor, El Da Sensei, Ill Bill, and more. The song I am posting here is a nice track with Expo and Last Emp trading verses.

posts.title: Time
posts.writeup: Sabotage Soundsystem is Kaleo and Dub from the legendary reggae/rock group Pepper. You can get this album on itunes right now!

posts.title: Pick Up Yourself
posts.writeup: Add these guys to your list of ever growing good reggae/rock acts. Rootdown is a 4 piece band from Oregon. Rootdown is kind enough to let us download their first EP free, just click HERE.

posts.title: Wonderful World
posts.writeup: D-Sisive is a emcee from Toronto. He has a full album already out (The Book) and 2 EP's available HERE. This track I am posting here boasts a slick bass guitar with D-Sisive singing a smooth hook and his 'Opio' like voice on the raps.

posts.title: Bureaucrazy
posts.writeup: Promoe just leaked this over at the Looptroop Rockers blog.

posts.title: Lullabies To Myself
posts.writeup: This is Million Stylez (Reggae artist from Sweden) riddum compilation album featuring reggae artists from all over Europe. It has the likes of Promoe (Looptroop Rockers) and Ziggi from Holland. This is a riddum remember so every artist does his own take on the same dub.

posts.title: O Saya Ft M.I.A
posts.writeup: I remember hearing this song during the movie and liking it. So simply here is that track and if you want to check out more from the soundtrack look for it in stores and itunes. That panning during M.I.A.'s verse is really trippy.

posts.title: Gold (Dubmatix Runnin Remix)
posts.writeup: On March 9th JBB will be releasing a remix project of songs from their last album Amplify. Click the cover art to pre-order the album now. This track was taken from ThePier.org, so thanks to them.

posts.title: Aint Much Really
posts.writeup: Here goes another dope track off of SonReal's debut album Good Morning. Click the cover art to check out some brand new songs and you can get the album at CD baby right now by clicking HERE.
I forgot about this track off of this EP from last year so I just copped it on Itunes, happy valentines day. Don't forget to look out for a new Blue Scholars album due this year!

posts.title: Remedy
posts.writeup: Yes I know another reggae/rock group....I can never get enough of the dub sound so your just going to have to live with it. CC capture the easy going reggae grooves, so you will either think it sounds like all the other reggae/rock groups or simply you will like it. This is their second album, they have a newer album from 2008 also. You can buy both albums from CDbaby HERE. The leader singer sounds a lot like Kaleo from Pepper, not that it matters but just saying.

posts.title: The Fear
posts.writeup: I operate under the idea that good music is good music no matter what type of music your into. So with that being said the thing that draws me to this album is the content of her lyrics. The first single 'The Fear' Lily sings from the point of view of a egotistical ignorant young girl being influenced by corporations media, TV, etc.. Its good to hear solid respectable content in a 'mainstream' album like this.

posts.title: Claiming Respect (Remix)
posts.writeup: Going through my archives today I happened to come across this classic vinyl single from 2004, that I'm sure most of you have never heard. Masta Ace's verse is pretty much one of the best verses I have ever heard. You know its a good verse when no matter how long of a break in between hearing a track that you still remember every word. Personally I liked the original over the remix, but check it for yourself. This shit is classic for me!

posts.title: Someone
posts.writeup: More tracks that I have never heard from the B-side of the Zephyr Song from 2002. If you have never heard either then enjoy!

posts.title: Bunker Hill
posts.writeup: I have never realized how many b-sides the Chilli Peppers have out. I have heard all the b-sides from Stadium Arcadium, but I didn't realize there are some from the By The Way album. If you put all these b-sides together on one album you could have a pretty damn good album. With songs like 'Mercy, Mercy' and 'Save This Lady' its a wonder how they don't make the cut. Here goes the b-side track 'Eskimo' from 2002's By The Way.

posts.title: Blind ft. Million Stylez
posts.writeup: Here goes this months Promoe mp3 leak. Thanks Promoe! Go Here to check out the Looptroop Rockers blog and keep up if your a fan of the best hiphop act out of Sweden. Take notice of the content of this song and turn of the propaganda box!!

posts.title: Take Another Step
posts.writeup: The Lowdown is a very dope group from Toowoomba in Queensland , Australia. These guys combine all types of genre's into their music from hiphop, reggae, blues, rock to funk.

posts.title: At The First Sign of Trouble
posts.writeup: This album was basically a compilation put together by the band for a US release in 2006. TBG have released many singles with songs that never made it to albums, so this album is a compilation of those tracks plus songs off of previous albums.

posts.title: Classic
posts.writeup: Here is some good ol boom bap rap from Cali Agent #1. This is a Japan only release.

posts.title: Together As One
posts.writeup: Elan's voice is pretty damn smooth, it makes you wonder if he is one of Bob Marley's long lost kids. Elan was actually playing with the Wailers from 1997-99, signing Bob's songs. You can tell why they chose Elan to be the man to fill in for Bob. Watch the video, his voice is amazing. Im pretty sure he doesn't need no damn autotune.

posts.title: Submersible
posts.writeup: Finally got my hands on this after I totally forgot about this release. This album caught me off guard with how good it is, you will be bopping your head in no time.

posts.title: Pain and Pleasure
posts.writeup: Here is some happy feel good reggae music that came out earlier in 2008. Close enough is No Needz, Tony A on the vocals with Justin on the bass guitar, Kamal on the rhythm gutair, Gabe on the drums and Jack on the percussion drums. You can grab this on itunes right now.

posts.title: Falliccia
posts.writeup: Japan is full of producers like this that just awe you with their instrumentals. This cd will take you on a melodic journey. Kenmochi is currently on Hydeout Productions with the legendary Nujabes. Don't sleep on the Japanese hip-hop scene! Make sure you keep listening to this song when the acoustic guitar goes on a solo riff at 1:26.

posts.title: Blood Bank
posts.writeup: I may catch some flack for liking this guy, but ever since I heard 'Skinny Love' I began to dig his music. This ep is just leftover tracks from 2008's critically acclaimed For Emma, Forever ago.

posts.title: Bringa Ft Apl (of Black Eyed Peas)
posts.writeup: Channel two consists of two musicians, Andreas Bingel (Hamburg, Germany) and Terence Yoshiaki (Los Angeles). Terence Yoshiaki is the drummer from the Black Eyed Peas. This album features Tre Hardson (Pharcyde), Apl (Black Eyed Peas) and singer Ming from Spooks.

posts.title: Please Believe Ft Diverse, Ka Di
posts.writeup: Longshot is a emcee from the Chicago area on Molemen Records. This album actually came out sept 2nd of 2008. There is no retail rip of this album or webrip so this is the only song I have heard off this album. Addicted features such notables as One Be Lo and Diverse. Click HERE to buy the album for 10 bucks in mp3 format.

posts.title: Lay It Down
posts.writeup: PBJ are coming out with a new album this spring titled Living Thing. After releasing a instrumental album earlier this year I was all worried there wasn't going to be a new album for awhile but that is not the case. "Hey shut the fuck up boy..........."

posts.title: Fire ft. Damian Marley
posts.writeup: You can never go wrong with some Damian Marley on a track. I have been fiending for a new Jr. Gong album ever since the classic Welcome To Jamrock. But I guess this is supposed to be promoting B-Real's new album Smoke and Mirrors due Feb 24th. So yea look for it!

posts.title: Live Show Ft Akil, Pupa Don
posts.writeup: Mabanua is another great producer from Japan. The album features Akil from Jurrasic 5, Kev Brown, and Eshe of Arrested Development.

posts.title: Return Of The One Drop
posts.writeup: Dogboy is reggae artist from Hermosa Beach, CA. This album touches on alot of different styles of music that is sure to have something for someone.

posts.title: Constant Reminder
posts.writeup: The Sabotage Soundsystem is Kaleo (the lead singer of Pepper) and Dub aka Remy Derochemont (Peppers' sound man). Judging from the songs posted on their myspace its going to be more of a mellow acoustic sounding album. Look for the new album (The Boto Machine Gun) Feb 17th 2009!. Click the pic to check out more songs.

posts.title: Killers of the Funk
posts.writeup: These guys are a hiphop crew from the Sunshine Coast of BC. The group consists of 3 emcees (Brett Mjanes, Eli Dill, and Eric Amberg). I actually saw these guys last week, so it was surprising to see a scene rip of this album.

posts.title: Zambony
posts.writeup: Look for a new K-Os album titled "Yes!" due March 2009. I am expecting another solid album, as K-os always makes quality music. (Image courtesy of Okayplayer.com) That advance version of Yes! that's floating around on the net is bad quality.

posts.title: By And By Ft The Grouch
posts.writeup: This was just recently released to the IN-ter-net. Bascially this goes the same as Vol 1, with tracks showcasing upcoming Legends projects due in 2009. Look for new albums from Aesop, Bicasso, Luckyiam, Sunzspot Jonz, The Grouch and Eligh album, and a Eligh and Jo Wilkinson album. Jo Wilkinson, a folk singer, is actually Eligh's mother. Be on the lookout for that album Feb17th as it looks pretty good with appearences from The Grouch, Slug, Pigeon John, and members of Ozomatli. Its a mom/son/folk/hiphop album...sounds good to me.

posts.title: Woman, I Love You
posts.writeup: Stephen is back with a album full of acoustic versions of his very good 2007 album "Mind Control" This is a perfect album to chill out to and to have playing in the background doing whatever it is you do. Its available only on itunes.

posts.title: Into The Spin
posts.writeup: This track is from a compilation of non-album tracks put out recently by the Doomtree collective. Dessa (pictured above) shows us with this track that she has a beautiful voice along with emcee skills. I usually don't like femcees, not in a sexist way, I just don't dig how they're voices sound sometimes. But Dessa is an exception. Dessa is part of the Doomtree crew. Make sure you check out their debut Album released earlier this year.

posts.title: Trouble
posts.writeup: Class will be back with a new album due this March. This is the first single.

posts.title: Say G & E
posts.writeup: G&E are back to follow up 2003's No More Greener Grasses with a new album"Say G&E" slated for a March 2009 release date. There is no press kit for this track, but I am assuming this particular track is produced by Eligh. Look for the album to feature Slug, Gift of Gab, production from Amplive (Zion I) and of course Eligh with his signature beats.

posts.title: Limitless Page
posts.writeup: This is Panacea's first album from back in 2004. I remember looking for this album back when The Scenic Route came out. So now I assume with their new album, On A Mind Ship Through Time recently dropping, that more people are checking for them. This album has more of the same feel as The Scenic Route.

posts.title: Goodbye
posts.writeup: P.O.S is a member of the Minneapolis crew Doomtree and is on the indie powerhouse Rhymesayers. His third solo album "Never Better" is due Feb 3, 2009.

posts.title: Tour Stories
posts.writeup: The Souls of Mischief seem to be back as they have just recently leaked this new track to various blog sites (2dopeboyz.com). No real news of a new album but there has always been that rumor of a album entirely produced by Prince Paul.

posts.title: Fine Again
posts.writeup: The one and only Anthony Hamilton's latest album due Dec 16th.

posts.title: Antenna
posts.writeup: This track was recently released along on the promo they released. From the snippets its shaping up to be a damn good album.
