If you liked Amplives "Radiohead Rainydayz Remixes" then your going to like direction of this album. Amplive seems to be letting loose with the creativity as he has been making some defying beats lately. The Takeover will feature Brother Ali along with Devin The Dude. Look for it this January.

posts.title: Level The Vibes
posts.writeup: Here is some classic old Half Pint. Its sad how I have never heard of this guy till this summer.

posts.title: What You Need (Smooth Current Remix)
posts.writeup: Some of that jazz influenced hiphop from L.A. The emcee is E Reece and Core Elements is his live band complete with the saxophone.

posts.title: Everywhere
posts.writeup: Common is back with another solid album. There is no production from Kanye on this album so don't expect the same sound as "Be" and "Finding Forever". Most of the production is done by Neptunes and Mr. DJ. UMC is out this tuesday and Common is apparently selling the album for 99cents.

posts.title: To Be Determined Ft Elzhi and Aloe Blacc
posts.writeup: New Evidence from the Dilated Peoples. This Ep is out now and features Phonte, Will.i.am, Elzhi, Defari, and production from the Alchemist.

posts.title: Stay Crunchy
posts.writeup: This guy is crazy and has amazing skills. He is a internet sensation right now. Look at how fast that guys fingers move, damn..... I can't even imagine the sheet music from that composition. In this video he uses a guitar synth and just goes off.

posts.title: The World Is Waiting
posts.writeup: This is Stick Figures latest album that came out earlier this year. Burnin' Ocean is a head nodding, upbeat album that needs to be on your ipod asap. So roll one up and lose yourself in the hypnotic spell of that dub. Buy it HERE. This album is very good from the first to last track..

posts.title: I Do It For Hip Hop (Ft. Nas and Jay-Z)
posts.writeup: Ludacris has the potential to make a good album. But its just another album with a couple good tracks. Hits stores Nov 24th...... This is the Jayz-less version.

posts.title: Everywhere I Go Ft The Game, Talib Kweli
posts.writeup: This single is taken from Self Scientific's new album Come In Peace, Prepare For War due later this year. Check out this nice track and you can begin to wish to be a fly on Game's wood grain. And I have no idea why The Game thinks Obama is going to bring back troops. Because last time I checked the guy wants to ship out 3 or 4 more brigades and increase spending on the military. Even Jack Johnson supports Obama (we are in trouble).

posts.title: Rest Of My Life
posts.writeup: This is a compilation cd put together by the members of SOJA. It features reggae artists from all over the world providing a diverse collection for fans of roots reggae. Look for a new SOJA album due 2009. Click the cover art for more tracks and ways to purchase. And if your wondering why all the songs off this album have the same dub sounds, its because this cd is a "Riddum" Which is album that has the same dub throughout the whole album with different takes by each different artist.

posts.title: One More Night Ft LV
posts.writeup: Yea its Coolio and he is still around, and surprisingly is pretty decent. LV from the classic Gangster's Paradise is on the album also.

posts.title: Early Prayers
posts.writeup: Tomorrow's Bad Seeds are a 5 piece band from Southern California. Any fan of the reggae/rock scene needs to add this to their collection asap. Its a well put together album that has no filler tracks, just good solid music throughout the whole album. Its gonna take a few listens to figure out my favorites off of this cd. You can purchase this cd via CDbaby or Itunes. Click the cover art to hit up their myspace and check out some more tracks.

posts.title: Foolish Win Ft Miles (Slightly Stoopid)
posts.writeup: Toko Tasi is originally from Hawaii but now resides in Long Beach. He is on Skunk records with Slightly Stoopid and has been featured on some tracks with Slightly. This is his debut album, you can copp it on itunes, its worth your 10 bucks.

posts.title: ABCs ft Chubb Rock
posts.writeup: K Naan has a new album "Troubador" due in January that will feature the likes of Damian Marley and Mos Def! It should be really dope for sure. Here is the first single.

posts.title: Black Friday ft Del & KFlay
posts.writeup: This is a free mixtape Zion I is putting out for free on their website. Look out for the new Zion I record "The Takeover" due early 2009. Click HERE to download the whole mixtape.

posts.title: Verbal Gunfight
posts.writeup: The original EP came out in 97 but has been out of print since 2004. This is a special re-release that features a bonus disc with exclusives and rare tracks. It also comes with a DVD of live preformances. Its in stores now. The track I am posting here is from a old rare 12".

posts.title: Monlith
posts.writeup: I am a huge fan of JMT but this album has left me disappointed. Its still has 3 or 4 solid tracks but with Jus Allah back I was expecting more. Stoupe's beats don't seem up to par. As always its just my opinion. I listen to ALOT of music and I tend to skip through albums that don't grab my attention right away. So if you ever have any suggestions for me, drop them in the comments. Thanks! History Of Violence is out Nov 11th.

posts.title: Time Is Of The Essence
posts.writeup: This is a brand new full length record from the emcee Surreal. I was not aware of a new album from Surreal but I am pleasantly surprised. Surreal is known for his smooth voice and positive lyrics. He is definitely one of my favorite true school artists. If you have never heard his collaborative album (w/ DJ Balance) " The Balance" from 2006 then you have missed a really good album.

posts.title: Break Bread
posts.writeup: Dujeous a 7 member hiphop group from the NY area. Its been awhile since we have heard a new album from these cats. They had a dope album in 2004 called City Limits with some great tracks. Check back for their new album "Day In, Day Out" due in November. Guaranteed it will have a few bangers.

posts.title: Politrikkks
posts.writeup: This is the first single from the upcoming Dead Prez album "Information Age" These guys are the truth when it comes to the content and lyrics. In this song they are warning the people about having to much faith in Obama to make all these changes that he is claiming to make.

posts.title: Reppin For You (feat. AB)
posts.writeup: If you have been up on your hiphop101 you know who this guy is. He produces and raps, this is his debut album. He pretty much sounds exactly like Royce....

posts.title: So Hi So Low
posts.writeup: Good news for fans of the man they call Matisyahu. He is releasing his brand new album "Light" due early 2009. For now we get this EP that was released on the 10th, so go check for it on itunes.

posts.title: Cycle Of Survival
posts.writeup: We will look back on this era as the golden age of Aussie Hiphop. Hopefully the trend continues because everything that comes out of Australia is always on point with the production and lyrics. I want to move to the land of OZ just for the amazing music, nevermind the beaches. Dialectrix is a emcee from the Blue Mountains (also the home of Hermitude). Cycles of Survival is entirely produced by the very talented Chasm. This album hits stores Oct 18th and is being released on Obese Records (Hilltop Hoods).

posts.title: Full Moon
posts.writeup: These guys are both beatmakers in their own right and this is the collaborative effort. Besides this track the rest of the album is sleepable. I just liked the bass lines in this track I am posting, so check it out bidges.

posts.title: Loosen Up
posts.writeup: Good Intentions are a 3 piece band from San Diego. The album balances hard rock riffs, with chilled out acoustic jams, and smooth basslines guaranteed to have you grooving. These guys are just starting out I assume, they have no Myspace page which is a shame. You can either cop the cd at CD Baby or purchase the album from a MP3 Site which I just did.

posts.title: Follow Me Now
posts.writeup: Kaboose is a emcee from Minnesota on Syntax Records (Braille). Royce Da 5'9 makes a appearance on a track and drops a nice verse. The beat on Follow Me Now is a hard hitting horn/snare beat with crazy left and right pans. The album is available now.

posts.title: Shackles and Chains
posts.writeup: Ziggi is a reggae musician from Holland and this is his second album. Some of you may remember the classic "Blaze It" from 2006's So Much Reasons. Blaaaaaazzzzze it, till your eyes turn red.......

posts.title: Amplify
posts.writeup: Brand new JBB that is in stores and on itunes now! In my opinion their last album "Pressure Points" is a lot better. I am hoping it will grown on me because JBB are dope.

posts.title: Tock ft. The Stereotypes
posts.writeup: Incise is a producer from Canada. But where I have no idea, unless you can recognize that skyline. This is his debut album and its pretty good, the beats outshine the vocals easily.

posts.title: Heartless
posts.writeup: The latest Kanye track to hit the net, hopefully people can wait and not release all the good songs off this album before its released. 808's and Heartbreaks is due Nov 25th.

posts.title: Folks
posts.writeup: The Mighty Underdogs are Gift Of Gab (Blackalicious), Lateef The Truth Speaker and Headnodic (Crown City Rockers) on the production. DSF features Damian Marley, Akrobatik, Mr Lif, DJ Shadow, Chali 2na, MF Doom, Tash, Zion (Zion I), and Casual. Pretty much a who's who of the bay area and with a line up like that what do you expect!? This album hits stores October 14th.

posts.title: La Femme Parallel
posts.writeup: TC needs no introduction. Just toss this is in the ipod and vibe out. This album is perfect for those moods where you just want to chill out and seep out of reality into the music. Hit up Itunes right now for the album, its worth it. If you have a Ninja on your cover it must be good...right?

posts.title: Set It Straight Ft J-Live
posts.writeup: This guy has some of the coolest, funkiest beats I have ever heard. Black Grass is a DJ/Producer from Brighton , UK. The album boasts vocals from J-live, Dionne Charles, Aaron Phiri, and more artists I have never heard. But rest assured the vocal contributions keep up with his amazingly funked out vibes. Put this on your must hear list ASAP.

posts.title: Fit 4 A King
posts.writeup: Sway is from the UK and this is his second solo album. Dont know much about him so if you care use your friend Google. I know some of you will hate on this, it wont grace a mix but its worth a shot.

posts.title: Change
posts.writeup: Recently in Puerto Viejo, Costa Rica I was rocking out a hostel with the lovely portable speakers and I got a request for some classic Spooks tracks off of 2000's S.I.O.S.O.S. Vol. 1. To my disappointment I realized that I completely forgot about how good that album was. Tracks like Karma Hotel, Things Ive Seen, and Something Fresh were and still are classic tracks. And yes I didn't have the album on the mp3 player...oops. But to get to the point I just recently updated my Spooks archive and I noticed this demo that I never heard before. So ya here is a track that you may have heard or not. But that brings up the question where the hell are the Spooks?! The last album they put out was in 03'. Spooks, drop some new shit por favor!

posts.title: Our Father
posts.writeup: The Philadelphia based duo of Planetary and Crypt The Warchild are back with their third album. Its in stores now.

posts.title: Monolith
posts.writeup: Vinnie Paz, Stoupe and yes! Jus Allah are back together again to bring in the fall with a new JMT record. It should not disappoint! Check for it on November 11th.

posts.title: Cool
posts.writeup: The best man in Soul music is back with his newest album "The Point Of It All" dropping November 17th. Check the cool first single here:

posts.title: The Pursuit
posts.writeup: Some hiphop from Hialeah, South Florida to check out. "My uncle came lodged in a AC shaft"....haha.

posts.title: Frayed
posts.writeup: Hermitude is back fresh off of last years Rare Sightings EP with 11 new tracks to vibe out to. Hermitude are two dudes (Elgusto and Luke Dubs) from the Blue Mountains, Australia. Elgusto handled some of the prodcution or Urthboy's the signal. Click the album art to listen to the whole album via Myspace. This album is out now on itunes.

posts.title: Smilin On You ft. Akrobatik
posts.writeup: >This is the first group I have ever heard out of Jacksonville...no disrespect to other acts. But The Smile Rays are Therapy (Emcee), Daisey (Vocals) and Batsauce (Beats). Click the photo for their myspace.

posts.title: Love Lockdown (Remix)
posts.writeup: Ap pretty much tears it up so badly and lets it known how he feels about that autotune. T-pain look out...

posts.title: Can It Be
posts.writeup: With all the hype and expectations for Murs' first major label release, I didn't expect much. But to my surprise its actually pretty good. Its good to see a guy like Murs who's been working hard to finally get some shine. I hope for his sake that this album sells well. It hits stores This tuesday.

posts.title: People Riddum
posts.writeup: Thes-one and Double K are back with their newest album dropping this Tuesday. I've been looking forward to this release. Its PUTS so make sure you listen and gain your own opinion. Here's two solid tracks to give you a taste...

posts.title: Losing Keys (Katalyst Remix)
posts.writeup: This is just a remix album of some cuts from the latest Jack Johnson album. This album is only being sold on itunes. On this remix Katalyst lends his production and adds a sick beat to give this song a whole new life. Some of you may remember Katalyst's album "Whats Happening" from last year. If not then click his name and check out what you have missed.

posts.title: Easy
posts.writeup: This is a very promising first effort for the Dirty Heads from Huntington Beach, California. The album crosses many genres and should appeal to a wide audience. So fork out that 10 bucks and get this on your ipod asap.

posts.title: The City Ft J Live and Surreal
posts.writeup: Dela is a producer from Paris, France that combines hiphop with jazz and soul. Along with his counterparts also from Paris is the Jazz Liberatorz who dropped the impressive "Clin d' Oeil" earlier this year. "Changes of Atmosphere" enlists some help from some of the best underground acts out right now ( J-Live, Talib Kweli, Surreal, Supasitition, Blu and Termanology). Click the cover art for his myspace and more songs to peep.

posts.title: Life Is A Gift
posts.writeup: This the official full length dropping Sept 17th. CY is hiphop group from Finland/Sweden with Mr. Singh doing the hooks. Redrama , Promoe and Paleface drop the verses. . This album is solid and sadly will be hard pressed to get exposure here. So do what you can and spread the word. Its on itunes now!

posts.title: Viva La Vida" (Coldplay Cover)
posts.writeup: In light of the highly anticipated debut album from the SoCal group Dirty Heads releases two singles. If you are unfamliar with them just know that they seem up hiphop and reggae with ease. The new album "Any Port In Storm" drops Sept 30th. So keep a lookout. You can get this single and "Stand Tall" on itunes now.

posts.title: Turning On You
posts.writeup: This is the intro song from this album and sadly is the only good track.

posts.title: EY (Termanology Verse)
posts.writeup: DJ Revolution is a well know/well respected DJ. The guy is a regular on the famous wake up show. But to get to the point here is the sickest verse off the whole album. Brought to you by Termanology. Look for his debut coming soon.

posts.title: Lets Get Up Ft Tree Woodz
posts.writeup: Effect and Joe Fury are Canto I from Las Vegas. Click the cover art for more info and songs.

posts.title: Land Far Away
posts.writeup: Another good track to check from "This Day" released way back in 2000. JBB also has released, Among Them (1999) and Spirits All Around Us (2002).

posts.title: Not Enough
posts.writeup: John Brown's Body is a 8 piece reggae group from Massachusetts. The band has been heralded as "America's best and most original reggae band". This album has the horns of C-Money (Slightly Stoopid). After this album C-Money split from the group. Some of the band members are also apart of 10ft Ganja Plant. Look for the newest album "Amplify" dropping September 30th.

posts.title: Karma Police Ft Citizen Cope
posts.writeup: Easy Star All Stars is a group of musicians who cover albums and put the roots/reggae twist on them. And for this album they re-worked Radiohead's OK Computer. Along on the cover is Citizen Cope.

posts.title: Whats This World Coming To? Ft Slug
posts.writeup: Emcee out of Minneapolis, Minnesota. Click the album art to check more from Muja Messiah.

posts.title: Oh My ft. John Wilson
posts.writeup: Its time to show some love for some local talent. Originally from my hometown of Vernon, BC. Son Real's flow and voice reminds me of Hezekiah, which is impressive. The content of his lyrics is refreshingly original and you can tell the kid has skill. The production is on point also. If you are in the Vic, Van or Vernon area you can pick up his cd at Dunas Skate or Beat Street. Click the album art to check out more dope tracks from his new album "Good Morning"

posts.title: Splash Ft Killah Priest
posts.writeup: Pumpkinhead, Block McCloud and Mr. Metaphor are the Brooklyn Academy. Already solidifying their solo careers comes the debut album of the group. It features Ill Bill, Killah Priest, and Jean Grae.

posts.title: Absinth Tears Ft Mother Mother
posts.writeup: Josh is back at it with his 5th solo album. The new album features Devin the Dude, Moka Only, Classified and Pigeon John. Show some love and peep the catchy songs. The album is out Sept 23rd!

posts.title: Blinded By The Sun
posts.writeup: This is the first glimpse we get from the new album from the crew due Sept 9th. This track caught my ear right away, and hopefully it catches yours.

posts.title: The Truth ft. Brother Ali & Freeway
posts.writeup: One of Seattle's best producer's is dropping a album October 7th. If you are familiar with his work one would be stoked on this release. Its got Brother Ali, Little Brother, Elzhi, Royce, MF Doom, Posdnous (De La Soul), Slug, and a shitload more. Click on the album art to check more tracks off his new album. After some listens you only want to hear this track for Ali's sick verse, so I just cut out the bad Freeway verse. Bad verses get the heave ho!

posts.title: A.D Ft SUN
posts.writeup: Dope group out of Ann Arbor, Michigan. The album features Buff 1 and 14k of the Athletic Mic League fam.

posts.title: Nina Sing
posts.writeup: Feast your ears on this from the highly anticipated album "Tobacco Road" due Sept 9th.

posts.title: For U (M-Phazes Remix)
posts.writeup: The Gold Coast's best beatsmith drops a remix album.

posts.title: Mary Jane
posts.writeup: The Bermudan soothes easily over the guitar driven Sublime cover of "Mary".

posts.title: Play
posts.writeup: Conscious Youths is the Scandinavian super group consisting of Promoe ( Looptroop), Redrama, Paleface MC, and Capenape. There is no word of when the official album will drop but you can check out "Play", and "Don't Worry" for now. Expect a good album when it does drop.

posts.title: Hardcore Hiphop (Prod Marco Polo)
posts.writeup: This is for all you fans of the 90's boom bap era. Large Pro brings it back with his newest album. Its in stores Sept 30th.

posts.title: Ladder Of Success
posts.writeup: The Justus League is a big group of emcees, dj's, producers from the NC area. Some of the most notable members are Phonte, Nicolay, Median, 9th wonder and the man himself Khrysis. This is just another bootleg put together full of some new shit from the camp.

posts.title: Letter To The King Ft Nas
posts.writeup: Game's 3rd album out August 26th. It features the all-star lineup of Common, Nas, Travis Barker, Raekwon, Ice Cube, Lil Wayne, Ludacris and more. This track is taken from the Clean version that hit the scene a few days ago.

posts.title: Edge Of The Moon
posts.writeup: Xav is back with the brand new album due August 19th!! This album is a bit different from White Moth. Dark Shades of Blue is a little bit darker (hence the title) with more heavy guitar songs. This is classic Xavier Rudd, he keeps getting better and better. Albums like this make dust build up on your skip button.

posts.title: Easyflowgoodflowbadflowinthewater
posts.writeup: Representing Des Moines, Iowa is Maxilla Blue. Consisting of 1MC (Asphate Woodhavet), 1 Producer (Aeon Gray) and 1 DJ (TouchNice). People jump out to think wtf Des Moines? But these days where your from is irrelevant when it comes to good music. So put Des Moines on the map now....

posts.title: Om Numah Shiv
posts.writeup: Apache Indian is a British vocalist that uses his East Indian background to create his own unique brand of music. He has released 6 albums since 1993...so he has been around for a minute. Check back for more later as I will be going through all his past releases. Thanks goes out to Balchin for setting up the path for this song.

posts.title: Kid Gloves
posts.writeup: Doomtree is a hiphop collective out of Minneapolis. This album has been making some noise on the internet lately. This is the type of album that will take a few listens to decide your opinion. Doomtree's sound is influenced by punk and alternative.

posts.title: Colors
posts.writeup: If you have never heard of Elzhi from Slum Village then you need to stop getting music from your sister. His flow is ridiculous, and the beats are infectious. Elzhi is the Masta Ace of Detroit. The Preface boasts production from Black Milk, T3 and Dj Dez. Listen now....

posts.title: Pass It Around
posts.writeup: Donavon has his 3rd album being released on August 19th. It features G. Love and Ben Harper. Check the first single here. You can preview some of the new tracks on his myspace (click the cover art). Its sounding very good.

posts.title: Small Town
posts.writeup: After a long hiatus, Nappy Roots is back with a new album. Its a pretty solid effort over a first listen, Small Town and No Static are easily the best tracks on the album. It hits stores today. (Warning....the song is very catchy)

posts.title: And Now Ft Vinnie Paz, Apathy
posts.writeup: King Syze's sophmore album from the AOTP fam.

posts.title: Over The Color You Like
posts.writeup: Based on the myth that if you sync up Pink Floyd's "Dark Side Of The Moon" with the 3rd roar of the MGM lion from "The Wizard Of Oz" it will match up. The Problemaddicts basically made this album on that. Full of Pink Floyd samples and Wizard of OZ soundbytes. Don't miss out on this cool album.

posts.title: Street Life
posts.writeup: The Production duo of K-Salaam and Beatnick give us a diverse collection of tracks on their debut record. They prove to be on point with the hiphop and reggae production. The album features Dead Prez, Talib Kweli, Sizzla, and many more. Out on Itunes now.

posts.title: Darling This Scene Must End
posts.writeup: Mat Mchugh of the Beautiful Girls is putting out his first solo album this September. The new album is called "Seperatsita". Click the album art for his myspace page for more info.

posts.title: Motown 25 Ft Royce Da 59
posts.writeup: A tour Cd? uhhh are you kidding me. This cd has atleast 10 bangers and there is only 13 tracks! Elzhi is so consistent with his music. Always on point and always has great soulful beats from Detroit's Black Milk. If this is just a tour cd, damn. He has his first solo project dropping on Aug 12th called "The Preface".

posts.title: One Good Time
posts.writeup: First of all, its hard not to notice how ridiculous that cover is...hahah. It seems every Tech n9ne release there is always one song that seems to catch my ear.

posts.title: Bang Bang
posts.writeup: The Knux are a up and coming group from New Orleans. They have been getting some hype and recently received a XL in the magazine XXL. These guys are on interscope so expect some decent exposure. Look for the full length album coming soon this year.

posts.title: Elephant Rome
posts.writeup: Finally we get the LONG awaited follow up to Evolution Flight! Enoch and Speck are back with the Jazz/Soul/Electronic beats that we expect. And of course Cise and Akin drop their 2 cents over each track. This is one of those albums that is impossible to sum up in a paragraph and would be unfair to judge after one listen. But if your a fan of CYNE dig into you pocket and cop this off of itunes for 10 bucks. Click Here to purchase!

posts.title: So Many Times
posts.writeup: Just came across this today....This is some very original music from L.A. I am feeling this track, check it, the beat is sick. To buy the whole ep go HERE, its only 6 bucks for some quality music!

posts.title: Alphabets
posts.writeup: The first single from GZA's new lp "Pro Tools". Check for it August 19th.

posts.title: She Can Be So Cold
posts.writeup: Mr. Green does the beats and Pacewon on the vocals. Mr. Greens beats are banging throughout the whole album. Don't let this fly under your radar.

posts.title: Heart Of Dem
posts.writeup: Some up and coming emcee out of London, England. The song is dope.

posts.title: Eliza
posts.writeup: Jackson Jackson is Harry Angus from The Cat Empireon the vocalsand Jan Skubiszewski is the producer. And yes his name his Harry Angus...... and I have no idea what type of music Pschobilly is ? Take a listen to find out.

posts.title: Wild International
posts.writeup: One day as a lion is a collaborative of Zach De La Rocha (Rage Against The Machine) and Jon Theodore (Drummer from Mars Volta).

posts.title: I Got No Time For Yall
posts.writeup: Apparently my head has been under a rock.....these guys combine reggae and hiphop with ease.

posts.title: Filthy Rich
posts.writeup: Muph and Plutonic are a hiphop crew from Melbourne. This is a album that won't blow you away but is good enough to stick around on your playlist. Plutonic's beats are head nodding goodness with saucy soul samples that keep you satisfied.

posts.title: Yae Yo Ft Al Tariq, Beatnuts
posts.writeup: This new Ill Bill hits stores August 26th! Check the very dope track with the sick flute.

posts.title: Stomach
posts.writeup: This is a very good 5 piece band from Victoria BC. The album is very upbeat and good for sunny days out on the deck. Check for a live show near you.

posts.title: Hero Ft Keri Hilson
posts.writeup: This album is alot better than I thought it was going to be. The production is good and Nas sounds great on every track. The album hits stores tomorrow.

posts.title: After All
posts.writeup: This is not your typical Paperboys track at all....its Vinnie singing most of the song. Which is something we haven't heard yet. Its still a dope track. Look for a new Paperboys album by the end of the year!

posts.title: Amsterdam
posts.writeup: These guys hail from Stockholm, Sweden. I heard this catchy song while I was travelling. So thanks to the back broken american from the JERSEY SHORE!

posts.title: No Static
posts.writeup: Nappy Roots is back on the scene with this very solid track produced by Greg Nice. Shouts to Kermode for the heads up.

posts.title: Things That You Love
posts.writeup: This is the first taste along with "Davey Jones Locker" that we get from the new Pepper album. If this song is any indication we are in for another classic Pepper record! This album drops July 22nd.

posts.title: Heart Of A Lion
posts.writeup: This is Staylefish's newest track. Look for the new album this year!

posts.title: Tobacco Road
posts.writeup: Common Market has been making some serious noise lately with some quality music. And of course Sabzi has a little to do with that....This is the first single from the new album "Tobacco Road" due September 9th. This album should be great, so keep a lookout.

posts.title: Wontcha Come Home
posts.writeup: Full of feel good songs just in time for the summer. If you are a fan of G Love make sure to make a effort to get this asap. Your life needs music!

posts.title: No Cocaine
posts.writeup: Don't be fooled into thinking that this is a re-release of the ep from 2006. The official album drops on July 22nd with 20 tracks and are all mostly new.

posts.title: Mission ft. Stephen Marley
posts.writeup: This is the single released off a new Riddum Cd called Mission. This is the remix I am posting here that is a bit slower but with a acoustic guitar. Check the other version on his myspace page. No news of a new album yet, sadly.

posts.title: Loopholes
posts.writeup: 3 years of waiting for some new Cyne and we get 2 new albums! This is the first one to drop and to me its not really a official Cyne album, with only 11 tracks with old songs and used verses. We do get 7 new tracks though. To me this album is mostly a disappointment compared to other Cyne releases. The beats just don't hit me that much and seem a bit boring. Don't get it twisted though this cd is better than most of the shit out there. Its nothing to bitch about but these guys are one of my favorite hiphop acts and I just expect alot. So keep a lookout for the full length "Pretty Dark Things" due any day now. It should be epic

posts.title: Pearl
posts.writeup: Australia's The Herd returns this summer for their 4th release. The album's production is definetley top notch with alot of live instruments from horns to violens. It just seems that Urthboy and Ozi Batla get outshined by the production. I find myself just wanting to hear Jane sing her parts. But still this album is good and is worth your time to seek it out. Its available on Itunes right now.

posts.title: Be No Slave
posts.writeup: J-live's newest outing is full of dope beats and witty rhymes. If you are a fan of his past work then I'm sure your going to enjoy this album.

posts.title: Necro" ft. Ghostface Killah
posts.writeup: This is the first single from the highly anticipated album from the God himself Raekwon. Look for this album to drop this summer as if its anything like the original it should be classic.

posts.title: L.E.S Artistes
posts.writeup: Now this isn't normally the type of music I would catch myself liking. But you just can't deny that this girl has talent and a ear for good production. There is a crazy hype for this album right now, shes being compared to M.I.A. In Common's words, singer-MC Santogold is "one of my favorite artists right now" Her voice reminds me of a Nelly Furtado. This album is in stores now.

posts.title: Pure Sugar
posts.writeup: These guys capture that 70's roots/reggae sound perfectly. They have 4 albums Hillside Airstrip (01), Midnight Landing (03), Bass Chalice (05) and the recently remastered album (07). The majority of their albums are just the instrumental dub songs with the occasional vocals. But when they do drop vocals on the song its for sure going to be a good track. These albums are a must have to rock on the beach, cause you can't always be banging that 50 Cent.

posts.title: One Loaf Of Bread (Something For You)
posts.writeup: This was released last year on the Tuff Gong Label (The Marley fam's label). I slept on the song when I first heard it on Damian's myspace, but after recently seeing the video I realized that I f'd up. Peep the video. This cd is a "Riddum". A Riddum is a cd that uses the same beat on every song throughout the album. I don't understand why but you only see this with Reggae. R.I.P Cedella Marley.

posts.title: Royal Flush ft. Andre 3000 and Raekwon
posts.writeup: This is the first leaked single from Big Boi's new album "Sir Luscious Left Foot" due later this year.

posts.title: Drama Ft Monk and Thea
posts.writeup: This summer on June 24th RZA will drop his newest album "Digi Snax". The track im posting is on the B-side of the single. With the beat in this track one would wonder why didn't RZA use these beats for the Wu album? The girl on the hook has a dope voice (Thea).

posts.title: Ready (Brand New)
posts.writeup: New 9th and Buckshot from The Boot Camp Clik. This was the only track I was really feeling off this.

posts.title: These Walls Don't Lie
posts.writeup: The new Looptroop has inspired me to do a little mp3 diggin and look up albums I have not heard yet, from the Looptroop crew. Im sure peeps in Sweden would slap me if I told them I haven't heard this album. So to prevent getting slapped by a swede make sure you hear this album.

posts.title: Butter and Gun$ (Loyalty Pt 2.)
posts.writeup: Blue Scholars are definitely making their name heard with some amazing music these days. And this EP just adds to their resume of great records. This EP has 2 new tracks "Butter&Guns (Loyalty Pt 2)" and the autobiographical "27". You can get this at itunes on April 29th....I am def going to buy this as soon as it drops and you should too!

posts.title: Criminal Ft Saigon, Truck North
posts.writeup: The Legendary Roots Crew return with the anticipated release of "Rising Down". I just got my hands on it so im not going to critique it that much. But I will say through a first listen that Black Thought just tears it throughout the album. Rising Down features Mos Def, Talib Kweli, Common, Styles P, Saigon and many more. Go get it April 29th! The version of Rising Down I have posted here is the album quality, the last one was shite quality.

posts.title: Ronnie
posts.writeup: This is taken from his Demo ep from 2001. I would definitley like to get my hands on that. Redrama is a Finnish emcee, for those that don't know. The beat and sample on this track are great.

posts.title: The Building
posts.writeup: For those that don't know, should know now!. Looptroop is the biggest hiphop act out of Sweden. They consist of 2 emcees Promoe , Supreme and Dj Embee. This is their newest album, its available right now on itunes. The beats are good for some headnodding and the emcees have the skills to make this good international hiphop. Peep Looptroop's myspace to preview the whole album. This album is dope!

posts.title: Xmas Seasons ft. Nate Dogg & Snoop Dogg
Driftin
posts.writeup: This is Daniel Wesley's solo acoustic album. Its pretty chill.

posts.title: Perfect Timing" (Prod. M-Phazes)
posts.writeup: Perfect Timing is taken from the "Unearthed" compilation being put out by Coalmine Records this year . M-Phazes adds his touch to this nice track with Cl Smooth and Skyzoo.

posts.title: Puppets
posts.writeup: Atmosphere returns with their sixth studio album! It hits stores this tuesday, so go get it! This album is dope, plain and simple. I give this album 5 out of 5.

posts.title: Mogadishu
posts.writeup: E-Dot from Uncle Howie Records and Darp Malone from the Toronto area come together to make this unique album. There is plenty of variety on this album that is sure to be a hit. Album is on itunes now!

posts.title: Readup
posts.writeup: Sick Beat on this one....http://www.rawpoetixrecords.com/

posts.title: The Dark Ages Ft Murs
posts.writeup: Mighty Joesph is Vast Aire Of Cannibal Ox and Karnage. Murs lights up a dope verse on this track. Peep here.

posts.title: Grand Scale
posts.writeup: This is DL's newest album that is in stores now. Make sure to check him out in Vernon on the 16th. This album is mostly a disappointment for me, sadly. Tech twelve's beats are average and not great like how they were on Life Is A Collection Of Experiences. Im not sayin I want to hear another album exactly like L.I.A.C.O.E but I just want good quality music that I know DL can make. And with the last 2 albums it just doesn't seem to be there. The beat he did for Grand Scale is dope tho. As always don't take anyone else's opinion to heart before you hear it yourself, so go pick it up.

posts.title: The Exorcist (Prod. DJ Premier)
posts.writeup: Personally whenever I see an artists name start with little, lil or whatever prefix you wanna think of, I immediately hate. But this is one of those rare cases. Little Vic has the lyrics, beats, content, and a voice to back it up. Each dawn I die has DJ Premier and Buckshot helping out on the production tip.

posts.title: The Upgrade Ft Oddisee & Posdnous
posts.writeup: This is the first track leaked from J-Live's newest album "Then What Happend?" due out May 26th. This will be a banger no doubt! The 2na fish himself will be featured on this album also.

posts.title: Im Out Ft Cise Star and Akin
posts.writeup: Debilorithmicos are 2 producers from Barcelona, Spain. Their beats are very fresh and have a unique sound. And to even make it better they did a track with CYNE!

posts.title: Less One
posts.writeup: Just when you thought Slug and Ant ran out of material for these sad clown bad dub series, they go and drop the Bad Spring. It was originally going to come out April 29th but they leaked it on April fools day. Click Here to download the EP. And don't forget to buy When Life Gives You Lemons.... on April 22nd!

posts.title: Wolves With Flowers
posts.writeup: This is just a new track that Cise did with Sharpsound. Check it out. Also some good news from CYNE, they are dropping the new album (finally) in May. Its called Pretty Dark Things, it should be epic. Stay tuned for more info.

posts.title: Keep It Coming
posts.writeup: There is not much I can tell you about this group but that this is probally a Japan only release. They aren't on myspace and google only shows up pages in Japanese. But peep the song anyways.

posts.title: Worlds Apart
posts.writeup: While touring Europe the crew had 4 days off in Geneva, so they decided to rush into a local studio and bust out some new beats. When u get 3 dope emcees locked in the studio for 4 days your going to get some good music. Ohmega Watts handles the beats on this. Click Here to buy the album in Mp3's and u can listen to more songs off of the album.

posts.title: In Your Eyes (Dub)
posts.writeup: This is New Zealand's Black Seeds first album from '01. Very dope and chilled out dub songs all over this. A must have for fans of the dub sound. You can hear Will Ferell's cowbell in that In Your Eyes track.

posts.title: Original Rudeboys
posts.writeup: Everliven Sound are Cymarshall Law and Skit Slam. These guys have a dope sound to them, its reggae influenced hiphop. The beats are done by a production team (The Beatnikz) from Sweden. Make a point to hear all of this album, you wont be disappointed, its some good hiphop.

posts.title: Paradise Ft Krayzie Bone
posts.writeup: Album hits stores April 28th. TQ really hasn't done anything good since "They Never Saw Me Coming" from '98. Paradise doesn't help much to his resume. The track with Krayzie Bone is pretty catchy and has made me post it.

posts.title: I Need A Beat Ft Kato and Dendenann
posts.writeup: I am assuming this song is off of one these french guys albums. The beat is siiiick, love those horns....

posts.title: Come Over
posts.writeup: This is Estelle's debut album that is being released on John Legend's label. Estelle hails from London , England. This is a feel good album with good production to back it up, make sure to show your girl this album. Estelle has the number one song in the UK at the moment with "American Boy" so its only a matter of time before she blows up here. So hear this. The guest spots on Shine are Kanye West, John Legend, Cee-Lo, and Kardinal Offishall. With Will.i.am, Wyclef and Mark Ronson handling some of the production.

posts.title: Guarantees
posts.writeup: This is the Vinyl single for "When Life Gives You Lemons...You Paint That Shit Gold". With "Shoulda Known" being the lead single and the acoustic "Guarantees" on the B-side. The new album is in stores April 22nd. I have a feeling the B-side will just be a bonus track so check out Slug over the acousitc guitar, its different for sure.

posts.title: What Would You Do Ft Jay Rush
posts.writeup: This is AZ's newest full length featuring all new songs. Undeniable hits stores this tuesday!

posts.title: Lilacs
posts.writeup: I accidentally let this one slip under the radar (oops). This is Matt Costa's second album. Unfamiliar faces was released in Jan , so go look for it in stores. Its perfect for those Jack Johnson type of moods.

posts.title: The Girl Who Fell Through The Ice Ft Kate Rogers
posts.writeup: Aim is a British DJ and Producer and this is his 2nd album from 2002. Its mostly a really chilled out instrumental cd that you can just press play and not skip a track. Diamond D , Substantial and singer Kate Rogers contribute to the 3 songs with vocals.

posts.title: Hot Air Balloons Ft Scarub, Bicasso
posts.writeup: This is the Grouch's newest album since '02's Crusader For Justice. The album doesn't disappoint! Show you the world features Murs, Scarub, Bicasso, Marty James, Mike Marshall, Raphael Saadiq and Abstract Rude. It hits stores April 8th, pre-order Here

posts.title: Children Of The Earth
posts.writeup: The Aicube Music Crew is a crew of producers out of Japan. Peep the nice instrumental below.

posts.title: Paper Cup Ft Percee P
posts.writeup: Grand first hit the scene in o4' with the "Poppin Pockets" 12 inch single with A.G. This new album is gritty hiphop with soulful beats, its good ish.

posts.title: Sweet Addiction Ft Cupid Lee
posts.writeup: Dj Hoppa is a beatmaker/Dj from San Fernando, California. Network Networth features Bliss N Eso, Josh Martinez, and KRS-One. Listen to the very cool song with Cupid Lee. (Girls make sure to not miss this one).

posts.title: Raw Ft Vinnie Paz
posts.writeup: The only reason this song makes my site cause the beat is sick and Vinnie P tears it. Then the dudes from Randam Luck ruin it. Sorry for the hate but their voices are irritating. Purchase the song Here. Randam Luck are a group out of San Diego and are on Babygrande records.

posts.title: Peace
posts.writeup: UK emcee Life from the Phi-Life Cypher.

posts.title: The Gathering
posts.writeup: This 7 track EP from the legends crew features all 8 emcees on every single track! This is the prelude EP for the full length they will be dropping later this year. This album comes out April 8th, along with The Grouch's new solo "Show You The World".

posts.title: That White (Prod DJ Premier)
posts.writeup: This album actually surpassed my expectations. He's strayed away from a album full of pop rap and actually made a dope album. The beats are solid with Premier lacing up a beautiful beat for Joe on "That White". The album is out now.

posts.title: The IV Ft Rob Swift (Cuts), Marco Polo (Beats)
posts.writeup: On April 15th, Braille drops his 4th Lp (pronounced I-Vee). This album has a different producer on each track and he got some help from some of the best .....Marco Polo, Kno (Cunninlynguists), M-Phazes, S1 (Strange Fruit Project), K-Murdock (Panacea) and Ohmega Watts. Braille covers a wide rage of topics in his rhymes on "The IV Edition". Its self described as "celebration soundtrack". Braille is from Portland so keep an eye out for a show near you. Make sure to not sleep on this release.

posts.title: Going On
posts.writeup: Dangermouse and Cee-lo hit us back with the sequel to St. Elsewhere. Through a first couple listens I can't decide how much I like it. I am hoping the songs will grow on me, just like St. Elsewhere did. But make sure to check it out for yourself. The Odd Couple hits stores April 8th.

posts.title: Nonverbal Communication
posts.writeup: Ive been playing this all week and cant seem to get rid of it. Its trippy but dope. Ill insanity is Rob Swift, Total Eclipse and Precision (X-Ecutioners).

posts.title: Vicious Circle Ft Ivens and Muph
posts.writeup: Chasm is a one half of the producer duo that is Astronomy Class  and this is his debut album. It features Australia's Urthboy, Ozi Batla (The Herd) and Muph (Muph and Plutonic).

posts.title: Everything Aint Easy
posts.writeup: Chaundon is a member of the Justus League (Little Brother, 9th wonder, Nicolay) and this is his debut album.

posts.title: The King Is Dead
posts.writeup: The Ozzie hiphop group The Herd is dropping a new album this May/June. Its tentatively called Summerland. Check out the first single to hit Australian airwaves in March. Urthboy does the first verse with Ozi Batla doin the second verse. This album should be dope, keep on the look for it in the coming months.

posts.title: Ready Fe War Ft Chip Fu
posts.writeup: The soul brother returns after a 4 year hiatus. NY's finest focuses more on the local talent out of NY. It features Little Brother, Raekwon, Redman, Chip Fu, and Masta Killa to name a few. Its out now.

posts.title: Soldier
posts.writeup: This album is full of dark loomy beats that you can't help but nod your head to. Its some cool shit if you can open your mind up. Its in stores so go check for it.

posts.title: Southbound
posts.writeup: This Ep features the title track, a remix of Joe Metro, a new track "Southbound" and a remix of North by Northwest that features Macklemore. This is a digital only release so click Here to buy it.

posts.title: Kindred Ft Chuck D, Brenna Gethers
posts.writeup: Akrobatik drops his new album on Feb 22nd. This album is just honest , soild and refreshing hiphop. Ak deserves alot of credit. "Kindred" is a amazing track, the beat is unreal. Chuck D makes an appreance also on the song with some strong words.

posts.title: The Marijuana Song
posts.writeup: Chad Hatcher first hit the music scene for me when he lit up "All About You" from Classified's latest "Hitch Hikin Music". Tunnels and Pathways is produced entirely by Classified and is being released on his label (Half Life Records). You can preview the album Here. Its in stores now so go check for it. This is a good album.

posts.title: Who We Be
posts.writeup: Masta Ace, Wordsworth, Punchline and Stricklin make up for EMC. "The Show" follows the same thematic story style as Ace's last 2 albums. This album is sick period. It boasts production from Marco Polo, Nicolay, Koolade (The Croation dude who produced "Beautiful") and DJ Premier. Little Brother and Sean Price are featured on 2 tracks. This is the type of album you need to buy if your a fan of good hiphop. Support good music please.

posts.title: The Gunshot
posts.writeup: Nicolay is a producer out of the Netherlands. He is mainly known for his work on the classic Foreign Exchange album from '04 with Phonte. Nicolay links up with Kay of the Foundation for this full length LP. This album has only been released in Europe for the time being.

posts.title: Acting
posts.writeup: This was actually released in '96 only in Japan but is now being reissued. Apparently this cd back in the day was a cult classic and "extremely rare ep" The title track has that classic sound to it with that sick sample.

posts.title: City On A River
posts.writeup: This is a group outta Saskatoon and as random as it gets. No idea how I came across this but maybe some people will like it? Check it out

posts.title: Let Them Try
posts.writeup: Redrama just upped two more new songs on his myspace page. "Let em Try" and "Follow my Lead" Check em out

posts.title: Enemy
posts.writeup: Well what to say....Do you think he would put out a bad album? is that possible? I just got my hands on it and will be on the playlist for months to come. Its in stores now so go get it! Here are some teasers....

posts.title: The Game Needs Me ft Common Market and Blue Scholars
posts.writeup: Guru links up with RA Scion (Common Market) and Geologic (Blue Scholars) for this track off of his new mixtape.

posts.title: Cool Down ft. Raashan Ahmad
posts.writeup: Jazz Liberatorz are three French producers from Paris. These guys have some slick beats with some dope emcees. Included on this project is Asheru, Raashan Ahmad (Crown City Rockers) , J-Live, J-Sands, Sadat X and Tableek from Maspkye....just to name a few. These guys clearly know good music. Peep the dope song with Raashan Ahmad.

posts.title: Shorty
posts.writeup: Black Sheep? I thought these dudes had fallen of the face of the earth. All I remember of them is their acclaimed debut album "A Wolf In Sheep's Clothing" And to be honest, I can even remember that cd very well.....anyways peep the track.

posts.title: Hopeful ft Fat Rat Da Czar (prod M-phazes)
posts.writeup: Hot of the heels of last years "In the face of danger", Dan Johns returns with the free mixtape. It features Supastition, Chaundon, iCON the mic king and K-Hill. Click Here to download the free mixtape.

posts.title: Not You
posts.writeup: Finally some new shit from the Heiruspecs. Its been 4 years since the classic "A Tiger Dancing". Some of you may remember that 5ves song from '04. This is a best of plus some new tracks, its to bad its not a full length new LP. But thats alright because every one of the new tracks hold their own. Quality music right here....the bass guitar on that Guns and Knives track is dope

posts.title: Ok Now Ft Gift Of Gab
posts.writeup: Living Legends member Sunspot drops his newest Lp since Only the strong survive. The album features fellow Legends alum (Grouch, Luckyiam) and Gift of Gab from Blackalicious. If you like the Legends then don't miss out on this release. Order Here

posts.title: American Boy Ft Kanye West
posts.writeup: Estelle is the newest edition to John Legend's Homeschool Record label. If you don't like this song then im sure your girl will...or not

posts.title: Slow Down
posts.writeup: Finnish MC Redrama just hit me up with a email sayin how he's droppin a new album this year and will be releasing songs on his myspace every 2 months for free download. So if your a fan definitely make sure to keep up.

posts.title: Be Prepared Ft Little Brother (prod. 9th Wonder)
posts.writeup: This is the Bside song to the first single for "Absolute Value" The A Side Features Talib Kweli. In my opinion I think the track with LB is better. Absolute Value hits stores Feb 19th...this album should be bangin. Akro is sick.

posts.title: Business As Usual (Cuts by DJ Premier)
posts.writeup: Ive had this kickin around on my playlist for awhile now and I can't really decide if I like it enough to grace a mix. But check it for yourself. A.G and Show are from the D.I.T.C crew for those that don't know.

posts.title: Despertar Para Novo Dia Ft Bob Da Rage Sense, Supreme
posts.writeup: This is the bonus cd you get with Promoe's dvd. But I am assuming that no one has heard of Promoe....well maybe if you live in Sweden. Promoe is the lead MC from the Swedish supergroup Looptroop. If you remember mix 162 the "Headache" track, dude has some skills with some crazy dreads.

posts.title: Play On" Ft Pep Love
posts.writeup: DJ Deckstream is a Japanese DJ/Producer. This is another one of those Producer compilations. His beats are average, but he pulled through on the track with Pep love. Soundtracks features DJ Premier, Zion i, Talib Kweli, Lupe Fiasco, Surreal.

posts.title: Imma Make It Feat Masta Ace, Wordsworth
posts.writeup: HMD is a up and coming producer from San Fran. This is his debut release and is sure to get him noticed. Masta Ace and Wordsworth help out with the standout track "Imma Make It" And of course Ace drops a flawless verse...the man has to be the most consistent hiphop artist out today.

posts.title: Little Math You
posts.writeup: Just recently Slug and Ant put up a free bootleg cd full of unreleased songs for free download. To Quote Slug "Its Great for first dates, crappy houseparties, or to play in the background while you google yourself again" Haha. Anyways click Here to download the free album.

posts.title: Love One
posts.writeup: Common Market consists of RA Scion (Emcee) and Sabzi (Beats). Sabzi also does beats for the Blue Scholars. I slept on this heavily back in 05', so go ahead and check it out its full of slick beats from sabzi and quality emceeing.

posts.title: Gangster Blues (Acoustic)
posts.writeup: In November Mattafix did a live radio show in Germany, doing acoustic versions of their songs. And the result was a dope musical experience. You wont find these acoustic versions anywhere else but here so enjoy! And if you haven't already make sure you find a way to order Rhythm and Hymns online, this cd needs to be in your collection and should not be bootlegged!

posts.title: Hip Hop Saved My Life Ft Nikki Jean
posts.writeup: Lupe's newest offering ends off a solid year of music in 2007. Although "The Cool" is not as good as I hoped, its still a good album and should not be overlooked.

posts.title: Exile
posts.writeup: Hailing from London Ontario, Shad is one of those down to earth cats that you meet after a show and will spend the time to make you a fan. After seeing him open for Sweatshop in Kelowna, I was amazed at how well he warmed up the crowd. People will know his name in the next year or two!

posts.title: Floatin
posts.writeup: Finally some news from CYNE. Grey Matter is a digital only ep available at their website for download ( http://cyne.net/CYNE_GreyMatter.zip ) As for the new album.... its titled " Pretty Dark Things" and is due in Feb 2008. If you purchase the album from CYNE directly they will include a bonus Cd of all new unreleased material, and even the "Fallen Stars" Ep that never saw the light of day.

posts.title: Campfire
posts.writeup: The highly anticipated new album from the Wu camp. Sadly this album disappoints. Its been well documented by hiphop news sites about the turmoil within the group. The new Ghostface is better in my opinion. I just expected more but it still has a few solid tracks.

posts.title: The G Gap
posts.writeup: One man army's newest offering since S.O.N.O.G.R.A.M. For those who dont know one be lo is one half of the legendary Binary Star. Fans of real hiphop will enjoy this, check uggh for the preorder.

posts.title: Lets Dance Instead
posts.writeup: Madcon's newest cd is pretty good, and it features Vinni from the Paperboys! Unless your in Norway you can pick up this cd, good luck getting it otherwise.

posts.title: Aint Got Time To Waste Ft Yz
posts.writeup: This is a throwback from 99 that just got reissued recently. Aim brings that classic hiphop sound with jazzy loops, horns, and upbeat lyrics. I suggest looking up his discography...

posts.title: Celebrate Ft John Legend
posts.writeup: This has been out for awhile now...but what do u expect when I work 12 hours a day! Nice beat on this song with some nice vocals from John Legend. Not a big fan of Cassidy but this song is worth checking out.

posts.title: Dolce Vita
posts.writeup: There is a new TQ album dropping feb 12, 2008. Judging by the sampler this one should be better than his last. Peep 'Dolce Vita' below.

posts.title: Made
posts.writeup: Face's newest album since 2002's The Fix. For me this is the type of album that will take many listens. Its solid, but it will take some time to figure out how good it is. Make sure to pick this up if your a Scarface fan, it hits stores Dec 4th. (Along with a tonne of other solid releases)

posts.title: Yolandas House Ft Method Man, Raekwon
posts.writeup: New Ghostface hits stores Dec 4th!

posts.title: If I Had Eyes
posts.writeup: This is the first single from his new album "Sleep Through The Static" Due Feb 08'. You can purchase this single from Itunes on Dec 11th.

posts.title: Things I Dream
posts.writeup: This cd is reeeediculous. Another classic from Cunninlynguists. If you call yourself a fan of cunnin and dont buy this cd, you should be SHOT. Full of flawless beats from Kno. They only get better with age. This will be the best 20 bucks you will spend. In stores Nov 27th.

posts.title: Art Of Storytelling Pt 4
posts.writeup: First Leaked track from the new Dj Drama full length. Dre kills this track. Gangsta Grillz due Dec 4th.

posts.title: Live at the BBQ Ft Styles of Beyond, Celph Titled
posts.writeup: Features new and old Apathy tracks. Celph tears up that verse in live at the bbq.

posts.title: Beggin
posts.writeup: New single from Madcon's new album "So Dark The Con of Man" due in 2008. Madcon are from Norway.

posts.title: Focus Ft Buff1
posts.writeup: Dj Brasco is a beatmaker from Paris, France. And this is his debut album that features Buff1 (Athletic Mic League), Phat Kat (Slum Village), Hezekiah, Rasco, Black Milk, Oddisee. Very good beats on this!

posts.title: Life Changes
posts.writeup: Another leaked track from 8 Diagrams....This is the ODB tribute track. 8 Diagrams due Dec 11th.

posts.title: Untitled #2
posts.writeup: The follow up to Outlaw, and once again a very good album...

posts.title: Johnny
posts.writeup: Fans of Jack Johnson will really like this band. This is a great cd. You can get this cd at Zulu Records in Kits, or by getting it off of Itunes, so go buy this!. I expect these guys to get picked up by a label soon. Support good music!

posts.title: Cant Stop
posts.writeup: New best of Classified hand picked by fans. By way of voting on his website. It features this one unreleased song. The beat is sick with a nice guitar.

posts.title: Lesson Learned ft. John Mayer
posts.writeup: How could you not like Alicia Keys? Her voice is amazing, this is her 3rd album. In stores Nov 13th.

posts.title: Then Till Now
posts.writeup: Remixed album of Bliss N Eso Latest. M-Phazes makes the album sound 100% better, check out the original if u want... M-Phazes is one of the freshest producers working right now in my opinion.

posts.title: Stop, Look, Listen Ft Styles, Termanology, Q-Tip
posts.writeup: Its dope so check it. Read up on the nfo and tracklist in the link below.

posts.title: Hopeful Feat M1 (Dead Prez)
posts.writeup: Debut album from the UK duo. If you can get your hands on this you wont be dissapointed, some sleeper tracks all over this. Luvolution features M1 of Dead Prez and Sean Price.

posts.title: Heart Gently Weeps
posts.writeup: This is the final album version of this track. Features Dhani Harrison (George Harrison's Son), John Fruciante on the guitar, Erykah Badu on the hook, and with Ghost, Rae, Meth droppin the verses.

posts.title: Used To Get High
posts.writeup: Finally got around to getting this. And its good, I really like "used to get high". Listen Below

posts.title: Cookie Jar
posts.writeup: Finally got around to not being a cheap ass and copped this off of cdbaby. I recommend you do the same if your a fan of Pepper, The Expendables or Slightly Stoopid. Click on the link and support independent music! This album features Mikey Dread from Pepper. You can play this from start to finish without skipping.

posts.writeup: Hot off the heels of the Sad Clown Bad Summer, comes the newest fall edition in the sad clown series. If Atmosphere can afford to release amazing songs like these 2 before the new album, "When Life Gives You Lemons" (drops in early 08)....Then expect a good album. These songs are creative and Ant's beats are top notch.

posts.title: Heart Gently Weeps
posts.writeup: Heres the first teaser we get from 8 diagrams (now pushed back to dec 4th).

posts.title: Hurting ft. Masta Ace

posts.title: My Shit
posts.writeup: Right now all of New Zealand is losing it over the eagerly awaited release of the new Scribe. Its been 4 years and with tons of expectations the album pulls through with dope beats and dope rhymes.

posts.title: Deja Vu
posts.writeup: Dope group from Ontario

posts.title: Minimum Wage
posts.writeup: Fans of Slightly Stoopid, Pepper will love these dudes, very solid album. They are on Stoopid Records and hail from Santa Cruz.

posts.title: Hold On ft. Sene

posts.title: Dump The Clip
posts.writeup: The Supergroup is back with their sophmore release. The Army of Pharoahs Consists of Vinnie Paz, Cheif Kamachi, Outerspace, Apathy, Celph Titled, 7L, Esoteric, and features the return of Jus Allah. Lots of good beats on this one.

posts.title: Brendas Baby
posts.writeup: Highly anticipated debut album from Justus League member Median.

posts.title: Living Darfur
posts.writeup: Mattafix has their new album (Rhythm and Hymns) dropping on November 12. Check the out the first single....'Living'... The video for 'Living' was shot in the Darfuri refugee camps in Eastern Chad and will hopefully help raise awareness of the struggle taking place there. For more info check out their website  Mattafix

posts.title: Dont Sleep
posts.writeup: Debut album from Chicago emcee Moonshine. Hes got skills. Check him out.

posts.title: Looking Up ft. Bilal

Latley Insight has been busy, as he just finished the Shinsight Trio Lp. If you are fan of jazz infused hiphop with intelligent rhymes make sure to check out this out.

posts.title: My Shit
posts.writeup: Check out Scribe's new single from his new album "Rhymebook" due in Oct. This single has more of a commercial appeal to it....hopefully P Money handles most of the production on this. Its been 4 years since "The Crusader" so hopefully Scribe can pull through with a good album. Scribe is New Zealand's biggest hip-hop act.

posts.title: Superstar ft. Matthew Santos

posts.title: Tear Him Down

posts.title: Duppy Conquerer (Fort Knox Five Remix)
posts.writeup: This is a cool collection of remixed tracks with dope new twists on already amazing marley tracks. This is a perfect mix to just have rippin at a bbq or at a cabin. This is the first official Bob Marley Remix album put out by the Tuff Gong label.

posts.title: Happy
posts.writeup: With Killah Priest's new album dropping next tuesday, here is a teaser from his new album "The Offering" You will notice this is that Dr Dre beat that he used for that unreleased cut with Nas, and Nature.

posts.title: Part Of The World ft. Oddisee

posts.writeup: Urthboy?? yes now dont dismiss this just yet.... This is another example of never ending dope artists that come out of Australia. This is a different kind of album, with a crazy style that is refreshing. The production is nice. Make sure you give this a listen.

posts.title: Gray

Kanye handles most of the production with one joint by the late J Dilla with others from Devo Springsteen (Misunderstood) and Will.iam (I Want You). This is a great album that you can just play straight through. Props to Common for these dope beats. Sidenote....whats with the long hair??? is he trying to look like jesus?

Class' lights up that verse in aint hard to find. Sick beats on this. Dont Sleep!!! For those that dont know jordan is on classifieds label "Half Life Records" and they both hail from Halifax. Jordan Croucher is best well known for his hook on "All About You" from Classifieds last album "Hitch Hikin Music".

posts.title: So Nice
posts.writeup: Dope track to check out, nice beats on this LP. Featuring production from one of the dopest producers in hiphop right now....Kev Brown!!

posts.writeup: Well...here is some more nice reggae to check out...these guys are dope. def check them out. Visit the cdbaby site to peep 'Courage to Grow'..

posts.writeup: Finally got around to checking this out and its a decent offering from jazzy jeff.

posts.writeup: dope new album from Athletic Mic League member 'Buff1'...dont sleep

posts.title: Who Needs Actions
posts.writeup: Finally got my hands on this...and its dope, a must have. That 'who needs action' track is nice. Dont sleep on the best emcee out of the UK

posts.title: The First Post
posts.writeup:
Hillydilly???.... Its a nick name that has stuck with me so I just rolled with it.....

Hillydilly.com has been 11 years in the making from the humble beginnings of File Quest back in 1998 to the days of when CD burning was new and of course the days of Napster. I Instantly fell in love with searching out new music and spreading the word about all the good music that is out there. Furthermore researching into how music gets on the internet I came across topsites, which allowed me to access all types of music from all over the globe. All this music was at my fingertips and I started to feel a burning desire to get this music out to people. Little did I know it was the start of a lifelong goal to spread the word of music that needed to be heard. I would scour through everything with relentless abandon making sure I would never skip a track, because you never know when that random classic could pop up. It first started with releasing mix cds when I would compile weeks worth of un-earthed music into a well conformed mix. To this day I still work on these mixes meticulously everyday of my life. It wasn't until I was sitting in a hotel in Kuala Lumpur with my girlfriend at the time talking about ways to spread this music easier. And boom she said it "why don't you start a blog?" Duh! And here we are still going strong with no plans to slow down. I am trying to gain a trust with you in that you can trust that I will always have quality control. Nobody likes bad music! And for the record I support the music I love!
